api.name: GREAT PLUMBER 65
api.description:
I have a phobia that is very close to Triskaidekaphobia, and it involves a plumber, a princess, a castle and a speaking mushroom…

Discover it in this mini-game, or more precisely, this little tech demo, in which my challenge was to recreate the exterior of a childhood game as accurately as possible in 13kb, by mixing 10 different tools that I developed during the past 10 years (3D framework, texture generator, emoji polyfill, MIDI parser, piano synth, 3D platform engine, 3D map editor, JS inputs handler, speech synth and sound effects editor).

For once, the source code is readable and commented!

Controls: click to start / WASD or ZQSD or arrow keys to move.

The game should work on most browsers and devices but the best results are on Windows + Webkit browser (Edge or Chrome)

Decentralized challenges: OP Guild, Arcadia

comments.author: Alex Delderfield

Great music and 3D here. Surprised to find speech audio in a js13k entry - I do wonder how that was done. A nice surprise to find you can swim, I always have to jump into water in a game to see what happens.

But I need to deduct points here - about 4 hours in, in the snow level - I found myself with a deep, longing desire to pick up the baby penguins and yeet them off the edge of the cliff into the infinite void - in direct view of their family. But it seems like there is no pickup function implemented to do this, so for now they live on. 🐧 -1 ⭐

comments.author: Christoph Schansky

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: GREAT PLUMBER 65 brings a lovely dose of nostalgia with its retro-inspired 3D platforming aesthetic, reminiscent of classic games from the 90s. The bright and colorful palette, along with the simplified character models and blocky environments, immediately transports players back to the days of early 3D gaming. The attention to detail in the castle and surrounding area makes the world feel familiar and welcoming, capturing the playful charm of the genre. While the overall aesthetic successfully evokes nostalgia, the visual experience could be improved with more dynamic textures and shading to enhance the depth of the environment. Adding a bit of variety in the terrain or background could also make the game world feel more alive and less repetitive. Additionally, smoother character animations would help elevate the game’s immersion, making the gameplay feel more fluid. The visual style is a delightful throwback that will undoubtedly resonate with fans of classic platformers. The vibrant colors and simple yet effective design are perfect for a game that prioritizes fun and accessibility. With some small adjustments to textures and animations, GREAT PLUMBER 65 could become an even more engaging experience.

api.name: Economy of Motion
api.description:
Help Dot reach the goal in under 13 steps. It’s measured in keystrokes, you have to be judicious about every movement.

  • WASD, Arrows, Dpad: Walk/Ladder
  • Space, Z, A(gamepad): Jump

If you complete all 13 levels, you’ll be scored by total time and total keystrokes. No storage; remembering scores is up to you. Time spent standing on the goal doesn’t count.

If you get stuck, just dance until you explode.

There’s a fabulous mini comic, and comments form, on Itch: https://aksommerville.itch.io/economy-of-motion

comments.author: Ryan Malm

I got 4 -? levels in, and I couldn’t get by the one with all the long ladders? Takes exactly 13 to navigate up and down to the goal, but then you die. Great game idea! I played longer than I thought I might because I wanted to beat the ladders level. Tell me how to beat it!

Great music! I love that you managed to get in a ‘game over’ separate music riff. Good touch, polish that’s usually missing from JS13K entries.

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Thanks for the kind words! A hint about the ladders: Keystrokes only count when you press them, and you can hold as long as you like.

comments.author: João Lopes

I wish there were more and more difficult puzzles. I missed a reset button to restart a level when I already knew I screwed up. The final scores seemed to sum up everything, but most rows were zero? The music is lovely.

If you’re looking for areas to improve, I think the jumping could be polished a bit, and platform graphics could be more varied.

Solid entry overall!

Great entry!

Love the concept and implementation of mechanics also! This game is just plain fun.

comments.author: Hogia

comments.author: IroncladDev

Love the idea and simple gameplay.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text: Fun game with nice gameplay and music!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Innovative! And a good level design - not too easy, not too hard.

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Love your GitHub README, especially the fact you thought about what to do afterwards; ie. having a TODO for things you’d like to implement.

Cool take on the theme. The music is fun, and the “winning” fanfare is great. The colour animation on the words (ie. when you get to 10 it goes red) is a nice touch.

The 8 bit animation on Dot is great too - nice work (ie. claps hands when reaches the goal).

Fun and easy game to play - good intuitive controls.

I got stuck on the six ladders; could only work out how to do it in 13 moves, but it kept exploding on the goal line. Nice work though!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Nice integration of the theme into a platformer! The “press the key as long as you like, it counts as one” mechanic is great. And i love the small details, like the clapping when you reach the finish ;-) Good music, and sharp controls. Great and entertaining entry!

comments.author: John Edvard

comments.author: Cody Ebberson

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Simple, fun, sometimes challenging. Music, sound effects and pixel art all good,

14 deaths I think I had, a few tricky levels.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: really polished and tight gameplay mechanic!! very hard, but very fair! great work, I really enjoyed this; the levels were just right

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text:
I tried this one during drafts and loved it then already! Awesome game, great use of the theme. I completed in 630 something steps, I have no idea how you can complete in < 300 steps, that will have to remain a mystery. The whole vibe of the game, the graphics, sound and music remind me so much of Dangerous Dave II, one of my favorite games.

Congrats on a well-executed, well-polished & innovative game!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun platformer with a hard-as-nails mechanic limiting your moves.

The pixel graphics were well designed and worked well.

The music was funky as were the sound effects.

The levels were challenging and looked well designed, although in places just touching the goal felt it should have been enough instead of needing to be on top of it.

It felt a bit harsh for a directional jump to take 2 moves away

I got stuck on the level with the 6 ladders.

Perhaps a suggestion for improvement would be to allow jumping through platforms from below (although I know form experience that this isn’t trivial)

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: That’s a good idea! When the goal is floating, as most of them are, doing them as one-ways would feel more correct.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Cool game! Really clever implementation of the theme.

It made me really conscious of how many keypresses/micro-adjustments I naturally make while playing a platformer game.

This would be a cool game to expand upon after the jam to have more levels/obstacles, etc. (but maybe the concept is too weird without the context of this year’s js13k, haha)!

My final score was 185, 238, 10

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Such a clever game. So simple, yet so fun.

Honestly, I love those types of games that don’t have one unique concept they use all the way long and is really fun. Music is top, game is super easy to grasp without the need of a tutorial.

Really not much to say! Super fun!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
First off, best instructions ever, “If you get stuck, just dance until you explode”

But really fantastic game! Simple gameplay, easy to understand, but still challenging. The audio fit perfectly, and the clapping animation was adorable.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Innovative use of the rules to mix puzzles and skills!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Very enjoyable game and cool idea. Sometimes my inputs got counted twice, not sure if this is a bug or just a quirk of the controller. As others have said, the ladder level is a difficulty spike that requires you to learn that you have to use diagonals. It’s actually a very clever idea, but maybe should have been the final level, or closer to it. Once I realized that, I actually noticed I was able to change diagonals without it counting against me and finished it very easily though.

api.name: Cooler
api.description: Arrow Keys (or WASD) To Move. X or Space to shoot.

comments.author: xem

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
This looks like a great pixel art platformer with ledge grabbing. It looks great and shooting the arrows is cool too.

I couldn’t get past the part where I started to see a square ghost-looking thing. Maybe it doesn’t work on Firefox but I didn’t know what to do. I don’t know how to kill those weird black enemies. It seems like I need to shoot them with their backs turned on me but they come back alive again. So it’s confusing and frustrating when I die because It’s not clear whether I’m doing something wrong or right.

There seems to be a blood effect to the arrow no matter if it hits something or not.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: I wanted to love this, but the frame rate was too high for me to play well I think? Really cool and polished shooting animation, awesome pixel art. I couldn’t get by even the first enemy though, everything was super-fast. Chrome on Ubuntu Linux.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This game looks really impressive although minimalist. The tunes are really smooth and the animations are cute. But I couldn’t understand the goal, or progress much. I reached a door that wouldn’t open and that’s that. I don’t see how this gits the theme either.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
I really liked this entry. It has simple graphics but a cool looking character, camera shake, quite a bit of juice.

In terms of areas to improve, I’d polish the platforming (I could not reach the second checkpoint), combat (found the first enemy to be oddly difficult to fight, took me 3 tries), and the shaky camera (it felt like it was often moving to the right then readjusting back to the left).

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Pretty decent sidescroller with cool effects (screen shake, etc).

I like the animation and effects a lot, and the enemies aren’t so bad either.

It would have been nice to have a little more info to start with, but at the same time was kinda cool to figure out what a few things in the game did.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I loved the graphics, the atmosphere and the jump style! However I was not able to pass the second green door which is frustrating. Maybe I missed something :(

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
I liked the level design.

I got stuck early when you find the other you.

comments.author: Logan Tempez
comments.text: I haven’t been able to finish the first level, the character control is too hard. It’s moving too fast and it’s really difficult to be precise. Sadly I don’t see the link to the theme at the moment. But the graphics and the music are cool and I’m sad to not seeing more of it!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: cool jump, animation, but no idea what to do or how to win.

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: I love the shooting feedback, very well done the double jump is also a nice touch!

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I enjoyed the music, and the game had a decent vibe, but I struggled to figure out what to do with the ghost/text bubble thingy. The theme escapes me. Personally, I’m not a fan of the UP button being jump, but to each their own.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I enjoyed the side scrolling, I think the clambering was a super nice touch. I tried to kill that first enemy, but fantastic game!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice pixel art and general design, fun with nice enemies. The controls and jumping are fine but could be a little slower, this would give you the feeling of being more in control. The jump has quite a high arch.

I like the auto climb and shooting, the enemies are cute and quite tough to work out, like how they sleep and get back up. The doors are a nice save point and have a cool animation. Also nice to see some screen shake!

Not sure how the theme fits in here but still a good entry.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Great little game, I loved the graphics, the animation and the sounds.

Once I learned you could double jump things got a bit easier, although the first enemy which shot green goo didn’t seem to be defeatable.

I came across a door which I couldn’t shoot open so wasn’t sure if there was another game mechanic other than the double jump that I was yet to discover by trial an error?

Not sure what the connection was to 13 or fear of 13. But still an impressive platformer all in all.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text: Nice graphics and style. Couldn’t get past the 2nd checkpoint, though.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: I like the gfx style and the different animations of the character for jumping, running, climbing, idle..

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: I didn’t even make it to the second checkpoint, the platform was just out of reach and I didn’t know how to get there.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Intuitive controls. Cute 8-bit graphics, love the music too.

Great platform, the screen moves around a little too much, making it a touch jarring for the player. Would be good to have lives or something at the top. Love that if you die, it restarts at a point rather than at the start - very classic of platformers. The first enemy was wayyyyy too hard to kill for a first encounter. I also died by running into something invisible (maybe “off the screen”). The second door didn’t open for me. Maybe the character moves a little jarring too.

Overall cute graphics, nice music, and sound effects that fit well within the game.

Not quite sure how this fits into the theme though. Some explanation at the start would have been helpful.

Add a bit of information to your GitHub README.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I beat the game! (I think?) Nice job! This was a fun short platformer. Very nice pixel art, including ledge climbing animations, which you don’t often see in js13k games. The music and sound was also nice. Well done!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I like how the player moves (and stretches) and can latch onto edges. The fire animation is cool too. I appreciate the screen shake, but I feel it’s too much right now.

The game was different from what I thought by looking at the Thumbnail. I think the thumbnail could have been an in-game screenshot, and it would look more attractive.

I wasn’t able to get past checkpoint 2. I tried to look at the other comments on this page to look for hints, but I’m still not sure what to do.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game under 13kb!
    • The graphics! I really enjoyed the particles and the added movements for the character.
  • I liked that you:
    • Added some extra abilities to the basic platform idea, like grabbing to the edges.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • The platform was hard! After the third death on the second spawn, where I had to go through the jumps, I gave up. Somehow my shots didn’t kill it and they always crushed me with the gems.
    • I’m so curious about the lore behind a person with a gun, plus those gem spawning dogs. What’s their goal? What they are hunting in that dungeon/high-building?

api.name: Tower Defence Walking 13s
api.description:
Zombies have recived the number thirteen, and they can now walk.

Marching toward your castle and aiming for your brains.

Hope still remain as you pocess the towers of defence, you can survive.

Place towers and defend your land, upgrade to handle the massive waves of strong 13s.

Castle is also a tower, as last line of defence but the strongest ranging tower.

Controls: mouse or touch on screen

Menu: click on 📋 in header to show menu

Config: you can enable and disable music and sound, there are two songs available that you can switch between in config.

Infinity Cash: as a fun mod for having overpowered castle since the only way to max it out is having this much cash, from config you can enable this but it cannot be disabled.

Win: survive the waves and move to next level getting different map but stronger apponent.

Game is open ended as long it is enjoyable

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Got inifinity money and no more waves after 23th. Simple Tower defense, it should be nice to add one or two other ennemy patterns (faster or heavier)

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Works very well on mobile! Got to play some rounds on the train. It’s fun :)

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Interesting little game. There are a few areas that could be improved but pretty neat effort!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text: I like a lot to play that game. It is simple but enjoyable. Music is rythmed. I prefer the track Peace.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
After wave 23 i got infinite money and the waves stopped coming. There was no errors in the console, so not sure what happened. I don’t understand what the infinity cash mod is about.

I’m a sucker for tower defense games, but this one seemed to be a matter of numbers and little strategy. As long as I kept 2-3 long range overpowered towers out of reach of the enemies, they were unable to make it past the second curve of the path.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: I love a good tower defense. I enjoyed playing, pace was a little fast, I miss having some time for placement between waves.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
Tower defense games are always fun!

I liked this one and played through a few waves. I think you could easily improve with a few simple changes:

  • enable music by default (you have several good tracks, most people won’t hear them because they’re muted if you don’t go into the settings)
  • make enemy health bars more visible (+ tower health bars)
  • animations when hitting enemies (a simple white overlay would go a long way)
  • resize the game to fit the screen (not sure why full screen was required, maybe for mobile?)
  • give it a twist! (I’m not sure what the tower types did, maybe that was the twist but it wasn’t overly clear
  • You have a different level every time, which is great but it’s not obvious when you only play the game once. Maybe change the level after a few waves? (only made it to wave 7, maybe it changes after?)

Hope to see you put up another entry next year!

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Cool layout and game in general but balance is way off and either has a bug or I triggered something by exiting out of full screen and maybe clicking money to make it go infinite.

I had way more than I could spend any way and had whole map covered with weapons.

13 theme barely wedged in. No sound or music that I could hear.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Nice tower defense.

Some audio would had help to get a better feeling of what is going on.

comments.author: Logan Tempez
comments.text:
Nice game! I struggle a little at first because I didn’t understand that it was possible to click on the grass to add towers so I only relied on the Castle and it was … complicated!

Working very well, nice work. I’d like a better link with the theme.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I played several times and I had fun. I like the fact there are 2 songs. I would recommend to start playing one song as soon as the player enters the game.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice Tower Defense game, the graphics were amazing and the game played very well! Nice work!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun little tower defence game, easy to pick up and play

I enjoyed playing this but feel the balance was a bit too much in my favour, the enemies didn’t get very far along the track and my money went up quicker than I could spend it, so I got to the end pretty quick and the enemies stopped coming

I didn’t see the map change as I progressed, and each new level I still had the same towers available

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: The music was really good, but I didn’t realize there was any until I read some comments. Making the options more visible would be nice. Also liked that there were different maps each time! I would have liked the tower-upgrade menu to have not been over the gameboard, since it obscurred the play.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Game is too easy, went to the Infinity money and no more waves without much effort (if we don’t count the hundreds of spam clicks I had to do on the tower menus). I did not understood the difference between the towers. I wish I could hide the initial tower menu by clicking again on the same tile. Also the buttons of the upgrade menu need hand cursor and a way to not require hundreds of clicks / taps. In general - too much micro management that displaces the joy of the game, but nice effort. Game can be improved easily.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: You have all the proper foundations of a great tower defense game, just needs some tuning. I hit infinite cash in level 1, and then it seemed like everything stopped. I agree with others, you should turn the music on by default! Nice job, I’m busy reviewing all 4 (!) of your entries!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
I enjoyed this game, and I’m proud to say I beat it at difficulty setting 3. The strategy I used: place ice towers early with a high fire rate to slow them down, and lightning towers at the end to hit them with damage. I only needed to heal the ice towers because they were also used as shields.

I did wish the sfx weren’t so loud. In a game like this, you’re going to hear a lot of shots, and they were very loud and overtaking the music.

The game is also missing an ending. It’s fun to have Infinity cash but there’s no more enemies, or anything to do. It would probably be better to have some “the end” screen.

All in all, thank you for this game. I like the tower defense genre, and I think this is a fun entry.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
I almost missed the music and sfx! Just found it in the config…

Solid tower-defense, maybe a bit too easy. Are there any (noticeable) differences in the tower-types?

Good game!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Cute graphics. Interesting twist with “mortal” towers, but then they should have a hp bars too. Also, some explanation on the difference between towers would be great.

api.name: Drunken Cornhole
api.description:
Cornhole is hard enough, but you are a drunken player with wobbly balance!

Compete against the CPU to reach 21 points first to win the match.

tournament rules are used, and the boards conform to the standard tournament configuration.

Cancellation Scoring

“Innings,” like in baseball, have a top and bottom, with each team/player taking a turn. At the end of each inning the team/player with more points in the inning subtracts the other players points if any from their own, keeping any remaining points.

Bags thrown into the hole are worth 3 points each. Bags that land atop the box are worth 1 point each.

Goal

First team/player to reach 21 points total wins the game.

Note

Per official rules, if a bag bounces or touches the ground, its points are voided.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Nice 3D graphics, impressive CPU / AI. All worked really well, difficult to score, a few times I over dragged the mouse and the bag didnt throw. Some sort of onscreen assistance with the power and aim could help or maybe the holes could be slightly larger targets.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: I’m sorry, but I don’t know how to play the game. The only key on keyboard working is “S” which change design, but no mouse nor keyboard has any effect.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: I found the overall mechanics to be solid but…I think it would have been better if the camera wasn’t always wobbling around. I mean if the theme was being drunk…I guess I could see it. But ya, if It had a two player mode (just take turns on the mouse) and a steady camera, I would play it more for sure!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Interesting. I have learned that I suck at cornhole :D
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
It looks awesome, but I don’t understand how to play. The S key turns the background bright, and clicking with the mouse zooms in a little. But other than that I couldn’t really to anything. Clicking on the square boxes does nothing. This doesn’t seem to fit the theme either.

Edit: ok, it seems i’m supposed to drag the square boxes? But i have no idea how to actually control them?

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: It’s Cornhole! What’s to say.. I’m not very good at cornhole in real life either. The throwing controls felt good, there wasn’t much keeping me engaged in this one otherwise though. The graphics are amazing. I missed having some sounds.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Controls were a bit difficult at first. There’s a lot on the screen and it’s not obvious you’re meant to drag the bags to throw them. I think with a few tweaks (+ a tutorial) the UI and the controls could be made much more intuitive.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Pretty cool concept. Cornhole execution is pretty great. Drunken part not so much, I think sort of an afterthought.

No 13 theme at all. Fun game though and AI seems pretty impressive and fair from the one game that I played. No sound or music that I could hear.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: 3d games are cool even when they are so hard.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Fantastic game with GREAT physics. The controls feel amazing and the bags do go where you toss them. Very nice work!

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text: Congrats! The simulation feels pretty good! Not sure how this goes with the theme. Also, some instructions would be nice: had no idea what to do at the beginning.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Strange controls, and hard to understand. I side view would had been more useful when throwing.

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
I recall playing this game IRL once but I didn’t know what it was called, so thank you for teaching me its name today :)

I feel like the game would be plenty difficult even without the drunk camera effect. I admit I struggled with controls. My throws barely made it to the board, and if I tried to flick my mouse all the way to the top of the screen, the Mac’s menu bar would appear and steal the cursor.

I liked the graphics and the physics. I wish bags collided with each other, so that there’s a chance I can put a bag into the hole by hitting it with another bag. (Would that count in the actual game?)

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Looks and feels great! I can see how some other people were confused by what to do at first, but it feels very natural once you know. One way you could simplify is remove the grabbing mechanic and just start with the bag in their hands. This would remove the confusion, speed up gameplay, and give you a few more bytes to work with. The particle effects when you get one in is a nice touch!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text: Took me a good while to figure out how to even play the game. Some sort of instructions would have been helpful. Also some sort of meter on throw strength would be nice as well just so we can get some repeatability in our throws. Otherwise it feels too hard to score points and the AI wins every time.

comments.author: cantelope
Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
At first I though it was not working but then I figured out how to throw a drunkie. A pre-tour could come in handy.

After that, the game was fun and well crafted, congrats!

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text:
A lot of folks are suggesting similar things. I still feel that figuring out the controls can be part of the official “challenge”, and honestly it occurred to me, but i chose, instead of a tutorial, to make the bags glow conspicuously :)

i stand by my choice haha

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Game logic is well done, although an explanation of the controls would help, it took me a few minutes to figure out you had to drag the squares from the right-hand side and throw them forward, correcting for the roll. Indicators for the aim also would come in handy.

Gfx are good although you could have added more scenery, would also help figure out the depth.

The drunk part is mostly annoying, whereas the CPU player is sober and always seems to aim better than you do.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Figuring out the controls was hard! Despite this, the game is fun and has potential. The FX (shining particles!) and camera work are super cool.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
pub-sports simulator. i even scored some holes, by mere luck, i guess?

cool artstyle and simulation of having one too much… but the irish pub visitor with style never is drunk, as long as she/he can hold on to a blade of grass to prevent falling off the earth. :-P

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Reminds me of a time when I played this whilst coincidentally being drunk. I forgot about it until now. I had no idea of it’s name at the time. Instead I remember vaguely hearing the words corn hole being used but thought it was something quite different. I worked out the controls after a few goes, drag and let go, pretty standard for a game with this perspective.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Is this cornhole game made up or is it really a game IRL ?

It was quite hard to play against the CPU who almost always got amazingly accurate shots on target.

It would be good to have a 1 on 1 game too with players taking it in turn on the same PC.

An interesting game, not sure how well the control system matches the feel of playing with beanbags?

Where was the theme here?

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I do like that the game seems strange because you are drunk! I am not sure what the theme has to do with the game of cornhole, but I did a pretty ok job!

comments.author: Dann Sullivan
comments.text: Absence of a theme, but good simulation of 3D space within the constraints.

api.name: TRON 2.0
api.description:
HUMAN VS CPU

Maintain a superior score against the clever AI drivers. Use speed and reflexes to cut them off, or trap them into areas where they eventually run out of space.

Features -

  • Arrow keys to navigate/accel/declerate
  • Colliding with another player’s ‘tail’ or a permimeter wall kills you. You may respawn indefinitely.
  • V to change views between 2D/3D, which can be helpful
  • P pauses game
  • L toggles leaderboard visibility
  • O toggles map & speedometer visibility

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: I am slightly better at Tron than I am at cornhole! Nice 3d graphics!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Nice game, it’s hard to beat the AI as they make really fast changes of direction. On 2D view camera change is a bit slow in motion, I prefered 3d view

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: I was a bit overwhelmed with the 3 different views and mostly ended up looking only at the mini map and sometimes at the top view, but rarely at the 3d view. This doesn’t seem to fit the theme.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: I played this a bunch already when you first submitted! Outstanding graphics, great gameplay, reminds me of Armegatron, and of course Tron itself. Gameplay feels great, I was really really craving some sounds in this one. I love that I have a 2D split view of the action, that’s a unique feature in all the lightcycle-likes I’ve played.

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text:
ayyyy. long time no see!

remember this? https://srmcgann.github.io/rotoblaster

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text: Great! I love this game since I installed a indie implementation on a old computer on the 90’s… You you put a spice with three simultaneous views, and play it again in the browser warms my heart.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
I think it’s an interesting choice to have three different views on the same game (3D, 2D, minimap). I would maybe focus on 3D with a minimap, and adjust the camera handle to give better visibility on what’s ahead.

It does look like you put a bunch of work into it. The 3D is smooth even if it could use more detail (add a rectangle for the bike?), and there are a few nice finishing touches which I always appreciate.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Not a bad game, quite a few times my inputs were read multiple times. Given the control scheme, I think you should check that the player releases their button press before registering another one.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text: Cool execution but this is just a game clone to me. 13 theme absent.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice game, good 3d movement and camera location, solid enemies and fun to play. if it become a multiplayer server io will kick.

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: Very cool game, the mini map and the different views were very helpful! GL

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text: 6.7kb with 3d … on canvas?! … very nice

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I really like the dual view and being able to switch between them! I think this has a lot of potential for a new style of games. Great work!

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text: Congrats on the game. The graphics look pretty cool. Love the two views! Not sure how it relates to the theme, though. One thing is the navigation controls are pretty sensitive. So, it’s a bit difficult to control the navigation even when you’re going slow. Would have liked to hear some audio.

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text: a lot of requests for audio. in retrospect, this would have been good and there was easily room to add it. next time for sure!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
And then finally….. I got in!

Always loved this game, best enjoyed in a lan party with friends!

Great 3d and 2d graphics, clever how you brought all the views together, I thought 2d would be easier but the turning was a bit too sudden on the rotation, the 3d view is easier on the eyes and smooth.

The AI works well, maybe smaller grid would have made for faster win / lose, maybe even having levels with goals.

Very clever implementation of the game!

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text:
for me, multiple views come in handy depending on situations. like, for tight quick cornering 2D is easier. The 3D, and practicing quick switching made the feature worthwhile.

thanks for playing!

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I enjoyed the graphics, and the controls felt pretty decent. It could really use some futuristic-sounding music! Not a bad entry and I killed a few snakes, but I’m not really sure what it has to do with the theme.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Great adaptation of a classic. Maybe a little too far from the theme but still very enjoyable.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A really cool looking game.

The combination of 3D and top-down was nice, and actually quite useful to get advance warning of impending doom.

Perhaps the level could have been smaller to force more interaction between the players

I enjoyed making spirals by just holding forward and left or right.

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text: indeed! thanks for the feedback. note: the “large” level is why there is speed control, you can zip pretty fast, just remember to slow down before hitting something :)

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Excellent. This captures what I assumed that Tron would feel like. I haven’t figured out how to consistently score on the AI. But this has all of the foundation for an excellent game. Impressive 3D graphics, impressive UI overlay. Well done

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text:
since it originally appeared in arcades back in dinosaur times, scoring in TRON has been about “cutting off” your opponent(s)…

how to do it

i should have included this video in the README, but this shows the technique 😃

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Not the first 3D remake of Tron that I see, but one of the best. Nice gfx and particle system, responsive controls and smooth camera motion.

The minimap should be on by default. And since you have some bytes left, how aout adding options, such as the number of AI players, the room size, or obstacles on harder levels ?

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text: Ok, it’s a good Tron game, But there is no use of the triskaidekaphobia theme or even the number 13.

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback! I don’t usually abide by the suggested theme, for a few reasons: 1) it’s a suggestion. 2) most of the games which DO involve the theme just tack it on, which is kind of lame, and 3) I like to remind people that themes and topics in general are not prisons but recommended focal points. this is a very important distinction, which involves censorship and micromanagement, versus independent thought and sustainability respectively. it’s a big conversation. but i appreciate your observation!

Thanks again!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text:
Looks great, but gameplay is not that compelling.

First, minimap makes the main view useless. I think removing the minimap and figuring how to make game fully playable without relying on it culd be better.

Second, adding some “rabbits” on the field for you and other “snakes” to try and eat would add some depth to the game, make snakes more concentrated in one place, and add a non-trivial objective.

comments.author: cantelope
comments.text: there’s a key symbol @ the minimap [o], and other items, for toggling it/them

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
Great Tron game, it feels fun to cut off the enemies at high speed. The AI seemed to respond quickly if I moved closed to it, so that felt like a nice challenge.

I also really liked that you could swap between 3rd person and top view, as well as being able to control the speed with ease.

Would have loved to see more of the theme and audio being present in the game, but other than that it was really fun!

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: It is a very good looking version of Tron. Under 13kb, it is quite impressive! I am not sure what else to say. It’s Tron after all. Thanks for submitting it :-)

api.name: Cards13k
api.description: Spider solitaire. But as we fear number 13 there are no kings - only 12 cards per suit. This seems to make it much more likely that you can complete the game! Click and drag cards, Ctrl-Z to undo as many moves as you like. Enjoy the animated deals and suit completions.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: I didn’t play spider solitaire for a long time! I didn’t remember the rules, it tooks me some game to win :) Good idea removing the king to fit theme!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Interesting twist for fitting to the theme. I tried but could not complete a single game, I’m really bad at solitaire.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Honestly impressive you fit solitaire spider in 13k. The controls are a bit weird. Clicking on a card sends it to a random fitting destination, but either I remember the rules wrong, and the game allows cards to be places wrong. The card designs looked broken for me, with text overflowing their boundaries. You must consider that fonts render with different sizes in different systems. The game area also didn’t fit my screen, so I had to zoom out the page, which then made the cards look small.

comments.author: Graham Relf
comments.text: Unfortunately I didn’t scale the font size with the canvas size. I will in future.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Hard for me to be objective as I’m not a huge solitaire fan, good solid game, nice animations.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Seems cool but just an implementation (game clone?) of Spider Solitaire.

Granted, it is fairly impressive to do this in 13k bytes.

Does inject the theme by taking out Kings so there are only 12 cards per suit.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: The game works but I think the graphics and gameplay are done well. Great work!

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, the game was wider than my screen, so I couldn’t properly play it. You should scale it down to fit the available size.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Graham Relf
comments.text: I chose to make the canvas fill available screen size rather than window size. Perhaps a bad choice.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: OK implementation, but as basic as it could be.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing the game, and it looks great for being a 13k game

The game didn’t scale to browser size, so I had to make browser bigger to see the whole play field.

I wanted to be able to drag the whole stack of uncovered cards, but only the bottom fully visible card could be dragged, I assume this is a variant of the solitaire I’ve played before.

Also noticed that the game has a kind of cheat mode where just double clicking on several of the exposed cards would automatically put them somewhere.

I liked the Ctrl-Z aspect to undo moves

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: It all works well; fitting the game into 13K, well done. Always good to add extra twists to games to make them your own.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text: No kings is a funny interpretation of the theme. Unfortunately the game broke a lot for me. Multiple duplicate cards (2 Ace of spades for example), clicking the card off to the right side spawned a new row of cards (made lots of duplicates), I dragged a card off the screen and it disappeared, etc. Clicking on a card would automatically move it to a valid stack, which was confusing at first.

comments.author: Graham Relf
comments.text: Yes there are 2 packs! It is behaving as intended.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: very nicely done! I like the feeling of dragging the cards around and matching them. I enjoyed that you had infinite moves, so I could “cheat” by lazily grabbing a card then randomly let go while wiggling the mouse and the game would be delightfully generous and assume I must have meant a nearby correct stack. Tee hee! I loved that! YEAH, that is totally where I meant that card to go, thanks! I also was impressed that you implemented undo. this all made for a very playable game! all it needs now are particles and screenshake and a huge boss battle evil card with TNT that makes nearby cards catch fire! muahahahaha! that sort of chaos is what I think this stately and classy and well made card game yearns for! =) thanks for joining js13k!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text:
Nice solitaire! I haven’t actually played it before so I had to look up the rules (it would have been helpful with a link in the description!).

You can add a lot of “juice” to a card game (have a look at Balatro!. I’m thinking that a card shadow would be nice and a smooth card movement animation when cards are moved from one pile to another.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Solid implementation of spider solitaire.

Some animations (moving cards, shadows, etc.) and sfx would add much to the game.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
As the name suggests, it’s Spider Solitaire! There are no instructions included, which is a bit of an omission IMHO. Essentially, there are 2 decks of cards and you arrange them in suits, highest to lowest. The difference in this version is the lack of the King cards, because we’re scared of 13 cards in a suit :)

The game works really well. You can drag same-suit stacks, you can click cards to fast-assign them to valid spaces (or drag them if you have multiple destinations they could go on). The graphics are functional but perfectly good, it’s quick, responsive and stupidly addictive. There’s no sound at all but I’m not sure you really need it. The lack of Kings is a great move, making it on-theme and giving it a subtle twist. Yes, you need to know how to play it to start with, but if you do, this is an utter time sink!

title: Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies
author: Cliff Earl

api.name: Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies
api.description:
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies is an uncomplicated casual game where you must avoid the falling thirteens and collect coins.

You can get the magnet to attract coins to you for a amount of time, and the shield which allows you to deflect the thireetns for a short time.

There is also one hidden mechanic that can boost your score faster.

Controls

Press Z to move left.

Press X to move right.

Controls are fully customizable.

Pressing Enter or Space will start a new game from the main screen, and return to the main screen from the game over screen.

Features

  • Full HD graphics.
  • Three musical scores.
  • Persistent options.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Funny game, a bit too much inertia which cause collision sometime, a bit frustrating. key binding is a really good option!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: I like the idea of an episodic game on multiple entries

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks xem, I tried to get 3 entries made but couldn’t quite get there. Next year!

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Nice simple game, good polished graphics. Controls were a little stiff and fast in my opinion

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Simple but fun game. My biggest complaint is about the default controls: why Z/X and not arrow keys or WASD?

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks Rémi. I just figured not all people have arrow keys on their keyboards, next time I’ll go with WSAD since most people seem to like or expect that :)

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Pretty fun, very nice visuals, bit loose controls, fun power up effects, basic gameplay.

Like the high score. Sounds get a bit eh after a while, but they are there.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Causal game as you said, but very well done ! I like the fact you can configure the controls, I found the game dynamic and pleasant to play and I love the bluesy main song!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Fun little game. Looks polished, and with a few different powerups. Nice music.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: cool animations and fear of 13 applied nicely

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: Intresting game and very fun to play it would be nice to have different movement options

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I had a lot of fun playing this one! The music is very fitting, and the graphics are nice and shiny. Personally, I’d prefer the controls were A and D. Nice effort!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks Noah, you can customize the controls to be whatever you like :)

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: The game played well and had 13 theme in it. Great work getting this together.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Fun game. It’s nice to provide customize key feature!! And I think the game is simple and classical.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing this game, despite it being pretty tough and calling my score abysmal ;)

I like the graphics and sound and also the little touches like confetti and avatar leaning when moving.

I played for quite a bit but I didn’t discover the hidden score multiplier.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Thanks. I feel that I was too harsh with the ratings, you have to get a very good score before it even compliments you a little ;)

Okay, there is a certain angle range where the 13 is almost flat facing the right way up. If you collide with it when its between those angles you get bonus points and don’t die.

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text:
Congrats on your game!

It is fun. Something I really liked was the way you did the magnet and how it feels once you have it. It provides a good experience to the player.

Something that could be better would be the controls. It is kind of difficult to control the player to be able to avoid the 13s effectively.

Thanks for the game!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Fun game, would be nice if it got harder over time, maybe it does? Was hard from the off.

Nice graphics and concept, simple but fun.

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
At first I thought this game was very hard and got a bit discouraged. After discovering what the power-ups did, I still think it’s hard, but I’ve had fun playing it! Nice music and sound effects, too.

Also, I don’t think I’ve ever seen a js13k game which allowed to customize controls. I changed them to left and right arrows which helped me get higher scores. Thanks for thinking about it and for designing a dedicated options screen!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks for your kind review. The code that manages the persistent controls has been present in all of my js13k games for the past 3 years and has been constantly evolving :)

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: My best score was 30,750 (“terrible”). The game really rewards patience and discipline, if you try to go for everything you almost certainly die. My best run was mostly a matter of staying alive and taking whatever coins just happened to land in my lap. Catchy music, cute graphics. Nice job!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Thats a well implemented casual game. The artstyle is catchy.

It starts quite fast, maybe a little buildup in the difficulty would be nicer?

It’s very good that the controlls can be configured, as on a QWERTZ-layout keyboard it would be unplayable without rotating the keyboard 165 degrees ;-) (this applies to the second game aswell)

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: so polished and cute! everything about this is slick and smooth and nicely put together. I really appreciate the animation tweens and wobbles everywhere. great work.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Animations/transitions were very slick! Felt very natural to play!

Nice touch that you can change the keys.

I ended up with a pathetic score of 25,550 over 80 seconds.

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text:
The BGM is pretty cute and funky and I like the cartoonish.

Alas I am so bad at this game I can only get Abysmal. I like the hover effect on the buttons and the star effect when clicked. And of coz 13-phobia you have to avoid the number 13.

And I failed to find the hidden mechanics.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Loved the music!

Maybe with some tweaking, could work on mobile as well. Seems there is potential for that.

Congrats on the submission!

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: It’s a simple concept, but it’s very polished and has a lot of juice, which is nice to see. I also love the music! Well done.

title: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
author: Cliff Earl

api.name: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
api.description:
Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space is an uncomplicated casual game where you must avoid the thirteens and collect coins.

You can get the magnet to attract coins to you for a amount of time, the shield which allows you to deflect the thireetns for a short time, and the clock which slows the thirteens down for a short time.

Controls

Press Z to steer left.

Press X to steer right.

Controls are fully customizable.

Pressing Enter or Space will start a new game from the main screen, and return to the main screen from the game over screen.

Features

  • Full HD graphics.
  • Three musical scores.
  • Persistent options.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: First game I thought it was harder than the other version, but I scored more that one after few games :)

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
Great looking complete game. It’s better than Episode 1. You have to survive long enough to find powerups that will help you live longer and collect more coins.

Z and X as default controls was and odd choice. I would have had it as A & D (for WASD players), Arrow Left & Right, or Q & D (for ZQSD keyboards) and then key customization (although a friendly feature) may have not been necessary.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: I enjoyed the unique player constraints and controls in this one, it was more interesting than part one. Good game!

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: I like the idea of this much more than episode 1. It feels like it has more depth and is more innovative, unfortunately I think it’s held back by the difficulty. I could not survive for more than 3 seconds. Maybe the game is tied to the framerate?

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: In hindsight it could start easier. I played so much during testing I guess I kept ramping it up because it seemed a little easy for me after so long. I think next year I’ll try asking for playtesters :)

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Much more fun version of Chapter 1 implemented with the overhead controls and space and chain, etc.

Nice that coins,etc come from all sides.

Nice theme tie in also, if a bit blunt. Sound is there but is a bit eh.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Finally, the much awaited sequel to Badluck Butter Chicken. This was surprisingly a lot more fun that the prequel. I think the chaos really tickles my brain.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: hard controls but good that this can be changed, animations are cool. loved the first episode more.

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Better than the first! I really liked the graphics and umbilical chord/egg theme, but it seems somewhat arbitrary from a thematic perspective. My top score was 33,450….not sure if that’s good or not, but I had a lot of fun getting there. Great entry!

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I really like the control theme, the game gets hectic very quickly! Great work getting this finished!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A nice change to your first episode, but way way harder :)

Easy enough to control, but feels more based on luck how you actually end up progressing as now instead of just 13s coming from the roof, they are coming from all sides - yikes.

Maybe my reaction times are bad, but I feel like the clock slowmo mode could have been the default, and then for it to get slower.

A good entry all in all, very similar to the other game, but with different game mechanics

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Love your GitHub README - even got a header pic - love it ❤️. Even got info about your workflow etc. Very thorough.

Brilliant and high quality loading screen. Sound effects and music are great - even under 11kB, amazing!

The star clicks on the main screen are super fun. I love the information, controls, and configuration screens. Nicely done.

Fun and simple to play game. The use of just two keys is really easy to get the hang on. Great graphics, good implementation. Well done. Love the use of score counters and other metrics at the top of the screen.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks for your kind review Mish :)

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: Great mini-game with nice look and feel. The UI and effects are juicy. it’s cool that you’ve made 2 episodes, and both are actually two different games. I liked episode 2 better than episode 1, mostly because it was more original and easier to control.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: I think version 2 is a more fun! Is there a number 3?

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: There is 1/3 of a third one but I ran out of time this year. I’ll save it for next year maybe :)

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Oo, tough game. My high score was 2750. I agree with other commenters, this version has more potential than part 1, and more opportunity for depth, but it has a much steeper learning curve with the controls. As with part 1, great graphics, sound effects, and music. Congrats on submitting multiple entries!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Thats also a well implemented casual game. Its a bit better as the first one because of the unusual movement-constraints.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: Even better than part one! Awesomeness.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Painfully difficult, but very addictive. I survived for 18 seconds.

Cool powerups in this one. The clock that slows everything down is a great idea.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Avoid the number 13, litererally. Its difficult to not get 5 stars for that. I got an amysmal score and went blind momentarily. I couldnt work out the controls until I bothered to read the instructions and the options. Nice bright presentation. It is quite difficult but you do get better with more goes. I like that its short with that one more go feel. Accept this broccoli in appreciation 🥦

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text:
Took me a few tries to get the hang of it, but then I had a lot of fun! And I really like the driving music! Well done!

PS: you must have pretty good reflexes ;)

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game, with sounds, a lot of graphics and details! Congratz.
    • Adding configuration for keys and music control.
  • I liked that you:
    • Added a bunch of small things that added to the feel of the game – the egg, the stars, the active effect particles.
    • Put a challenge up – 5000. That made me try at least once more to beat the game.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • The play sessions were really short. Not hitting a 13 is VERY HARD. My top score was 5450, after luck of getting some clocks. But outside of it, I was either getting snipped by a surprise 13 on the borders or just was cornered without a way to track back my path or turn quicker.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Nice entry! The controls are simple, I would have considered adding some hint in the screen as it is not obvious at first.

This version 2 is a bit more challenging than the version 1, but more fun!

api.name: SQUAD 13
api.description:
When all hope is lost, the World Police Organization sends SQUAD 13.

They are tasked with the most dangerous missions.

SQUAD 13 is feared even by the most wicked evil terrorists.

This is their story.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Outstanding entry as always, really quite addictive, the controls are excellent.

Could be made into a larger game for sure!

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: To be realistic, flappy bird is the only concept I can see. However the effects of the helicopter landing are making a score

comments.author: Santi Herranz
comments.text: I loved the gameplay, the presentation and the memories it brought back from playing Konami’s Super Cobra as a kid. Excellent work.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: 24/26 prisoners, 30 crash xD I enjoyed this game, controls remind me old game I played on DOS whrn I was young. Loved the graphisms, parallax and different time of day. Explosions are really cool too. During first levels,every one introduce new mechanisms, it’s nice! But the 5 last levels are mostly the same, game should have counted a bit less levels (It as just 13 for being a little in the theme I think)

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
This game is insanely fun. It’s actually a very hard and unforgiving game but it’s very simple and rewarding. Fighting other heli’s is intense.

It fits the theme where I’m very afraid of the 13 levels and anything I could collide with.

Gameplay is simple but hard to master. Getting kills without the lock-aim system is so satisfying. Also taking out a helicopter is satisfying. Watching them go down is great. Rescuing a prisoner is hard in itself, but having enemies try to shoot you down gets callenging.

There’s music and satisfying sounds.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Solid, feels great, love the particle effects. Had a lot of fun with this one.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Amazing helico simulation, particles system and audio, reminds me old Choplifter games.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Smooth stuff. I really like the graphics and particles!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Very funny tone! Laughed out loud when the super serious “world police” style intro text ended, and I immediately killed a hostage and exploded into a wall. The controls are pretty finnicky, but that’s part of the challenge. I like the silhouetted graphics and the color palette!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: This game is very addictive to play!! I spent almost 30 mins to complete all the 13 levels on mobile phone!

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Had a ton of fun playing this, its really challenging to shoot those bad guys! I really enjoyed the addition of rescuing people, since testing this one early on. Really great game.

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text:
Flappy Bird and Moon Lander make love and their baby was weaponized to become this game.

Beautiful! Thank you for giving me this great time.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Looks awesome, fun but very hard at the same time. Great mechanics and controls. I wanna say the controls feel loose in a way (esp on hard/normal) but I think they are supposed to and kinda have to really.

NO real score persay and wicked level design at parts, esp playing hard or even normal.

No real 13 theme past the name and number of levels.

Sound and music is eh.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Very fun game, as last year. Loved the chaos in some moments, and the helicopter controls (they remind me my entry to the JS13K 2022 called EVAC3D).

Not much comments, just keep the good work!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: I found it very challenging to control the helicopter. I think it’s a fun game, but I didn’t have the patience to control it for long. Good graphics and nice music.I couldn’t see how it fit the theme except for the name of the squad?

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: very cool game and one of my favorits and wanted to learn from but its really hard

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Controls are super interesting but hard to master, fortunately there is the “easy” mode !! The game is very polished and fun to play. Well done!

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: This is kinda addictive, love the gameplay it feels very good

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Fantastic Game, the controls feel perfect, the maps are challenging. It took me back to Atari days with way better graphics! Thank you for this game and great work!

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: I love the classic gameplay. The flying feels difficult in a good way. The particle effects from combat are great. Really fun entry

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I enjoyed the movement and rescuing the hostages. I really liked the animations for the rescue. Solid music. I had fun!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Great entry! Although the game isn’t my usual genre, I was impressed by the atmosphere and how will it was built. The controls felt smooth and responsive, and the visuals were impressive for a 13k game! Overall, a well-crafted experience with attention to detail.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Fun! Loving the graphics here, bring me back =)

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: Really polished, reminds me of a cabinet arcade game I played as a kid. Would have really liked a level select screen so I could come back without redoing all the intro levels. Good on Mobile too except for the missing fast-down button. A little insulted when the game asked me if I wanted to switch to easy mode 🤣

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
One of my favourites from this js13k so far! Theres too many good things to mention here, from the polished controls and graphics, to the challenging levels. The missile combat is a lot of fun, and making a game this fun in 13kb is a real achievement.

A neat addition as well, to have the score tweet at the end. The only things I feel are missing are some kind of end boss (maybe a big boss helicopter), some weapon upgrades, and I think it would be perfect to have different damage modes, so a lower speed collision with a wall could make your helicopter damaged and smoking, and chaotic to control - just to give you that extra bit of fun and the potential to still finish the level even after a light tap on terrain.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: A solid game to play and difficulty scales well across levels while learning new mechanics! Well done!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: The theme is not that well defined in this entry, but it sure is entertaining. Great visuals and effects.

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text:
Your game works on Apple Vision Pro. A plus for me. I like the dim sunset background on the back. The helicopter feels ok to control and I will always land on the spot.

Good job on the game tho.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Amazing work, as always. I completed normal difficulty in 17:52, rescued 12 prisoners, 31 crashes. For whatever reason, level 8 was the one that tripped me the most. I’m always so impressed by how you’re able to create such compelling graphics with basic shapes. It speaks to your taste, the color palette and sizing is always perfect. Game feel was just right, perhaps a little bit on the difficult side for a game jam entry, but I imagine a skilled player could sail through. Impressive work, great job.

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Nice controls and feeling with different day times and simple graphics. The competence theme is not much exactly followed.

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text:
Oh that game is good! And so hard! ;) I would be a terrible chopper pilot :D That night level really got me!

Very very well done! This feels almost too polished for game that small! All these details! Lovely!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: this game is incredible, one of my very favourites in this entire year’s js13k. it’s so polished: the graphics are amazing, the levels are well designed, the movement is just right.. I love the propeller motion blur effect, particles, and sfx too. and I absolutely love and appreciated the difficulty settings which allowed me to enjoy way more despite my lack of skill! thank you for including an easy mode!!!! =) really fantastic work.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Wow what an amazing game, 13kb or not, this is really well made.

The graphics were great, and very smooth. I liked the parallax effect as you flew along. The explosions were cool and I was glad the nearby explosions didn’t seem to affect my helicopter.

The music was atmospheric, and the sound effects added to the overall experience.

The levels got much harder on level 5 with the darkness and rain. Then on level 6 with the enemy helicopters too :)

Reminded me of a cross between Thrust (caverns/gravity/enemies) and Volcano (helicopter saving people) from the BBC micro

Overall a really impressive game, my kids loved it too

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Very fun and polished. Great overall game feel! I love the use of ZzFX sound! A simple save feature to continue where you left off would have been a nice addition because the levels get difficult, I want to come back later and try again. Also lacking on theme gameplay integration. Overall an outstanding game, congrats!

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: I don’t remember Choplifter being so difficult on Thompson MO5. Airwolf makes it look so easy…

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Choplifter, Spacetaxi, Moonlander garnished with a hint of Flappy Bird. Sprinkles on top with a bucket full of particle effects.

What an awesome game this is! Had much fun playing it.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Amazing gameplay, audio, and sound.

I played the whole thing through, and had a blast.

Great job.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is challenging but really enjoyable! “Easy to learn, hard to master” type of game. The flying physics are really fun

Not sure how many people encountered it, but it’s nice that you give an alert to change the difficulty if the player dies too many times, haha

By the way, I studied abroad in Murdavia, and it’s just as bad as you depict it in the game

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: A good redux of classic games with a great presentation (SFX and FX are amazing). I wish it added something new to the old formula of spaceship-chopper-needs-to-land-without-crashing, but other than that very well done!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Choplifter, Woohoo!
Flappy Bird, Oh No!

Great atmosphere, light, dark, rain, storm, EPIC!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
I really liked this one!

I liked the mix of the hard flying controls with rescuing and fighting. I would have preferred to be forced to aim my missiles by tilting, instead of an auto-targeting thing.

The graphics are great and the animation feels pretty smooth.

Overall a great entry!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
I think this is one of the most fun game I played in this jam. I kept wanting to get back to it. The controls are a bit rough, but it’s part of the challenge. Graphically, this is gorgeous. And the gameplay is fun because it’s difficult, but you can overcome the challenges by becoming better. Great work!

Oh and in terms of the theme… wait what was the theme?

comments.author: Nik Lever
comments.text: Simple physics works well. Fun to play. Effective graphics.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I must admit I had to pick the easy mode :D

Nice and polished! Congrats. Very fun game!

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: On top of polished gameplay, this has excellent graphics and tone. Those explosions are epic!
games: XIII Days To Save The Kingdom

comments.author: Igor Khotin
comments.text:
Very distinct and consistent style, super-polished gameplay, and great replayability. This game has everything any js13k game wishes for.

I enjoyed playing it, though it takes some time to master - my kid controls the helicopter so much better than me. And he still plays it, which tells a lot about replayability value. Levels were hard for me, but totally fine for my kid - so that’s my skill issue :)

It is hard to say what can be improved here. Destructible landscapes would be a nice touch, but not in a 13k game :)

Great job!

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: This is a really fun game! I love the animations, the story, and the general gameplay. Really well done!

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
While playing this game, my wife came into the room and said “You seem to be swearing quite a lot, is everything ok?”, “It’s just this bloody game!”, “Well, could you play something else? You’re scaring the dog.”

And she was right. But first, a little context. This is essentially a vector styled version of Choplifter. Pilot the helicopter around the 2D side-scrolling landscape, scouting out the little green prisoners aimlessly stood on the most precarious clifftops and drop a ladder down so they can climb aboard. Collect them all and make your way to the landing pad. If you’re lucky, and you don’t land upside down (like I did), hit the sea (like I did), hit a mountain (like I did), get shot out of the sky by an enemy chopper (like I did) or just rage quit in frustration (like I did), then you’ll rescue all the prisoners and move to the next level.

The game does make some affordances. You can toggle it into ‘easy’ mode, which makes for slightly smoother physics and less aggressive baddies. If you die to much on Normal mode, it handily suggests you do this. You can also shoot - which honestly, is great fun. The missiles arc across the sky in a beautiful trail, exploding on impact with enemy soldiers, the landscape and, more often than not, the prisoners you’re meant to be saving.

Graphically it’s stunning, too. Simple vectors, yes, but they work so well. The animation is good, the backdrops great, the rain effects, the lights, the sun. Just everything is coherent and works together, as does the sound and music.

My only major complaint? Next to nothing to do with the theme beyond having 13 levels. Although that does appear to be very common with lots of the entries this year, so I won’t hold it against it too much.

So there we have it folks: Squad 13 - dangerous for your sanity, your pets, your relationships if endured for too long and probably significantly harder than flying an actual helicopter. Damn good fun, though :)

comments.author: VincentBounce
comments.text: It’s so pleasant to play with the beautiful sunset, and the automatic outgoing scale to rescue people, I love it!

comments.author: Individooms
comments.text: Thanks for the fun game Thanks for the fun game

api.name: 13 Hours
api.description:
🕐 The Curse of the 13 Clocks

In a world where superstition and legends affect reality, there is a curse from an ancient clock with the number 13 in its mechanism. The clocks in the game represent time that is being altered or corrupted by this curse. Your mission is to remove all these clocks before time stops forever at 13 o’clock.

Game Description

In 13 Hours, your goal is to remove all the clocks in each level before the time runs out. If you complete the task in less time than the limit, you will earn a star as a reward. The more stars you collect, the more levels you can unlock.

The game has a total of 60 levels, each with different levels of difficulty and time limits. Additionally, 13 Hours includes an infinite mode where you can remove clocks without a time limit, allowing you to play and challenge yourself endlessly.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Nice clean graphics. Easy controls but not easy for me to play haha.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: This could be one of those games that I routinely pick up when I have a few minutes to kill. Great, fast paced puzzle solving game with a huge number of tiny levels to complete. Very innovative and clean execution. One of the most “finished feeling” games this year! Great work! A slight criticism is that the theme does feel a bit like an afterthought though.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Clean graphics, nice concept, but very repetive. I thought levels unlocked after would add some new features. For better accuracy, I think it should have been on “mouse down” and not “click” to trigger ball sending. sometime get the star is really hard when we spend 2 seconds waiting for a clock to end its turn, it can be frustrating

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
This has a lot of potential. A lot of levels, cool! (My usual complaint is, everything has too few levels, including my own games.)

Interesting use of DOM for graphics.

I think I disagree with making time the (scarce) resource. Would much rather double down on the puzzle element, and make the player collect stars by shooting through them during the level, or something like that.

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Interesting game idea. Love the super clean and polished minimalist visuals.

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text: Graphics are clean and how to play is clear.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Interesting entry, mostly because a lot of time, effort and polish were put into this I can tell. Unfortunately it is not for me. It gets repetitive very quickly, especially with the absence of audio as well.

However, I do think that the idea and execution along with the visuals and controls are great. Theme tie in is decent, but would be nice to have a little of that translate into the game itself.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The game looks very nice and polished but I found it a bit repetitive. Although the levels looked different, they all had the same task: align the clocks and click at the right time. I also had the feeling that there was a slight delay between clicking and the effect that made playing (and specially re-playing levels) a bit frustrating.

I would explore different mechanics to make the puzzles more challenging but interesting. I would also have liked to use another control instead of clicks, like the space bar.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: not very easy to play, very fast clocks or slow with very annoying precision that someone who did not know how to play would say.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
