The normal game has thirteen levels, the short game is, well, shorter, for those who just want to quickly see as much as possible to judge the game. The levels are randomly generated, so no two games will be the same.

If winning is either too hard or too easy for you, you can try to improve your score.

Moving

  • Use the number pad to move (arrow keys also work, but lack diagonal directions). Press shift to walk until you hit a wall or see an enemy.
  • Press > or < when you are on a ladder to climb down or up.
  • Press x to automatically explore the level (enemies will interrupt you).
  • You will automatically collect all items you walk over.
  • Or click on the place you want to go to (if you have seen it before), this also works for using ladders.
  • If you prefer the traditional hjkl-yubn-keys, these are also supported.

Attacking

  • You can perform both melee and ranged attacks.
  • For a melee attack just try to walk into the enemy.
  • For a ranged attack press f (followed by a number if more than one enemy is visible). Or click on your target.
  • Press w to wait a turn and W to wait until an enemy appears.
  • Press e to eat a mushroom (if you have one, might be good before tough fights). Or click on it in your inventory.
  • Note that your initial attack is quite weak, so try to find a horseshoe as soon as possible. Running away from enemies can be a good idea in many cases.

Other

  • Toggle the sounds by pressing m or using the button on the top.
  • Start a new game by pressing Q (or just reloading).

Mobile users please note: The game works without keyboard, but your screen should not be too small.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Hey, a nice little roguelike! Well done!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: I liked the fog of war aspect of the game, it gives it more depth, animations to improve, overall a nice adventure

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I love the game and it plays really well! This was done extremely well for what it is! Great work getting a nice rogue dungeon crawler working with 13k!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: I think a prolog level would make this game alot better

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
A solid rougelike-like. The “fog of war” is nice.

Would benefit from some music, and maybe some tweens in the movement (not jumping from A to B, but slide the player fast but smoothly to the new place)

I was surprised, that my keyboard seemed not to work properly as i wanted to sniff the code with [F12] or just reload the page with [F5]. Found out in the github sources that you catch and abort all keyboard-events… the higher-level keyboardcontrols seem to work (change Tab, oben new window/tab etc.) so thats… ok-ish.. somehow,.

I never heard of “Volapük” - thanks for the educational input :-)

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: Got destroyed multiple times but enjoyed the game so much! Look forward to beating it someday!

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Love randomly generated levels so you can’t “learn” them. Plus one for introduction loading screens.

I like the concept; having “fog of war” around the map is interesting and fun to explore.

Good use of sound effects, add some music for a bit more immersion. Very intuitive with the movement controls, but I did have to figure out to scroll the screen down to see the luck, items, level etc. Also I wasn’t sure what the newts were doing and ended up with a bunch of them following me 😛. I also got stuck trying to figure out how to go down the ladder - figured out to click with mouse, but I was using the keyboard, so would be great to configure this to also use “ENTER” on the keyboard.

I wasn’t sure how to use items too… Q didn’t reload the game for me. Pop ups for end of game would be much easier to read rather than the in-game scrolling text feed.

Good little description in your GitHub README. I like that you have different versions of the game.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text:
Actually, the game tries to scale to fit the available space, so no scrolling should be necessary. But unfortunately and for some reason I don’t really understand, when you play it inside the iframe it fails to account for the height of the header. In this case you should open it in a new tab, there everything will be visible as it should be.

As for starting a new game via keyboard, I should have been more explicit in the description: That’s a capital Q, so you need to press the shift key as well.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text:
First run: 1192 steps, 1 lucky charm, depth 5, 1259 points.

Second run: 2276 steps, 3 lucky charms, depth 8, 3423 points.

Well executed roguelike! Nice job with pixel art and sound effects. The “13” monster was adequately terrifying. Very impressive that you squeezed in so many of the traditional roguelike features like pathfinding and procedural map generation. Fun game! Nice job!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Great game, always enjoy this genre and you have done it well. I played the short game but lasted quite a while.

Not sure what the diamonds are for? I did keep searching for luck but ran out eventially. I found the controls nice and simple, just used the mouse.

Graphics are simple but good, I like the fog and the map being discovered as you go.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: The lucky charms are what you need to win (6 are hidden more or less randomly, for the last one you have to defeat Lord Balsekil) and are the largest contribution to the score. Apart from that they have no function, especially they have no influence on your luck or fighting.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Fun little dungeon crawler. Figured out it was easiest to kite enemies as I shot them so the newts never could hit me. Eventually died when I couldn’t range attack because there were 2 enemies but the number keys wouldn’t target them but instead moved me around, then wouldn’t move or attack at all.

Couldn’t go down ladders with the keyboard (< and > weren’t working, and neither were any other keys). Eventually found out that if I clicked the ladder with the mouse it would go down the ladder.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: I’m sorry to hear that keyboard controls didn’t work properly for you. I have no idea what could be causing this. You can also use the mouse for ranged attacks, by just clicking on the enemy you want to attack. (This doesn’t work when the enemy is directly next to you, as a melee attack will be performed in this case, but in that case a melee attack is better than a ranged attack anyway.)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A funny little charming game.

Going around the maze of rooms exploring was good.

I liked the pixel graphics and sound effects. The levels were pretty neat considering they were randomly generated.

I thought I’d found a goat with a beef against me, but the info panel said it was a newt.

The ravens were a whole new level, but the laser penguins? Wow some interesting enemies there.

I couldn’t work out how to go down the ladder when I found one and thought I’d pressed every key on the keyboard. So clicked on it with the mouse which worked.

comments.author: Michał Chojnacki
comments.text:
Just to touch on your sizing issue and your reply to Michelle: it’s not related to the iframe at all. It’s not that the browser or the site is not taking something into account - you are not taking into account your bottom bar (inventory etc.) nor any padding and borders during sizing, at all. It’s a basic resolution responsivity issue and it’s as simple as that. It exists regardless of the iframe.

Given you entered in the mobile category as well, I’d expect basic device size responsivity to be a fundamental aspect to it, but you’re not actually tackling it in a responsive manner.

Amongst others, browsers have resize and orientation change events. You are resizing your canvas on every render tick, which only happens after input, and contributes to the confusion of players, because you are effectively subverting basic UX expectations of how things on the web behave. Websites don’t resize when you click on a button - they resize when their window resizes.

With those in mind, I think it’s disrespectful to tell others - like Michelle in this case - that they should do something, i.e. that they basically should adjust to how you played and tested your game, instead of you actually debugging the issue and adjusting to everyone else.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I try to move but only arrow keys work, no wasd. Ive never used number pad and not sure what it was nor could I find hjkl-yubn-keys. I was unsure of what to do so just held left and right continuously killing newts until I die but no restart. I also couldnt get up or down ladders, which was my fault after forgeting all the keys. I tried searching as I type but It didnt help. I also think you might be using the wrong keyboard event as the Rogue doesnt move for a short time when held down. Hope this helps

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I really quite like this game! It felt fun discovering things, I liked the amount of enemies, though it did feel like I wasn’t taking damage when enemies were trying to harm me.

comments.author: Dann Sullivan
comments.text: This is a really fun twist on the Rogue, Dungeon Crawler formula. I’m reminded of the shareware and freeware dungeon divers that I used to love as a child - great work.

api.name: The Tridecomino Coral Reef
api.description:
The controls are just tapping and mouse.

I wanted to design prettier tiles, but ran out of time. There is is also no sound, so do not panic, your speakers/headphones are ok. I think it’s kinda playable, but not more than just a prototype. Only worked on this during the first few days of the jam and then I had other stuff to attend to.

I tried to leave the copilot prompts in the source code as much as possible.

Here is the text of the startscreen in case it doesn’t fit on your screen.

This mysterious reef is constantly
changing, but somehow the shape is
always a polyomino of 13 squares.

The many tourist boats are damaging
the reef. It is your job to chase
them away by gently tapping on them.

Boats can only move back and forth.
Red boats are above the reef.
White boats are not above the reef.

If there are no red boats left,
the reef is safe.

Good luck!

Tap on the boat to start...

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Strange game. I couldn’t make any progress and nothing felt like it was having any effect.

Repeating the game scene all over the screen was weird but could have worked better if the one in the middle was brighter or something. It was a bit confusing to begin with. Then it also scrolls the entire screen about which is totally whacko.

Still, an interesting concept that could have been much better if you were able to invest more time. Next year :)
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very well done pattern style game play. I like the ideas and the controls. The game played very well and it was rather soothing with a twinge of anxiety! Great work getting this game together.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice puzzle game, if it can be in one window instead of everywhere

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: I disagree with the other commenters about the multiple repeat of the game screen being a problem. I got used to it within 2 minutes. I will say that its a pretty fun game, but I can’t seem to mentally connect it to this year’s game jam theme.

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text: I like the simple controls and feel like there could be a very cool game here with a bit more time. There’s something sokobanish to it, but since boats can move back and forth, it doesn’t invoke that sense of dread when a box would be pushed in a corner, so that’s good too. It was difficult to gauge progress, but re-reading the readme made the objective clear at least. Tricky puzzle, but did manage to get to the #1 display on a re-play.

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing this!

First I started trying to solve it in a random way, but then began to think about the moves and came to the solution, which was a nice feeling of accomplishment.

As you mentioned yourself, it could of course use some more polishing.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Love the endlessly repeating level, we need more of this in puzzle games!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An interesting game and concept.

The graphics were good.

One I realised the map was duplicated things were a bit easier.

It would be good if you could rotate a boat without having to go all the way to it’s limit before turning around

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Rules are quite simple, some boats only move horizontally, others only vertically. Find a way to place them all out of a given random shape.

Challenging puzzle, controls are efficient and the scrolling following the active boat is a nice touch.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Nice one!

We can feel it’s a prototype, but I think you’ve mastered it very well. The mechanics are kinda fun and makes me wanna see a few puzzles with it.

Too bad you didn’t got further :)

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello!

Great work on having a working game for js13k! The puzzle works well and the wrapping of the world was really interesting. It took me by surprise on the first levels with the “Aha!” that I was focusing on the wrong side of the boats.

It took me a while to get started and as you mentioned, this was not part of your plan, but worth noting.

After some levels though I managed to get into a brute-forcing mode where I just got the sense that the boats could move and if they were stuck I just needed to move the line blocking them to some direction, and repeat the action until everything was unblocked and in place. There was a flow to this but I couldn’t keep going on once I had this method figured.

I hope you will find time to work on it, it feels different and has something going on for it.

comments.author: Quinten Clause
comments.text: I am aware of your brute-forcing method. If you repeat those steps (even in a random way) you will indeed always eventually solve the puzzle, because you are doing the same thing the script does when it generates a level. Only backwards. For the moment any boat can also reach any tile (I think). Some variation on the movement of the boats (or what is underneath the boats) would help to challenge the players who figure that stuff out.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Very cool idea! The audio and visuals left a bit to be desired, but that is of course understandable. Still, the main idea of puzzling together how to get all boats to not be on top of the reef is fun one. Good job!

api.name: Brewing Disaster
api.description:
You are a wizard and you want to be the very best!

To be so, you have to duel other wizards.

You share a cauldron with another wizard. This cauldron has an amount of putridity. Add ingredients to the cauldron to increase or decrease its putridity.

First wizard to reach an amount of putridity of 13 or 0 has lost!

Gameplay

Brewing Disaster is a deck-building game. You start with some ingredients and, after each victory, you can add new ingredients to your deck. Sometimes you can even improve some!

A game of Brewing Disaster makes you face 4 wizards NPC with their own deck and passive effects. You’ll duel them twice: the first encounter will be easy, but the second one will be thougher!

Win 8 duels in a row with a character to unlock another one!

Mobile devices

Even if it’s optimized for desktop, the game should be playable on tablets and smartphones (espcially on portrait mode).
But have in mind the experience is a bit degraded on small screens!

Decentralized challenge

OP Guild

Goal of the game is to beat 4 NPCs. Each of them has its own deck of ingredients (and passives for the second encounter).

We can unlock NPC to play with their ingredients as starter deck (well, that means they aren’t NPC anymore, but I hope it’s still OK, let’s say it’s original to transform an NPC into a PC :p).

Arcadia Challenge

This game is available on Arcadia.

Thirdweb

The first time a player looses a game, it unlocks an optional feature based on Thirdweb: being able to create and save its very own starter ingredients. When playing with a custom starter deck, we can’t unlock other characters: this is just a bonus to test things :)

Assets

All sprites have been made by myself.

Musics and sounds have been made thanks to ZzFXM and NoteCraft.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Great work. A good variety of characters, items, effects to renew the gameplay while the game progresses. Impressive to do all that, with music, plus a web3 character editor in just 13kb. I see a bit of CSS3D too! Congrats.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Thanks buddy :) Your help on CSS3D was very precious!

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Very funny concept, and an impressive array of customization. Throwing bats into cauldrons is always a great way to spend a few minutes.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Very glad you’ve liked this game, thanks for your nice feedback :)

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Fun little game, the npc cheats though since I always lost haha.

I really like the gameboy screenshot and look and feel of the game, great!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Haha, the bots play randomly for the first four encounters. That’s said, they start to play “smartly” after :)

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Fun game. Having to try to get to 0 or 13 makes for an interesting battle (versus just trying to get to 13). I liked the variety of ingredients and how you could modify them them.

The audio was very repetitive and a bit loud. I had to turn it off after a few battles. I would also have liked an option to skip picking ingredients. One of the strategic elements of a deck building game is knowing when not to add more things to your deck.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: I’ve indeed planed to create a “destroy ingredients” bonus, but I ran out of time for that :’D

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: It’s fun. It was confusing at first with all the symbols, but you quickly start understanding some symbols and memorizing the values of the ingredients. The rest is just wizard’s blackjack.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Would love to know how I could make the game less confusing. What are the concepts you find hard to understand?

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Great idea and concept for this year! The game plays well and has a nice complete feel. Great work getting this together!

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Thank you very much! I’m glad you enjoy this game :)

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: loved the gamepaly and the variety of levels, make you want to try finishing all of them and see whats next. sound is neat and fits game well, it is rather easy to win knowing it is a simple math problem but it have to fit the theme somehow.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: You’re the first one telling me it is “easy to win”: it’s very hard to create a game with a difficulty curve fitting for everyone ^^’

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: I love deck building, I’m not really objective for your game! Really nice tone of colors, real work on ambiance on background and “light” effects. Nice replay value with character unlocked

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: I’m glad you’ve noticed the “light” effect, it was surprisly harder to do that I’ve expected :D

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I’ve played your game a few times, the first of which I didn’t get the concept at all, but then realised it’s all about strategy and forcing your opponent to fail.

It was quite fun to play, and great for teaching kids some maths in a fun way.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Would love to know why you first didn’t get the concept, and how I could improve that part of the game! Thanks for your feedback!

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: I like the ida a lot. At first it felt too easy but started to get better after few wizards when it was visible that they don’t just add putridity. It got much more interesting when I actually died because my deck was full of + cards. I wish I had possibility to avoid the first death ;-),

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: The first four wizards play randomly, while the last four have a minor AI so that they play in a more intelligent way ^^ I though about some items to avoid death: this idea came to life in the form of the bonus “Expert of X”, allowing you to throw some ingredients safely without loosing :)

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: The fantasy font is completely unreadable on my system, making it more or less impossible to play.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Damn! This font is supposed to be a fallback for Impact font, meaning you don’t even have the main one… I’ve tested the game on multiple devices (Windows, Mac, mobiles, tablets) and I’ve never faced this issue… Sorry for that :(

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
Fun game that fits the theme perfectly

Great depth of the game while also keeping it simple

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Thanks for your nice feedback! :)

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Interesting game mechanics, but losing game after just one failed battle is annoying.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: My goal was to recreate the feeling of roguelikes, but you are not the first one telling me that ^^’ I should probably add one extra chance to avoid this frustration.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
This game was a lot of fun. I wasn’t able to beat the first NPC, and it was frustrating to start from zero after being defeated. It would be nice to have a few attempts, 1 or 2, to at least have a chance. The sound effects are nice, but the music was not to my taste. I appreciate the “mute” button, and it would be nice with one for sfx and one for music, because the sfx felt right. The graphics style is nice, and I like how you made the game Thumbnail. The game does feel like a GameBoy game.

The Thirdweb integration is really well made. From what I digested in the source code, it looks like you are saving the state on IPFS? I would love to know more.

Battling the NPCs with my own custom deck of cards was fun, and would love to see the integration with custom characters as well, and maybe with some special passive abilities. Overall a good game.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text:
Thanks a lot for your complete feedback!

My intent was to recreate the feeling of roguelikes where you have to replay from start after losing, but that’s true it would be nice to allow one or two defeats.

I did plan to have two mute buttons (one for music and one for sounds effects) but… I’ve just forgotten to implement that, and you make me remember that only now… ! Oopsy x’)

Thirdweb integration allows me to save custom decks, and only that (not the whole state of the game). In short:

  1. I identify the user thanks to a “private key” that should not be shared.
  2. I generate from this key a “public” one, which is used as a uniq public identifier.
  3. This public id is used as a file name on my own server. Each player has therefore its own file.
  4. On this file, I store the path to an IPFS file. This IPFS file contains all the decks data of the corresponding user.

Using my own server to create a link between public key and IPFS file was necessary ‘cause I wasn’t able to search for files via Thirdweb ^^’

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
What a neat integration of the theme! I also like the artstyle, and the game-mechanic of deck-building, and throwing stuff in one couldron.

Good game!

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The pixel art style is absolutely charming, with a great use of monochromatic colors that give the game a nostalgic, old-school vibe. The design of the cauldron and the various ingredients adds personality to the gameplay, and the simplicity of the visuals helps the player focus on the core mechanics. The overall aesthetic fits with the theme of brewing potions and wizardry. While the minimalism works well here, adding more animation when ingredients are mixed into the cauldron could enhance the gameplay experience. For example, small bubbles or visual reactions based on the ingredients would provide satisfying feedback for the player and make the game feel more alive. The visual consistency is excellent, and the retro-inspired design works very well in this setting. You’ve created a cohesive world that complements the mechanics beautifully. The pixel art feels polished, and the game is visually engaging. Fantastic work!

api.name: M13E
api.description:
M13E: Overcoming Triskaidekaphobia

Welcome to M13E, a compact yet thrilling game crafted for the js13kGames 2024 competition! This year’s theme, Triskaidekaphobia (the fear of the number 13), inspired me to create a maze game with a unique twist. Game Overview

Each level gives you 13 seconds to:

Find the exit before time runs out.
Collect coins scattered throughout the maze.
Avoid dangerous traps like lava and obstacles.
Utilize portals to walk through walls as the difficulty ramps up.

The further you go, the more coins and obstacles you’ll face, but you’ll always be racing against that 13-second timer.

comments.author: Aleksander Szczepura
comments.text: The time limit suddenly makes a simple maze more interesting. What ruins the experience for me are the controls, instead of looking for an optimal path through the maze, I keep getting stuck on the edges of the walls.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Brutally difficult and not for the faint hearted player!

Oh, those controls! The hero gets stuck on the maze walls and doesn’t slide. And the hero also move too fast so getting stuck is guaranteed, and overshooting corners is also guaranteed.

It might have been better if you started with some smaller mazes to ease the player into the gameplay, the size of the maze is too difficult for a new player I think.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Wasn’t sure if the maze was procgen, but I’m guessing not. The countdown aspect added to the jeopardy of completing the maze.

It was quite hard to play because the walls were “sticky”, there was nothing to tell me how to move the dot, after trying mouse and WASD, found arrow keys did move bottom right dot.

13 seconds isn’t long before you’re thrown back at the start.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Controls are very bad… I spend more time fighting corners than searching for an exit.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Interesting game. The controls are a bit frustrating because the walls are “sticky”. Wasn’t able to collect any coins within the time limit.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: agree that 13 secods give a spark but the control should have a more fitting ease of use, also if some graphics added more view.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Great work! This game is a nice and fun concept. The controls are challenging and I think that plays well into the time part of the game. I like that I am able to press 2 directions at the same time! Great work getting this game together!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Well made entry, functions well and has progression, I think you could have granted more time when the coins were collected.

When you develop a game you get so good at it you have to nerf it a little.

I got to 3 coins and just could not get all 3 in the time, the collisions made it hard to travel as fast as I felt I needed too.

With a few power ups, stop the clock, extra time, speed boost etc this could be more fun.

Still a good entry.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
Neat concept, mazes are always fun. This one is tough though! Could barely pass the first few levels. I think with some more polished controls (ie less sticky player) it could be a bit more forgiving.

Good work getting a game together with bytes to spare!

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text: Really cool idea and very close to good execution. The controls/collision detection just ruins it from getting stuck on walls so frequently, like everyone has commented.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: The time limit makes it pretty frantic! I was able to manage one coin, but two coins proved too tricky. The biggest obstacle is getting stuck on corners, which is quite tricky. Nice job, congrats on submitting!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Getting stuck on corners makes the game immeasurably more challenging than it looks.

Nice interpretation of the theme!

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text: This game is very hard. I barely managed to get to three coins. I browsed the code and it looks like there’s lava and portals on later stages, but unfortunately I wasn’t able to see them due to the difficulty. I do wonder if this was intended or perhaps a bug in how you handle input? If you’re planning a post-jam version, consider being more forgiving around corners!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: It should have been fun, but hitbox ruined my experience. You should add more permissive collisions, or make movement step by step to avoid player being stuck by an edge. I cannot beat the 2 coins challenge

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Seems to always be the same maze, how about different layouts for multiple challenges ?

As far as controls are concerned, I wish you could cut a corner and not be blocked by a single pixel, it quickly gets annoying

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
The 13 seconds are hard, but fair.

If the player would not get stuck on a wall when moving diagonally, that would be a huge enhancement for the gameplay.

And, as my (and many other gaming laptops) laptop has a 144Hz screen which simply results in much more fps and calls of the update-methods. So now the player characte moves so fast that it is really hard to stop at the right position to enter a specific passage of the maze. heroUpdate() should take the time since the last update as factor for the position-update.

Anyways: Congrats on completing a game under 13 kB! (10 kB in fact)

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Maze time—well done! To be honest, the movement was pretty hard. I do like the attempt to finish before 13 seconds. The controls and movement can be polished to make them easier to access.

api.name: Not 13
api.description:
Game where you pick an item that doesn’t have 13 of it on the screen as fast as possible to get high score!

Challenges

Avalanche & Thirdweb

Implemented game themes using Thirdweb Typescript SDK that gets NFTs from Avalanche blockchain that determine look, feel and colors of the game.

OP Guild

NPC chick at the top left gives a hint to user to confuse them as it’s not always correct.

NFT NPCs switch game themes for the player

Arcadia

Published the game on Arcadia platform - https://arcadia.fun/games/2ce9da72-386a-49d1-a06c-fc729c45fed8

Ad Block / Brave Browser

On some devices (and browsers) ad block prevents game from loading in for some reason. Disable it to fix the issue.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Nice use of emojis, CSS, and SVG.

I love the fireflies and how you used CSS to blur them all.

I thought maybe the objects could be spread out a bit more horizontally and the clickable buttons could be larger. Maybe allowing the player to press 1-5 on the keyboard to select their answer might allow them to be more optimal when chasing a good score :)

My brain was too lazy to play a lot because I just had it wrecked by the maze game (M13E) haha. Nice stuff though.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Martin Tale
comments.text:
Thank you :)

Yep, I wanted to have hotkeys 1-5 but apparently somewhere along the way forgot about that :D

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Simple but effective!

Not much to say, besides nice work.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Great game! I enjoyed it while you were creating it, I enjoy trying to beat my own time as well! Great work!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Nice job! My best time was 13.323 seconds. I could get 18-19 seconds by actually looking and guesstimating. But then I figured that with 80%*60%*40%*20%, you should be able to get lucky with a blind run. Probably not how you intended for people to play the game! Nice job, polished graphics, sound, and gameplay. Congrats!

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
Fun game! Very nice and polished graphics and animations. And fits the theme perfectly.

I think control-wise it could benefit if I could also click any of the items in the game field to make a selection. I tried that a couple of times by reflex. Would avoid a context switch from I have identified the item to I need to find that item in the button row below.

On some point I noticed the hint and tested that feature and sometimes it was giving me wrong hints… Is that on purpose?

comments.author: Martin Tale
comments.text:
Thank you 🥰

Yes, the hints sometimes being wrong is on purpose - explained in game description. I was just trying to do something unconventional with NPCs for the challenge 🙂

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text: A pretty simple neat game that avoids the count of 13. I have some Arcadians but sadly it takes a bit of time to set up.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Won in 26 seconds from 5 attempts :).

Nice graphics and sound. Nice to play :). A rather non-standard take on the theme, but it suits it well.

The moving background is cool :).

I’m not sure what the chick is doing, but it’s cool :).

I think a leaderboard with all the players would be perfect here!

It was fun, congratulations!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An enjoyable little guessing game.

Trying to pick the one with the fewest symbols is fun. Some levels are easy done are really tricky when it’s closer.

But the graphics were good and the heart beat style tension music was fun.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The vibrant, colorful icons immediately catch the eye and give the game a playful, casual feel. The bold outlines and easily recognizable shapes help with clarity, ensuring players can quickly identify and react to the game’s mechanics. The clean, dark background allows the bright icons to pop, making it visually easy to follow the action. The game’s visuals are sharp, colorful, and do the job of keeping the player engaged. The use of icons with distinct designs makes the gameplay intuitive and fun. You’ve created a light-hearted and enjoyable visual style that matches the gameplay well. Excellent work!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Picking game-themes with the NFTs is a great idea for the challenge, and also makes me want to try all the themes to see how they look. The NFTs blend well with the look and feel of the game too. The UI is beautiful as always, and the gameplay is simple, yet fun. I always want to try to beat my score, but it ends up being a game of luck.

I tried to click the symbol inside the grid directly, but clicking the row on the bottom is still easy to understand.

api.name: Get the Flock Outta Here
api.description:
The story of our hero Tony and they’re friends who must gather the lost sheep and return them to their home.
A simple task you would think but Tony wasn’t to know who they would encounter along the way or the obstacles that they would face.

Based on a true story.

Controls

W, A, S, D Arrows to move
Space to perform action
Mobile touch controls

Reviews

‘I’ve never seen anything like it’ - The Northampton Tribune
‘Speechless’ - The Prescot Trumpet

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Congrats on the graphics and audio, impressive.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Very nice graphics and an interesting little game.

Once you have seen the instruction stuff and have to restart its a real pain to have to sit through all of that every time. Space bar should be present to skip all messages.

Having said that about the overly long stuff you have to sit through, it didn’t even mention that water is not a death trap. I was doing so well until I decided to take a shortcut through a puddle.. arghhh.

Ditto regarding the mud :(

Pretty neat game.

P.S. I also so desperately desired to move diagonally :D
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I remember one time when herding sheep that I was told never to go in water as its a death trap and also avoid mud or holes as they also mostly resulted in death, so I guess I took that for granted. The skip counter would have been active If you played one more game I think but I will reduce it next time.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Nice presentation. Controls felt stiff,and with such a relentless map, I died more times than I was expecting.

Moving faster would had help the game to feel more fun.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Love the humour. This sheep are dumb as… sheep. And dong the side quests just caused me to lose most of my sheep. Fun game.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I possibly made it a bit too difficult. The follow mechanic works once you get the hang of it but hey, theyre sheep! :D The timed levels are probably too hard admitedly.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Well done! I like being a sheep herder and I love the idea of losing the respect of the sheep. Very well done and nice graphics!

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Exactly 13kB, nice work! Very Minecraft vibes.

Introduction screen is very good. Love the personification; Tony as the main character, but cool that you can change characters. Sound effects are fun and on theme, a little bit of music would make it even more intuitive.

Love the RNG; each map is very different. I definitely lost a lot of sheep though! The narratives are really good and you out a lot of thought into this. Great work.

“Based on a true story” - I’d like to know more 😉 Nice job on your GitHub README.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I saved 10 sheep with a score of 3610. Great graphics and sound effects.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Thats a good score and 10 sheep is amazing. I think thats more than Ive ever got :D. Ive never got all of them. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: Just like everyone else is saying, it’s a little painful to go through the intro everytime I replay the game. Other than that, everything is pretty cool!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
Great graphics and so many characters to play! It’s amazing that you were able to fit so much content, dialog, and great indicators when you’re near an objective. The humor is great too.

But there’s much to be desired if you want people to play the game all the way through.

  • The game is deceivingly easy. The cute graphics were very welcoming and friendly. I think it could play much better without deaths. The game visually gives off Animal Crossing vibes; why not make it friendly like that?
  • Movement is very slow, rigid, and icy. I’m on grass, why am I sliding? and I have a very long way to go. Please let me move faster and diagonally. I achieve it by tapping the direction I want to go diagonal.
  • After completing a few missions and then “falling” into a tiny blue square I didn’t see, I did not want to start over again. A respawn without losing progress would have been really nice.
  • The sheep following me are so cute but with rigid movement, it’s really tough to keep them from falling into the many water and dirt holes.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: A friendly mode is in interesting thought, will consider. Im struggling to find slow rigid and icy unless you mean Trevor, but hes in a wheelchair! he has momentum like anything on wheels. Im not doing diagonal movement. Its clearly crossy road inspired with its look and feel and movement mechanic and no one said that about CR. It would break the game if I did I believe but thanks for the feedback

comments.author: retro-pigeon
comments.text: Pretty interesting, but the tutorial is really lengthy and can’t be skipped

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing the game

Graphics reminded me of a crossy-road type game. Animation was good, and the 3D worked well.

The music did become a little repetitive, the sound effects were good.

Love the credits and that all the sheep have names.

The sheep would get “stuck” behind trees really easily, which made it a bit frustrating.

I didn’t realise that the first time you go to the home space the game would end, I think it could be better if you could go out to pick up more sheep because otherwise the following flock grew so large that it was hard to navigate around obstacles without losing them

Overall a fun game.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Interesting idea about the home. I never even thought of it but depositing them in batches is an idea to consider. I was too single minded about herding them all up and bringing them home 😃 It sort of makes its own difficulty in that the more you collect the harder it is to navigate. Thanks for the feedback

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Oh those dumb sheep… pathfinding-skills: zero. I guess thats the point of shepherds and shepherd-dogs… :-)

Nice game, with cool 3d graphics! And pretty fun!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Low-key the most stressful game submitted this year, haha. I drowned so many sheep :(

Very impressive, though. Cool graphics, very funny story elements, lots of interesting mini-games. The main “sheep herding” concept, with lots of sheep following your character is really cool, too.

Also nice that you can choose from a bunch of characters.

I agree with some others that a “restart” feature would be nice. Kinda rough to start the game over when you die

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text:
Love that voxel style.

Great sense of humour too!

I never imagined sheep herding could be such an ordeal; catching thieves, evading crazy mobs. This was tricky and a lot of fun!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: That’s only the half of it! I had to remove some mini games. Luckily i managed to recover them for some sort of directors cut! Strap in, life on the farm is hard. 😉 Thanks for playing 😃

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
One of the earliest “home micro” games ever was sheep herding - and still this concept appears constantly. Thankfully, this version is no wolf in sheep’s clothing and has seriously cute voxel / Crossy Road style graphics and a nice English sense of humor (I especially loved the “Pair of nickers” mini game).

Your task is to round up all the sheep and guide them to the home pen. When you get there, it’s game over, so you have to get as many as you can rather than doing them in batches (which, honestly, would have been significantly easier!). As you traverse the grassy landscape you’ll encounter other characters that invoke mini-games that you play with your sheep in tow. These are sure-fire ways to accidentally kill off the entire flock as they dumbly plough themselves into tiny puddles and ditches.

Easy, this game is not. I enjoyed playing it a lot though. I liked the humor, I loved the credits roll at the end, I liked the graphics, and honestly, even after several attempts and despite feeling extremely guilty at all the deaths on the way, I did manage to save Pattie, Colin and Jimmy, and for that, I will be eternally grateful.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Thank you, and the sheep (that survived) thank you. Difficulty, is something ill take away from this year. I make stuff far to difficult. Im glad you got the ‘nickers’ level. I was worried it might not translate that well. ☺️🐑

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: good graphics and 3d game very mc like

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: This is a very good looking game full of humor! It took me some time to become good at it, though. The music could be better but the sound effects are really good. And thanks for adding mini-games here and there. Overall, it is a cute little game (but not an easy one!).

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Thank you. Gauging difficulty is a true art, which ill take away from this years competition. I think its a muti play game to get good at it, which perhaps doesnt work that well in this sort of competition. Thanks for playing 😀🐑🐏

api.name: The Way of the Dodo
api.description:
Welcome to the Dodo Dojo

Can you clear all the 13 chambers of fowl play ?

And finally help set the dodos free ?

Instructions

  • Space/tap to jump
  • Release jump to break jump
  • Hold jump to do a wall jump
  • In air tap jump rhythmically to glide/flap

Game made w LittleJS.

Music is procedurally generated.

comments.author: ZZ
comments.text: First one on the list I completed! Great game, well done!

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Really clever design! It’s amazing how much control you can have over the dodo, using just one button.

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: finished twice on mobile, I enjoyed so much the gameplay and the all the possible moves with one single control. I want more levels! :)

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Fun, nice graphics, music, controls and animation when the pixel-art dodo flies out of the level

comments.author: Rustie
comments.text: I really enjoyed playing this game on my phone. It was a great intuitive platformer with nice graphics and audio. The controls were easy to grasp and I liked how the dodo would take off when you completed a level, fly through space/night to the next level.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
I didn’t think I was gonna like this but was pleasantly surprised.

Really nice polish and the gameplay is pretty cool. The jumping mechanics are especially nice and could be used in so many other games.

Great entry!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: One button, so much fun! Great! The particle effects are quite good (ouch, spikes!), and the levels are fun to play. Good entry!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Great entry!! in my top 10. Loved the fast paced action and the presentation. You pay attention to the details and that pays off.

No suggestions. Great work!

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Absolutely amazing game, I like that you instantly know how to play. The concept of the Dodo bird is very well done. I like the feel of this game as well, the climbing of the walls was fantastic. Well done!

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: Very polished, fun gameplay. The music is great and I love the dodo squawking.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is awesome! So fun to play. Some levels were pretty challenging, but eventually I could beat all 13 levels.

Death animation is kind of hilariously brutal.

Also enjoy the squawking sound effect. Curious to see how you did the music, as you mention that it’s procedurally generated.

I need Way of the Dodo 2

comments.author: Jesper Rasmussen
comments.text:
Thanks for your kind review - glad you liked it :)

I’ve just written down some notes on how I did the music:

https://github.com/repsej/TheWayOfTheDodo

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Really nice game and very playable. Love the between level bit. Completed eventually.

comments.author: Santi Herranz
comments.text: I’ve had a lot of fun playing this game! It runs like a charm on mobile devices and offers an incredible little experience. The one-button controls for wall climbing and jumping are just amazing. Each level is challenging enough to keep you fully engaged and excited. Plus, the music and sound effects are fantastic. Kudos to the developer!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Lots of fun. Works well on mobile, and all levels were enjoyable. The music, sounds and juice all help elevate the game experience. The transition from a level to another is really great, such a nice way of presenting the game.

On my iPhone, the «share» context menu appears. I had the same issue in my game, and I think the following can fix it:

On the body element, add oncontextmenu=“return false” ondragstart=“return false”

Not a major issue, but thought I’d share just in case

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Passed the game on the second try!

When I died the first time, I was a little scared, as it was demonstrated :). I also liked the transition effect of each level.

It was fun! Congratulations!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Overall really fun, some nice details in there, the bird flying away, running out of time!

Fun to play and challenging levels, flapping could be a little easier, was bashing my space bar.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: This game was crazy fun to play and every level felt right in difficulty. I can see myself playing this over and over again!

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Simple, yet polished.

Had a great time playing this.

Awesome job.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: This game is so fun to play! The “one button control” concept is very interesting , I found the game addictive and I loved when the dodo flies at the end of the level, very funny!

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
This is so well made, congrats! The controls feel amazingly tight, the wall jumps are satisfying, and the flapping feels overpowered. All this in a one-button game!

I really like the transitions between levels, with the previous stage fading away in the distance. Very cool.

My only complaint is that it’s too short. I had a lot of fun completing the 13 chambers and now I’m ready for more!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text: Nice game! Everything is well polished and connected. Congrats!

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Great game, felt very fun and solid! I love the main character, great pixel art and sound effect to match. The single button gameplay feels really well done here with a lot of flexibility for player to control. I also love the procedural music and how it changes each room so never gets old. Cool level transitions were a nice touch. I would love to see this concept expanded on and it works great as a mobile game. Also, I love the retro arcade game joust and this reminds me of that too. Fantastic work!

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: There’s a surprisingly amount of polish and eye candy for such a simple game. Excellent difficulty balance!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text: I really love that I could play this on my phone and the tap only controls were very intuitive and had multiple functions. It was a fun play through.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text: Great fun! Excellent game!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A really neat little single input platformer.

Loved the graphics, the pixel sprites, levels and animations.

The death animations were funny.

Thought I was getting good until the levels appeared with spikes!

On mobile the highlight bubble came up sometimes.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Fun game! The visuals are cute and goofy, and the music is perfect. The sound effects are also very fitting. Everything is very polished and mostly cohesive. If I could improve anything, would be to give it a proper pixelated font.

The gameplay is really simple, requiring barely any instructions. I love that the mini-level in the intro screen works as “start button” and tutorial. The levels were fairly simple, with a soft difficulty curve. Apart from one level, I left the allotted time was plenty enough to finish all levels, and the difficulty never increased much. If anything, I would love more and more challenging levels. I liked the not-so-subtle hint “FLAP”. I would probably have wasted a lot of time trying to figure that one out! :D

I love the transitions between levels. Very creative.

The theme fit is a stretch.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Great game!

Well made platformer with very good gameplay and fun levels. Lovely main character. It’s full of nice little details as Dodo flying away at the end of the level. The main menu doubling as level / tutorial is a nice touch.

comments.author: Lance Ewing
comments.text: A game that becomes quickly addictive. I like that the controls are so simple and yet there is a surprising amount of differing control achieved through only a single key. I liked the graphics and the generated audio. Well polished!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Thanks for your submission! I really enjoyed the idea behind a single key platformer! And congratulations on having a complete game under 13kb!

I really enjoyed the graphics and the level transitions were top-notch! I felt indeed like a free bird :)

The jump/control was a bit finicky on the timing and I’m not sure I figured out exactly what was expected. In any case I managed to finish the game with 53083 points.

Also, great flex in packing multiple songs into it :)

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text: The single key platformer is really fun and easy to control. It took me not long to find out I can wall jump by just holding. I like how the DODO gets big and the level just shrink as if the DODO is flying a well. Simply loved how you can fit every content in 13kb. Good job,

comments.author: Igor Khotin
comments.text:
Simple yet complicated. It is amazing how many mechanics one might get out of one-button controls.

It is fun and engaging. Really enjoyed playing it.

comments.author: VincentBounce
comments.text: So addictive with only one key to control!

api.name: 13-Blades
api.description:
The 13-Blades are a notorious gang that follow the mantra of might makes right. They picked the wrong mark this time and you’ll show them who’s might is the greatest.

Controls:

  • a,d,left,right - move left/right
  • w, up - jump
  • space - swing sword facing direction
  • s, down - swing sword down

UI Explanation:

Player health are the hearts in the top middle of the screen. Player rage is the square boxes below health; increase this by killing enemies. The more rage, the more damage done.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: It would have been nice to disable page scroll entirely (the page moves when I play with arrow keys)

comments.author: kruffin
comments.text: Doh, just goes to show I should have tested with keys other than wasd after seeing they initially worked and moving on. Thanks for the bug report; it is annoying to see the screen jiggle up and down while playing. Can only recommend using wasd for the best experience then. Thanks again for the comment.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: The jumping down attack is AMAZING well done! it runs and works great and it is captivating!

comments.author: kruffin
comments.text: Thanks for the kind words; glad you enjoyed it.

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: After that cutscene and the stylized pixelart title screen, I must admit you had my hopes up for a more elaborate art style and gameplay.

comments.author: kruffin
comments.text: :) I still laugh when I go from the main screen to the game. It was an even starker contrast before I darkened the colors up in the main game.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Attack controls were pretty hard to use, and I wasn’t expecting touching an enemy to damage me.

The page scroll was pretty disturbing.

Other than that, nice job.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: I loved the intro and title screen! I wish my sword was two pixels longer; it felt like I had to get a bit uncomfortably close to my enemies; alternately, what if you were allowed to bump into and push around the enemies? or block! and double jump! and throw shuriken! ok ok ok I am such a featurecreep machine, my apologies. nice work! =)

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Pretty intro screen, but game is really hard. Controls are hard, I think there is a small latency between the time I hit and when sword is activated. Enemies should have been killed in one hit, or be stucked for a second after a hit, bacause I’m also hit almost everytime

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The art style is way more goofy that I expected.

The game has scrollbars due to a margin in the HTML element, so it keeps scrolling up and down. It’s a bit annoying.

The music seems to have taken a long time to load.

Kudos for the procedurally generated levels!

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text:
Cute endless runner, I loved the tiny sprites, reminded me old school Lemmings and Worms games.

The physics need to be improved a bit, anyway good job.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Very cool entry!

I love the downward sword move the best, really lends a good Zelda II vibe to this mini game. Movement was pretty good, but like someone commented the delay after you swing was a bit odd since the enemies didn’t seem to delay on hit either. I used the downward sword on them most of the time.

Boss is pretty challenging too; I should probably build my rage meter up more next time before fighting him!

Nice job!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I liked the look of the game.

Music was good.

I like the whole platformer dynamic, and the feeling of a kind of samurai battle.

The end of level boss was hard, no idea how to hurt them as much as they hurt me. So ended up feeling a bit one sided.

api.name: Triskai-deka-phobia card game
api.description:
Use cards to attack enemy and defense yourself.

Pick a card to collect attack or defense points. If you will collect 13 or more points in hand you lose your cards.

There are point cards from 1 to 11. 13th card is a Death card. 12th card is a Shamrock card. It will protect you from Death card.

You are granted one Shamrock card at start.

Good luck and win with as many green bombs as you can.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice card game, I like that there is a set card deck so you have an idea of what is left in each pile. Great work and nicely made game!

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thanks. I’m glad you liked it. The dekcs are randomly shuffled but I definetly thought it would make it better to see that the cards are taken out of it.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Very well polished mobile game. At first I was confused by the AT, but got the hang of it on my second try. The up-to-13 mechanic works excellently. However at Lv. 8 I drew XIII, I and XIII again ;/ felt like the game decided it was enough and I need to go down :)

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. I’m glad you liked it. That was my goal to keep it simple but polished with some degree of detail in visuals and animations. btw if the game decided you have to die the you have to die ;-)

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Interesting game, easy to play on mobile too. There is a sound when CPU discard, but maybe it should be better to display values: how many attack and defense enemy got, how many life point are remaining to player or enemy, so it can add a bit more strategy to make decision of attacking or defending

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thanks. The feedback about the attack/defense/HP numbers is something that totally makes sense and I plan on adding as next steps in the game.

comments.author: Logan Tempez
comments.text:
Nice game! The quality is top-notch. It’s very polished, and it’s truly a pleasure to play. I didn’t fully understand the AT system for the rounds at first, but I got the hang of it over time. The level of detail and the visuals are really impressive.

That said, sometimes the XIII mechanic feels a bit too punishing. Drawing a XIII and losing without having the chance to react can be frustrating.

But once again, congrats! You’ve done an outstanding job!

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thanks you for playing. Also thanks for the insights. AT system info and some changes to the death would be great. I acteally planned on some basic info screen or mini tutorial but was not able to finish before the due date ;-)

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: This game is really fun and addictive! I got used to it eventually, but I didn’t understand when I could attack. I realized later that you attack automatically when your AT bar is full. It would be helpful if there was a question mark symbol with explanations for HP, AT, and the cards in a simple modal. Mini tutorial takes our dev time and space but the modal would have been sufficient.

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thank you. Mini tutorial / info screen is a very good idea. The attack mechanic was also surprising and a little bit confusing to me but I couldn’t figure out anything better yet simple enough ;-).

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An interesting turn based card game.

The graphics were good, the animations were fun and the sound effects tied the experience together.

There was a heavy luck element, so I didn’t really feel like there was much skill involved or stuff I could control.

How do I start an attack, I just seemed to be able to collect cards that disappeared if I went over 13.

It would be good to see the opponents cards on the table too.

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thank you for the feedback. Both things were definetly something I was thinking about but didn’t come to the compo version. That is my plan for potential continuation to focus on more control, attack mechanic and some possibilites to know more about the opponent. Seeing opponents carsd is a good idea.

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
It’s a cute little game with nice graphics. I like the appeal of the game and its simplicity. I haven’t read the description, but I got the gist after the first attempt. And I died in stage 3 on the second attempt.

Polished experience.

comments.author: rkowalczyk
comments.text: Thanks for playing and feedback. That was exactly my goal to have something simple yet polished.

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text:
The layout is pretty neat for a card game. Some explanation about the red and yellow cards would be nice. Also a tooltip about what cards the green bomb collected would be nice.

Some time my knight HP is just gone and I don,t know whether the game is over so any indication would be nice.

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text: A turn-based card battler with really good graphics and polish. I couldn’t figure out how to form a strategy, because the gameplay seemed to be simply picking between two colors of cards. Nice presentation and appreciated the sound effects. Good job!

api.name: Dekatria Mansion
api.description:
Count Dekatria’s Mansion Welcome to Count Dekatria’s Mansion, a thrilling strategic board game where your characters must navigate rooms, solve puzzles, and avoid being expelled by Count Dekatria! The game is based on the concept of Triskaidekaphobia—the fear of the number 13. Can you outwit the Count before it’s too late?

Gameplay Overview In Count Dekatria’s Mansion, you control a party of 5 characters. Each character has special abilities that will help you navigate the mansion and solve its puzzles. As you explore, you’ll face challenges tied to the unlucky number 13. Your objective is to score points by matching characters with rooms and using their abilities to overcome obstacles.

However, beware! Every time a 13 is rolled, you get closer to being expelled from the mansion. If 13 is rolled 13 times, Count Dekatria will expel you, and the game will end!

How to Play

  1. Selecting Your Party You must select 5 characters from the Character Selection Area. Each character has a unique ability that can help you during the game. Once your party is selected, click the Start Game button to begin.
  2. Moving Your Characters Roll the dice to move your characters. Dice results range from 10 to 16, and each number corresponds to a specific room in the mansion. Drag and drop characters from the hallway into the room that matches the rolled number. Characters can only move to certain rooms based on the mansion’s layout and broken walls.
  3. Using Abilities Characters have special abilities that can be activated when in the right room or situation. For example, Muscle Joe can break through walls, and Magnify Mark can reveal all hidden emojis in a room. Use abilities strategically to clear rooms and score points.
  4. Matching Emojis Each room contains hidden emojis that correspond to certain characters. If a character matches an emoji in a room, they score a point, and the emoji is revealed. The goal is to reveal 13 or more emojis in the mansion to open room 13 and slay Count Dekatria.
  5. Handling a Roll of 13 Every time the

comments.author: Jselitto
comments.text: Great game utilizing them and thoughtfully created fun to play

comments.author: Doughboy3333
comments.text: Absolutely addicting game! Super creative and pretty intuitive from the start.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Interesting board game! I used Magnify, Muscle, Ghost, Bat, and Pumpkin. My score was 15/13. I cleared the left half of the map, but had 2 remaining slots on the right half. The drag-and-drop controls were well implemented, and always worked as I wanted. The room probabilities was a nice touch to save me from the mental math. Overall, this feels like it has potential. It’s clear that you put a lot of work into it. Great job!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Once I got to grips with the controls this was a lot of fun, got so close so many times but just could not win.

Hard to remember the different character traits, leaving the tool tips active on them would have been nice, or putting the info somewhere on screen.

Really well made, some music and extra graphics, sounds etc would have made it even better.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: In my third attempt I scored 11/13 which is not too bad !!! The rules are not simple to understand, but once you got it, the game is very interesting. The theme is well represented. I suggest you to add some sound effects and/or music to make this game even better :)

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text:
It’s nice to see a board-like game in the competition. The theme fits well with a dice-based gameplay. After several attempts I was able to finish the game!

It’s good that there is a help text showing what to do next. However, there are many elements on the screen competing of the player’s attention, so it can still be at times a little difficult to figure out what to do next. I think it would be better to show things when they are relevant at each moment. For example, when selecting the characters at start, only the character selection parts could be visible (or alternatively, the other elements could be dimmed). And when the game starts, then show the dice, etc. That way, there would be less need for writing long instructions (as people usually are too impatient to read them :). On the other hand, the tooltips on characters do a good job on guiding the player when it’s relevant. So, good things here on that regard and I believe that it pays off to make things as obvious to the player as possible, on every opportunity.

Also, I think another way to improve would be to make it clear right from the start what is the ultimate goal of the game (in the description text or in the game). For example: “Collect 13 emojis so that you can slay Count Dekatria”.

The board layout looks good and the dice animation is a nice touch. Having the emojis and a little storyline is enough to spark imagination, so I’m not missing any fancier graphics. Good work, and with some more strategic elements it could be even better.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Interesting concept for a game. I liked that there were different abilities for each character. I never did figure out what Muscle Joe’s ability really did since it wouldn’t activate unless the door he was sitting in front of rolled, at which point he would always enter.

I could never roll the numbers for the right side rooms. Seems too lucked based and there wasn’t anything I could do to improve my chances of rolling the right side numbers. Near the end of the game I was just rolling over and over again because I only needed 2 numbers, which wasn’t very fun.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: it seem alot of work has been put in this game, remind me of the board game inspection and its kind of fun to plan the win

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An interesting game, with lots of scope for varied play.

It did feel a bit like I’d just got this new boardgame and I was trying to play it without first reading the rules. (even though I had).

The concept was good, the character abilities added to the skill of putting them where they were needed, to get the ticks against the emoji.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I tried the game for a while, but i really don’t understand it.The gameplay is not only very complex, but things happen around the board without me noticing. Everything on the bard has the same vivid colors, nothing seems to be specially highlighted. There are no animations or transitions.

I’m confused why it says only the muscle and the magnifying glass have working effects. If the game incomplete? Other characters seem to have some effect sometimes.

I think it’s clear you put a ton of work on this game. Making board-style games is really tough, specially with the size and time restrictions. I just couldn’t understand it or have fun with it.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: There’s a lot going on at the same time in this game. It was difficult to understand how to proceed, and I don’t know if what I did was good or bad. Maybe limiting the UI and elements can help better understand the gameplay. The use of Emoji is clever, and I think the drag and drop functionality worked well. I’m glad there were instructions on screen, otherwise I wouldn’t know what to do.

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: This took me a while to figure out, although not much longer than a standard board game you’d find at a board game night. The abilities of the characters are pretty crucial to understand, it would be nice if they could be referenced after the game started. As it was, I had to have another tab open to hover over what they did to remind myself. It would also be nice if I could send characters back to their starting positions with a button after having exited the basement, but not a game breaker by any means. I did like how the dice rolled one at a time.
games: XIII Days To Save The Kingdom

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: This feels almost like a board game! I really like the amount of depth you got in there, and do like the emoji graphics as well. With new graphics and some polish, this could be a really fun game.

comments.author: Dann Sullivan
comments.text: Oh, this was incredibly satisfying once I’d gotten my head around how it plays. Quite a clever game that strafes close to worker-placement games in its own way.

api.name: Unloved 13
api.description:
In Numberland, everybody is afraid of you - number 13 - because you are supposed to bring bad luck…

Will you be able to prove them wrong and make friends ?

CONTROLS

  • LEFT : A/Q/Left arrow
  • RIGHT: D/Left arrow
  • JUMP: W/Z/Up arrow

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Lovely idea, cute graphics, story and music

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Hey nice wee platformer. I did feel the platform navigation a bit unforgiving.

Nice clean graphics and nice musical score.

Puzzle-wise pretty solid, not too much thinking required which is good for me at least :)

Well done!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Unplayable due to the fact that I have a 120hz monitor and you only coded the game for 60hz monitors. Game is way too fast and I can’t jump high enough to even get past the beginning.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Hi Rob, thank you very much for your feedback! I tried to be hardware independant by limitting the FPS to 60. I obviously have missed something, I will try to improve this next year :)

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice work getting this side scroller working! the jumping is challenging and adds a nice difficulty to the game! Great work!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Fun interpretation of the theme. Short story, but coherent. Loved the ending. The controls are in general good, but would love to have some Coyote effect, or making it easier to land on the platforms.

The bass in the music packs a punch, and sounds nice with my headset. I also enjoyed the lovely animations, the visual effects and sound effects.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun little platformer. Very cute graphics, and loved the walk animation.

I liked the concept of the game, and the little puzzles to solve in each level.

The music was good too.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Now I have real friends! Clever interpretation of the theme, sharp graphics, and nice slow jams for music. The platforming mechanics and level design were pretty well done. A couple small tweaks would take you from “A” to “A+” such as coyote time and resetting the jump key on jump. The difficulty level was pretty well balanced, so never felt lost or frustrated. Solid entry, great job!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
I liked the game, cute idea, simple and fun. I thought there was going to be some math involved :D

The jump could have been a little smoother, was a little too quick and some times difficult to get onto platforms due to this.

Other than that all worked well!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: Very relaxing music combined with lightning fast physics made everything feel smooth! great job on the polish; I loved the little extras like how the rocks fell down at the beginning of one level, for example. One thing that caused some frustration was I tended to always jump twice by accident (when I landed! I would hold down the up button the entire jump in a vain attempt to jump higher, then accidentally doublebounce right when I land, almost every single jump lol). Regardless, this was great; I appreciated the various unique mechanics or movements you coded for each new friend - stuff like implementing a cupcake or a spider all add up to fun interesting extra details and that make it special. loved it!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Very cozy music and gameplay! Somehow the player character reminded me of on of these birthday-cake-candles :-)

I like the various mechanics you buildt in (jump-pads, switches, movable elements, …)

I retried the game on my office laptop (60Hz Display), as it was far to fast on my gaming laptop (144 Hz Display). Maybe you forgot to calculate the time-delta between updates and USE it for all updates. (I found, that you calculate _elapsedTick but only use it to calc the FPS?)

Nevertheless: it’s very good game!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: fun puzzle game and the animation of numbers is very creative and good looking, enjoyable puzzles and storyline

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
I love puzzle games like this.

The art is very simple, the music as well, but it just works for me so well. Good job on the storyline as well, it sounds stupid but it’s perfect :)

Good job on this!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Short but sweet game. Will we ever find out why the others feared 13?

The music is lovely.

api.name: 13 Curses
api.description:
13 Curses is a card game where the first player to have exactly 13 curses loses.

Playing

A game of 13 Curses starts with each player drawing three cards into their hand.

Three cards are added to the shop at the start of each round. Any cards left in the the shop from the previous round are removed first.

Players take it in turns to act. The player with the most curses goes first each turn. A coin flip decides the order if each player has the same number of curses.

A dice is rolled by the player at the start of their turn. The result of the roll is added to the player’s points. The points gained are multiplied depending on how many curses the player has:

  • 7 - 9 Curses: 2x points.
  • 10 - 12 Curses: 3x points.
  • 14 - 16 Curses: 4x points.

The player can use these points to buy cards from the shop. Purchased cards are added to the player’s hand.
The player can play any cards from their hand. Cards that would result in a negative number of curses, or curses over 16 for either player, may not be played.

The player can choose to end their turn without buying of playing any cards. The next player then has their turn. Once each player has had a turn, the round ends and another round starts.

Once any player reaches exactly 13 curses, the game is over.

Controls

13 Curses uses on-screen buttons for all interactions. It can be played with a mouse or using a touchscreen.

There are some additional gesture controls available when using a touchscreen:

  • Play the selected card - Swipe Up.
  • Return the selected card to hand - Swipe Down.
  • Buy the selected card - Swipe Down.
  • Return the selected card to the shop - Swipe Up.

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: interesting card game, the curse mechanic is intriguing and add lot of depth to the game

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Fun game! Loved the presentation, easy to understand and play.

Maybe my only concern is that after a while the game starts being repetitive, it needs something to be more exponential so it keeps getting faster.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very well done card game and concept. It works and is under 13k, great work!

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Love your GitHub README file ❤️.

Sound effects are great! Controls are smooth. Took a couple of goes to get it, but rather intuitive once you get going.
