Is a game that could go for a VERY long time - lots of back and forth, need a faster way to end the game and have more rounds. Get stuck in a loop once past x2 etc. There’s no indication whether the person with the most points at the end wins or not. I got stuck in a loop and it steam rolls for one player or another.

The points system, as well as curses system is rather unique. Very interesting game, but can get frustrated with the never ending cycle. It’s also interesting that what you’d consider a bad card, can be used for good. There’s no good or bad.

Wasn’t clear what the skull symbol was for, but makes sense it means death, and you lose. Still not clear how to win the game - is it the most points once someone reaches 13? That would be too one sided. Great concept though and well executed.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • Great work on:
    • The UI. The cards interaction and all the work in making everything hold together can’t be go unnoticed here!
    • The AI. It beat me once while I was playing wrong and then it gave me a not so easy time.
    • Game design, is it based on some known game? It feels really complete and some strategies are sparking here.
  • I liked that you:
    • Added many layers of interactivity like the confirmation dialogs or the dice and coin flip at the beginning. It adds a bunch to the game.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • If you check my screenshots timeline https://x.com/javascripl/status/1838315151931351458 you will notice that it took me a while to grasp the curses thing. They look harmless and they were even giving me more points for having them! Heck, the AI gave me all their curses hahaha. I think with very little you could make this understandable at sight, you did an awesome job in general so I’m sure you can figure this one out!
    • Not playing down the AI work here, but I think against a human this game would be much tighter. It’s calm, calm, and then in one turn you kill the opponent. I might be trying this with friends.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Well done. The “who goes first” system is great for keeping the game balanced. The 2x, 3x, 4x multipliers also add an interesting strategic option. The AI was relatively straightforward (understandably). It would be fun to play this human-vs-human, and to see people flirt with danger in the 3x and 4x zone. Great job!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Wow! I loved your game! Excellent idea for the theme, point and curse management is really fun!

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
Won on the second try. Guess I should have read the instructions first but hey, it didn’t take too long to get a grasp of what was going on. It was quite tedious and repetitive before I figured out that I should just rush to at least 2x and completely dominate the picks, slowly amassing a hand that and deliver a 1-hit-ko. Trying to just slowly build the opponent curses pretty much ends up in a stale mate where neither is doing anything.

Every other mechanic was quite clear after playing around a bit but the 2x/3x/4x thing could have been clearer. Didn’t quite pay enough attention to enemy cash increases on my first try to notice that the roll value was getting multiplied each time before it kinda got out of hand. And my going to 4x gambit failed as the opponent was sitting on a perfect card to minus me back to skull.

It looked and sounded pretty nice. Obviously some music would have been welcome and the text on cards was fairly small to read on a laptop screen. I’m guessing it’s unreadable on mobile.

The game pace could be increased quite a bit in my opinion. The 13 curses seemed quite a lot and resulted in a lot of boring nothing happening 50% of the game. Even if you like to keep the thematic 13 goal, you could increase the amount of curses each card added to pick up the pace. And not having access to +/- 1 would make it more interesting in the risk vs reward department too.

The add curse to yourself cards weren’t really worth it. They could be a lot cheaper in my opinion. And the +money ones were kinda bland. There was no reason not to get and use one if you could afford it. At least the minus ones needed some timing to get the full potential out of them.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun card game.

Good graphics and animations.

I’m glad the game pretty much played itself.

Some of the interactions were a little complicated. But there was lots of variety in the curses. So no two games are the same.

Overall a fun game

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Great idea, excellent implementation, easy to understand, hard to master.

The computer strategy could be improved a bit though, while playing first it left me with a winning move using a card from the shop that it could have bought.

An extra request, can the text be readable even when the card is minimized ?

Otherwise, great game, and as other reviewers mentioned, it could easily be turned into a tabletop card game to play with friends.

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
An impressive card game that’s nicely polished and integrates the theme well. With new graphics etc. I could see this turning into a full game!

Especially enjoyed the programmatic visuals and animations. Felt very retro. Nice work!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Very nice game!! I enjoyed playing it, I almost played for like 30 mins. First time the Computer beat me, later on I got the hang of it and I won!!!

api.name: Dungeon Of Curse
api.description:
Dungeon of Curse is a top-down dungeon crawler where precision and speed are key to survival. Players must navigate cursed dungeons filled with treacherous enemies, engaging in melee-based combat. The twist? You only have 13 seconds to conquer each room, forcing quick decisions and sharp reflexes. Fail to escape the room in time, and face the wrath of the curse. Will you outsmart the dungeon and break the curse, or will time run out?

Here’s a Feature list tailored for Dungeon of Curse, aligning with its core mechanics and theme:

1. Time-Limited Levels (Core Mechanic)

  • 13-Second Challenge: Each room must be completed in 13 seconds. Players must defeat enemies and find the exit within this strict time limit.
  • Countdown Pressure: A visible timer constantly ticks down, building tension and forcing quick decisions.

2. Melee-Based Combat And Dash

  • Close-Quarters Combat: The player primarily engages in melee combat with enemies.
  • Quick Escape: Player can use Dash ability to avoid enemies. It will also help player to get the door before time run out.
  • Dash take some time to refresh, so keep an eye on the player HUD.

3. Dungeon Layout and Enemies

  • Room Variants: Player can explore different types of rooms - combat area and hidden room.
  • In the dungeon player will engages with different enemies.

Note: For a better experience. Grab your headphones and adjust the volume as needed.

Controls:

Keyboard:

  • Movement: W, A, S, D, or Arrow Keys
  • Attack: J
  • Dash: Space
  • Start Game: Enter
  • Return to Main Menu (In-Game): Esc

Controller:

  • Movement: Default joystick
  • Attack: A
  • Dash: B
  • Start Game: Start
  • Return to Main Menu (In-Game): Back

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Lovely pixel art! I seem to have hit a bug, tried a few times but after collecting the key I cannot face left to attack so die on my way out of the tunnel.

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. This was a known bug, but I missed that at the last moment of submission :). This happens because the previously pressed button was Up or Down when the player tries to change the direction. Unfortunately, now I can not put the fix for that… Again thanks for playing.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: The controls aren’t good, specially when it’s a kind fast movements you must approach, but the game has nice pixel graphics and its state of levels is not difficult to understand

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for playing and your feedback.

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: I had a little trouble playing with a gamepad – If I turn around too fast, the hero doesn’t change direction. But otherwise everything works great.

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you so much for taking the time to play and provide your valuable feedback.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Amazing graphics, good music and concept

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for your time and appreciation.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Hey, love the art style, and I think the gameplay idea is really cool. Appreciate the controller support, as I think a game like this is best played with a controller.

The one thing that stopped this from being amazing to me though is that the player can only really attack to the left or right, where enemies can attack from above and below. This made it feel kind of unfair, and like I didn’t have enough control over my character. I think a control scheme more like zelda where you can face (and attack) up, down, left, and right would make this a great game!

Still, I played through to the end and enjoyed it, nice work!

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for playing the game through the end and for your feedback. :)

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text: A really good realisation for this game. I like a lot the ambiance.

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for playing and liking it…

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text: Great little game. The fonts did not render correctly for me which made it a bit less great. Only attacking left and right was a bit odd, you must set yourself up so the enemies are on the sides. I wish it could be attacking in all directions (N,S,E,W).

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thanks for playing and I appreciate the feedback. Fitting those graphics in a small size was a big challenge, so I had to cut out many things. For the player’s attack, I balance it by adding a large hitbox so it doesn’t feel unfair.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice feel and style, I like the controls and the skeletons were work really well. The animation is fantastic, great work!

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thanks a lot for playing my game, and I’m glad you enjoyed it! :)

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: The graphics are lovely. The controls are a little bit difficult with only being able to attack left and right, while monsters can hurt you from the top and bottom. It feels great to smash the skeletons, though.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not properly play the game. I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to attack an enemy, the page search shows instead. You should call .preventDefault() for all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Great pixel art, decent combat feel. I really wished the game was longer, I was having fun! I had attempted melee combat with my game but couldn’t make it feel right or fun to play, kudos to you for making it work. Fun game, my only complaint is that it was over too soon!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Nice little game. Good presentation. I was expecting more maps but the core idea is there.

My only complain is not being able to attack upwards or downwards.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
A neat game, good pixel art and sfx. I really like that title picture design as well.

For the controls, I would have liked an upwards and downwards strike - its a bit awkward always having to attack from the left or right. And maybe some tricky dungeon traps too. But good work all round!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Very nice pixel artstyle!

The attack-direction should be at least 4 directional, better in 8 directions. Left and right feels to restricted.

Edit: oh, you got that feedback from almost everyone :-) Well: thats a clear sign, what should be refined ;-)

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Good graphics, but very basic gameplay. Could use more complex combat.

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for the feedback. Simple combat gameplay aims to ensure that everyone can enjoy it, considering that most players will not spend more than 5-10 minutes on our Gamejam entry. Although I had many ideas around combat.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I escaped the cursed dungeon! Nice pixel art and sound effects. This was a pretty polished experience. Great job!

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Nicely done pixel art. Good sound design. Time limit on each room adds the thrill to the gameplay.

It would be nice to attack up and down.

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text: The blurred title screen kinda bugs me a litlle and the gameplay is fluid. I like the timer gets bigger when you run out of time. Good quality of life arrangement. Besides that it is a good and fun game. Barely need to use the dash mechanic.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The art of this game is amazing. I love how everything fits together and how fluid the animations, effects and controls are. The sound effects were also on point, although I wish there was music.

The game is sadly pretty short, which is understandable for the complexity and the size limit (although you still had 2 WHOLE BYTES)

The title screen seems to use fonts that I don’t have in my computer, so the title screen text looks huge, blurry and cropped. Same with the remaining time text - I can’t see how much time is left at all.

The choice of J for the attack was a bit awkward. I never saw a use for the dash button either, so the space would have been a better attack button.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Your game plays really well and looks great.

I like the fighting sound effects.

The enemies were brutal and got hard quite quickly.

The timer was a little short at times. The graphics didn’t always fit on the screen, particularly the clock, I couldn’t see that at all except for about 2 pixels on the left of the number - which made things tricky.

PIxel graphics were cool, and the levels looked amazing.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Super cool game, I wish the dungeons were procedurally generated, other than that - very nice :)

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The graphics and sound effects are nice. After several tries, I was not able to figure out how to clear the first room. However, I read the description, and understood that “attack” is “J”. I was trying “space” and clicking on the screen, but nothing seemed to work.

The 13 second time limit makes the game really difficult, but maybe that’s the intention?

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Cool action game, has smart gameplay, and matches the theme really well. The 13-second challenge definitely makes the game harder, but also more exciting! It would be nice to show the controls in the game since J for attack is probably not player’s first instinct.

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback and for playing my game. I will definitely incorporate this in my next jam.

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I like the use of the theme, though it is simple. The rooms felt do-able and the attacking wasn’t super easy. Well done!

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thank you for playing the game and for your feedback. I’m glad you liked it.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: Nice dungeon crawler. It’s fairly short, but I can tell a lot of effort was made to make the game look and feel great. A background music would really improve the immersion, though. The countdown didn’t really have time to put pressure on me. I guess the game design could be improved here, or more levels maybe? Overall, a good entry for this competition. Thanks for submitting!

comments.author: Himanshu Bisht
comments.text: Thanks so much for playing the game and sharing your feedback. It means a lot.

api.name: Z0MB13
api.description:
Z0MB13 is an infinite horde shooter game where you face increasing waves of zombies. Defeat unique zombie species to collect currency and upgrade your weapons in the shop.

Controls:

  • Mouse cursor: Aim
  • Mouse 1: Shoot
  • WASD/Arrow keys: Move
  • Touch: Left joystick for movement, right for aiming

Decentralized challenges: Arcadia, OP Guild.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Some nice attention to detail here, but its’ a bit hard. Would be nice if you could keep your money between rounds, or at least some of it. Gave up after a couple tries, but I think some difficulty tweaking and some better graphics and this would be a really fun one. Love the blood particle effects.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice game, the kiting is done well! I could not figure out why or how I was beating them with the stick, but it worked. Great job!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: One of the games that hooked me in. Had to keep playing this. Wanted to get further but i was soon overwhelmed. There might be a bug in there as once killed and restart i cant get anything from the shop anymore.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Nice job, there’s a lot of fun stuff going on here. On my first playthrough, I completely ignored the store, and got to 400 points with just the stick. The pistol and SMG were great too. I particularly enjoyed trying to get chain reactions of exploding blue guys. Well done!

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
It was super fun to play. I was not prepared that one of them exploded. And it ended my first run. After a while I get used to the controls, and it was not that hard until a certain point when it is just impossible to avoid the horde.

Nice particles effects and cute zombie hand animations.

I wish I could start a new run with the last weapon I died with. Great entry!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: It was a bit too hard for me to remain engaged. The initial weapon is so slow and hard to use that I wasn’t able to every buy anything from the shop. I’m sure the game gets more fun with better weapons, but I couldn’t get to that part.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An interesting concept for a game.

Swiping sound was good. The graphics were a little basic but looked like a top down vibe.

I didn’t get as far as the shop because the stick was unpredictable. I had to be close enough to cause damage but in a way that I wouldn’t be hit by the zombies first.

To be honest it felt more like a white blood cell attacking an invading virus or something.

But certainly a better weapon would have helped. I played on desktop first time around but this time I’m on mobile and I think desktop controls were easier.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Nice and fun entry!

I did not realize at first, and actually had to read the comments, that you use a stick and swing it at first. I saw shoot in the controls and just thought my game was messed up and reloaded multiple times etc haha

Once I figured that out it felt a lot like Hotline Miami to me, which is a good thing IMO. Had fun taking on enemies via melee and then upgrading weapons once I got money.

As others have mentioned I think there is a bug after you respawn where the store does not let you buy anymore.

Great implementation and I will probably play it more just to see if I can get a higher score. Would be awesome if there was a score leaderboard!

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The concept of Z0MB13 seems intriguing with its minimalist design. The map’s abstract pattern creates a distinct visual style, which could serve as a good foundation for a unique gameplay experience. The use of different colors for the player and elements like the shop brings a necessary contrast that helps guide the player’s focus. The “Score” and “Currency” metrics displayed prominently suggest a system of progression, which is motivating for players. The background texture, although visually striking, can be somewhat disorienting. The flowing organic pattern might make it harder to distinguish between important game elements, especially during fast-paced gameplay. Simplifying or adding more clear visual cues could help clarify the layout and reduce confusion. Additionally, introducing a bit more context to the game, such as a storyline or clearer objectives, might improve player engagement. Overall, Z0MB13 has the potential to stand out with its abstract aesthetic. With some adjustments to clarity and gameplay mechanics, this could become a captivating and engaging experience for players who appreciate more experimental designs. Keep refining the visual contrasts and gameplay flow to maximize player enjoyment!

api.name: Miles to Mend
api.description:
In the dimming embrace of twilight, you wander through the shadowed woods, a young apothecary driven by a quiet desperation. The light fades faster than your hopes, casting long, mournful shadows over the earth. The leaves whisper secrets of the night as you search, your hands trembling as they sift through the undergrowth, seeking the elusive plants and fungi that may hold the cure. Time is slipping away, as relentless as the fever burning within your small, distant home. You know the night is closing in, and with it, the chance to save a life.

Miles to mend is a simple puzzle game where the goal is to find all mushrooms and flowers correctly over 10 levels without getting 13 incorrect answers.

  • Collect your ingredients by selecting the ingredient tab (mushroom or flower) and clicking an empty square on the board.
  • The numbers along the borders show how many mushrooms and flowers to include in each row and column. Bottom and right show how many mushrooms. Top and left show how many flowers.
  • Flowers need sun: they must not be placed next to trees.
  • Mushrooms need shade: they must be placed next to trees.
  • To remove an ingredient from the board, click on it.
  • When you’ve finished collecting, click a border number to score that row or column.
  • When all spaces on the grid are scored, if you have fewer than 13 incorrect placements you progress to the next level.
  • Beat 10 levels to win!

Works on mobile but is not optimized for mobile.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Oh, a nice twist on the original picross game: 2 ingredients, obstacles… that makes a good puzzler

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Nice idea, I think with some polishing could be more engaging. The rules of the flowers and the mushrooms would be nice to have at the screen at all times.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice game, well done. I like the idea of solving the complex puzzle. I did it in a different way that still worked and I got it wrong. So I think this game has a lot of potential for a nice style of game. I liked the flower and mushroom, so it makes you think later on. Great work!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Reminds me of the cross axis games with border counts type nonogram idea where you end up making a picture by shading in squares.

Fun twist to see that both type shared the same board

More difficult when the grid size increases.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text:
I like this type of puzzle on paper, so it is interesting to see that it works well as a browser game, too.

Just one thing: It would be nice if I didn’t have to click on all border numbers to end a level, just one button for all would suit me better.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I completed with 1 miss and final score 422. There’s a very satisfying logic and algorithmic clarity to the game. Nice job!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text:
Was confused about how to “submit” the solution. Was looking for a button to submit the fully solved level. On the other hand submitting it line by line was more satisfying.

Maybe a better approach for UI could be to do calculations for player, green-highlighting border numbers when you have the correct number of items checked on the line (not necessarily in the right spot). And indicate when you place items breaking the rules, along with the message explaining the reason. Then it would be easy for player to figure out the rules by playing, without rereading the text rules.

Levels are a but too easy because of the mushroom spam. With verey few exceptions, muchrooms where simply on the every cell where muchrooms could be. Flowers were a bit trickier, but there were never that much of them to make things difficult.

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
Nice variation of the classic Tents and Trees logic puzzle :-)

Graphics are really nice, though I would personally prefer the trees/flowers/mushrooms not to be pixelated.

Controls could be improved by allowing to mark a field as empty (if that’s possible I at least didn’t figure out how)

I enjoyed playing it :-)

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Interesting puzzle, I had fun to play :) Maybe a chill music behind would make the game even better. Well done!

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text: A puzzle game about mushrooms and flowers. Personally I prefer the gameplay to be intuitive instead of requiring reading many rules about the game. But it works great and is easy to play. Nice job!

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
The principle is already known on paper puzzles, but the extra rules bring an additional dimension to the game.

Controls are ok, yet it is not entirely clear which one of flowers or mushrooms are currently selected, and I don’t see the point of having to validate manually rows and columns.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

Great work on having a complete working game for js13k!

At first, I saw long rules, tried to quickly parse it, started game “I will figure as I will go”… and then restarted game to read rules. :P After I got it thought, it was quite straightforward, so I guess there’s just an initial barrier, not necessarily hard to play.

I found it challenging to fill up the flowers, but also had some contradictions while I placed a flower in a place it was proper, but the game marked it as wrong. The mushrooms were quite straightforward and I didn’t need to think much about it, I saw trees and added mushrooms.

The improvement I see is to improve the completion of the levels. At some point I had a missclick and closed one of the columns with one mistake, but also I hadn’t put any of the mushrooms required in the rows. I was left with many wrong rows on the mushrooms because of that, but I could still finish without having any more errors by picking the columns. Maybe it was intentional that I could choose which strategy to follow (rows or columns), but it didn’t feel like it.

api.name: Courage
api.description:
A game for JS13k 2024 by Nicklas Löf. Follow me on Mastodon

Story

Help Billy to overcome his 13 different phobias by playing 9 different chapters in a simple 2D dungeon crawler with arcade style battles.

Controls

Play with mouse and keyboard. Using a trackpad is probably going to be very difficult

WASD or Arrow keys to move around

Mouse to aim

Left mouse button to fire

Right mouse button or Space to glide on the floor to avoid taking damage

E to throw bombs

Lights

Please notice that the game is quite dark in some areas so it’s recommended to not play in broad daylight or in a very lit room.

Difficulty

There are three different difficulty levels. Easy, Normal and Hard. The difference is a balance between more enemies with more health, bombs with lesser range and faster boss mechanics. In hard difficulty you will get more bombs because you really need them.

Mini map

Below the courage meter there is a mini map of the current level. Green is the exit. Blue is the room you are in currently. Plan your journey trough the level by going in the opposite direction of the exit so you don’t have to run back and forth if you are reaching the exit too early.

Save function and random generated rooms

If you quit the game and return later it will remember the chapter you last played and continue from there but all levels are random generated so the level layout and also some room layouts will be different from every play.

Acknowledgements

Felipe Alfonso for tiny-canvas

Frank Force for ZzFX

Kang Seonghoon for Roadroller

Andrzej Mazur for organizing js13k every year! :)

comments.author: Mezriss
comments.text: Nicely executed, but just too easy - both weapons feel overpowered and enemies don’t really present danger, in part because of how they are placed.

comments.author: Nicklas Löf
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback :)

A beta tester said it was too difficult :D

That’s why I added the difficulty selection so it can be played on Easy, Normal and Hard.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Nice little arena shooter. I liked how you had to collect resource to proceed to the next level.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: I really liked this one. I like the art style a lot and the concept was good. I felt the collision detection was a little off. If I have to dodge a lot of things, I think you should make the hitbox really small, like a couple pixels in the center. Still really enjoyed the game though. Got really far but then couldn’t build enough courage to progress.

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text:
I like the phobias idea and to beat them with a shooter is not bad, but the main gun its just too overpowered, makes the game rather easy. There is no downside to just leave it firing continuously, and the fire rate is so high that all you have to do is walk.

I don’t particularly like maps where you find the exit, but can’t take it yet because (in your case, there is not enough courage built up) … makes it so you have to walk back to the start and then return in now empty rooms. This is a personal opinion, and it is quite ok to have them this way if it is by design.

On the bright side … the bomb is 100% amazing! the fact that the enemies are rather dumb and come together only makes the bomb more awesome than it already is. There is a bug where if you throw across walls, it just flies way away.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Absolutely fantastic game, well done! I enjoy the style and how it feels. The bombs worked and dashing was a nice touch. Great work with only 13k!

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: I like the theme of overcoming fears. There needed to be a reload time or some pause in the shooting. It did not feel dangerous with a constant stream of bullets protecting you. The physics on the bombs was a little funny sometimes. They would go in the wrong direction from time to time. I like the dynamic lighting.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Played for quite a while, lots of content in your game! I ran through on easy but should have tested hard mode. Lots more to shoot on hard!

All really well made.

The lighting is very well done, lots of good pixel art. I like the mini map, changing enemies and tie in with the theme.

If I was being picky, Id have said to zoom in the view a little, have have upgrades on the weapon and let you plough through the enemies quicker, might be more satisfying. Corridors also allowed me to cheat a little and hide from enemies, or maybe this is a valid tactic.

Great entry and lots packed into the 13kb!

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: I could not properly play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so sometimes (especially when throwing a bomb) the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Ryan Malm
comments.text: Excellent pixel art, love the pixel overlay effect, reminds me of Deepknight’s jam entries! Excellent dungeon crawler, I enjoyed it.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
The gameplay is good but kind of the same. I wanted more difficulty and combat in there.

Graphics were awesome. The lighting effects really added a nice touch.

Controls were intuitive and easy to use.

Great job.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Nice job. Lovely pixel art and lighting effects. A couple of times I think I got soft locked in a level, and couldn’t find any more enemies to kill, and then didn’t have enough courage to exit. Or maybe I was doing something wrong? Either way, this is an impressive amount of art and content. Great job!

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text: Good graphics, satisfying gameplay. I like the way the attacks feel, shooting feels effective and those bombs are a lot of fun to lob into a room. Well polished and balanced overall!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: NIce sound effects. Would be nice to able to move small movements to corridors. Very nice graphic effects and crt effect overral.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A really fun and slick game, I played this for ages.

The graphics were great, love the pixel sprites.

The skulls were everywhere so I’m glad they weren’t enemies.

The boss clown was a bit evil, spawning those mini clowns, and what was that red stuff they were shooting?

I’m glad the shots were infinite, as I just kept on holding down the mouse and whizzing around.

The levels were well made and challenging, and the lighting worked quite well to create a phobia type environment.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Wow! You managed to pack a lot of things in your game. The lighting is awesome and it feels good playing. I enjoyed my time quite so much that I managed to reach the end.

If I had to give some feedback, it would be using the actual -phobia names for each fear (arachnophobia, triskaidekaphobia, etc etc). But in general I think the idea of beating your fears and link that with your health (to “build up courage”) is awesome. Good job! :)

comments.author: Ben Gillbanks
comments.text:
I like it. A classic dungeon crawler but with phobias! :)

I found the controls a bit challenging but I use a trackpad rather than a mouse so that’s a me issue really.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text:
Love the lighting & CRT - looks beautiful!

Fun game, too! Nice job.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Nice gameplay with really neat pixelart. The CRT-effect adds a lot to the atmosphere of the game.

I like the autofire (so i dont have to kill my mousebutton by slamming it dozens of times per second)

Good job!

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: Nice dungeon crawler. I really like the art and style. I would have loved a creepy background music for this one. Controls were good, even if I played on a trackpad. And thank you for adding a minimap! Overall, a great little game.

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I think that killing things you are afraid of might not help with fears, but I do like the story and connection with the theme. The audio and graphics are really well polished as well.

api.name: Maze Runner vs 13
api.description:
You are trapped in a maze,

And the walking 13s have your scent, you need to run for your life.

Find all the cookies in the maze to open way to the next level,

After collecting all cookies the next stage portal will spawn somewhere in maze.

You can collect hearts to increase your health and have more survival chances.

Reaching higher levels will increase cookie cound and maze size.

Mobile have two option of control, click and drag or use the control

Pc controls are A W S D for movement

comments.text: Great game and concept! I loved the artwork and maze style game play. Great work getting this game up and running in under 13k!

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead

You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this. Also, the maze is a bit larger than my screen, you should scale it down to fit the available space.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Travelling salesman, the game 😀 This was fun. Simple premise, but pretty well balanced, at least for the few levels. Level 6 was tough, because you basically need to focus 100% on getting more time. Nice pixel art and music, nice job!

Fun game. I like that characters have some animation when moving.

Found two issues so far:

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke

I didn’t see a portal to the next level, but I did get a trophy appear.

A little tricky to play on mobile, but still possible.

Enemy pathfinding seems to work really well! I had a hard time figuring that out on one of my own projects, so I’m impressed!

Nice touch that on larger maps, the map screen scrolls up/down.

One nitpick: the top menu bar could use some more styling, I think. And/or it could benefit from being more connected with the game screen.

comments.author: Brian Hambley

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Pretty nice take on a Pacman style game with cookies haha After a while the cookies started looking like pizzas to me!

Nice controls and pretty fun gameplay. Would have been cool if there were powerups also, like extra speed or ability to destroy enemies (ala Pacman).

I like the simple control scheme too. I’d probably have even more fun with my gamepad but I don’t have it with me.

Nice job!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Cool music, enemies pathfinding is well managed too. A simple maze game with cookies. With keyboards it’s fine, but while using touch events on mobile it did not take the correct direction many times

api.name: King Thirteen
api.description:
King Godric XIII, called the Crimson Hand, the Withering Flame, the dreaded King Thirteen rose from his throne.

The whispers of his subjects had grown too loud, too bold. They had forgotten their place.

— Silence!

Clad in blood-red armor, Godric towered over them, an enormous and imposing figure.

— I am fear incarnate! — his roar echoed through the hall. — You will bow before me, or you will burn!

Soon after, swords were drawn, and scarlet splattered the floor.

How to play:

  • Join forces
  • Claim the throne
  • Revel in glory

Decentralized: Arcadia Challenge, OP Guild Challenge.

If there is no sound, try allowing autoplay in your browser settings.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: First game I scored 69, all worked really well, neat graphics, sounds and game play. I will need to play again to see if I can better my score. Reminded me of the 1048 type games that are quite addictive once you start!

comments.author: galika
comments.text: Creative and fun gameplay—keeps you hooked!

comments.author: noam2223
comments.text: like the take on the classical twist of 1024 with chess moves and the added king that kills you adds thinking element to the game. will play this even outside of the context of this competition

comments.author: Aleksander Szczepura

comments.author: Steven Lambert

I didn’t understand how to play the first few times. I didn’t realize the knight and the bishop could combine if they shared the same number. Also don’t know why the King captured one of my pieces when normally he was standing still or moving around.

Other than not understanding the rules completely, the game is pretty interesting. I’m not very good at advanced chess move thinking, so it was difficult for me to score well since I failed to line up the knights to upgrade them. Can easily see how people would love the game.

comments.author: Cliff Earl

comments.author: skyatmoon

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This was pretty fun. I was confused at first, and there are no instructions, but i eventually understood the point of the game. It’s a 2048 meets chess kind of game. Lovely music, and very polished art. The theme fit seems a bit of a stretch.

comments.author: Matt Light
comments.text: I am not very familiar with chess rules, but very quickly I recognized that this was chess meets 2048. The gameplay was fun and the music had some great emotional undertones.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: This was a fantastic game, it had me playing it for a while. I enjoyed the chess concept and movement. That made it more interesting as to what pieces you wanted for future development. Great work and really fun game!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: What a combination of games! Garnished with the clean and moody music. A very good game, even my son (7 years old) had fun with it!

comments.author: Sebastian Dorn

comments.author: Javi Agenjo

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thanks, I realize this now! In my head, the concept was that you discover the different interactions as you go, like in puzzle games.

You’re completely right though, in the full release I’ll add a training level or something. Thanks!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text:
I like both 1024 and chess, very nice game and a good idea, king kill peices is not always working and its movement is kind of unpredictible so cannot really make a strategy and alot of randomness will rely on luck instead of figuring out a strategy for winning and getting better position. should add button to mute the sound with music I think. also add some hints in game about how it is played.

came back after second match, looks like the 1024 killes the king and win the game. wanted to go for more but I think I would not have lasted much after that lol. nice nice btw my score is 5467 let me know if someone beat it.

edit 2 : also seems that you can make a strategy to win, especially after the queens get spawning.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Really fun to play, the music jazzy, and a tune that I’ve heard in previous JS13k entries.

I like the way you can multiply upwards, when there’s a reachable onward step.

The graphics are well made and look great.I played for a while but wasn’t sure if there was a specific target or time limit? The numbers just kept getting bigger.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: I played this for much longer than I expected. There’s something truly mesmerizing about it. The audio and graphics are excellent, and it’s incredibly addictive. I just want to keep growing my chess pieces. The theme is also very prominent.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Very innovatie. Could use more explicit rules. Like, when the king can capture pieces?

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thanks! :)

King resorts to violence only when he is cornered and has nowhere else to go. It’s below him to kill commoners otherwise.

However if you might lose due to the board being full, then the King won’t help you by clearing the board; he’ll just stand there.

I see how this all can be a bit confusing but yeah, he’s nonlinear like that, an agent of chaos.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is really cool! My score is up to 838 :) I think the game saves, so I’ll try to see how much I can increase it.

Very cool how the new units (rooks, queens) gradually appear.

Lots of strategy, especially in the beginning, deciding how many bishops/knights you want to keep.

Not too much to improve? Sometimes when trying to “unselect” a selected unit, I would accidentally move it (that might be a “me” problem, not a UI problem… and you also have an undo button, so this is kind of a non-issue).

Very fun!

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text:
Reminded me 1024 game, but with the chess figures. Amazing idea and I was very delighted when discovered that you can combine knights and bishops together!

Good job and congratulations on submission!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Solid game without any compromises to fit in 13Kb.

Great tile graphics, music, gameplay, works well on mobile and desktop.

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
So far, this is the submission I spent the most time playing this year (except my own 😇). However, I almost missed this gem. Here’s my story:

The first time I launched it, I completely misunderstood how to play. The description has nice flavor text, but I doesn’t explain the gist of the gameplay!

  • I din’t occur to me that I can capture my own pieces to combine them.
  • I tried checkmating the king with my two knights, but it didn’t work.
  • I avoided putting my pieces next to the king, assuming the king would capture them.

I decided to come back after a few days and give it another go. Am I glad I did! It was after skimming through your post mortem that I noticed the mention of 2048 and had an a-ha! moment.

I managed to beat the game on my second attempt, scoring 5,332 points. I’m sure I made a lot useless moves.. Once I got to 1,024 I’m glad that the number turned red—it was enough to suggest that it was king slaying time :) Without this small indication I would have likely continued to 2048. Some other notes:

  • Bishops are the worst. My winning strategy was to hunt them before they become a problem. The light-square and the dark-square bishop are the only pieces in the game that are impossible to combine, which can be dangerous when space is limited.
  • It’s crazy how powerful queens are, just like real chess. A well-executed queen combo feels like one of those moves in checkers which clear the entire board.
  • Combos feel great, but sometimes I miss-clicked because I actually wanted to select the other piece rather than continue the combo. Thankfully, there’s Undo!
  • I was pretty happy with myself when I managed to surround the kind on purpose with low-rank pieces and force him to help me make some room on the board.
  • I’d like to see a move counter or a timer. The game is quite addicting and can be a time sink!

Overall, this is a a very polished experience, with great audio and very pretty design. I commend the attention to detail, like the lower-left square being dark. Awesome job!

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text: Nice! I think your learn-as-you-go concept works well, and I would probably not read or go through a lengthy tutorial anyway, so personally I enjoyed learning what makes the game tick step by step and without guidance. Love the little polish touches, like the king shaking about when you want to move him. Great music and sound, lovely clean graphics, it all fits a style. Very useful local storage save point too.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Success! I killed the king! Final score 5457, which is probably bad because it took me a while to figure out what was going on. On one hand, I feel like the readme could have been a little more forthcoming. On the other hand, I’m getting a sense of your personal style and I suspect that this was an intentional cheeky way of contributing to the experience 😉 This was great! Novel puzzle design, at it’s finest. Brilliant combination of 2048+chess. Excellent graphics, sound, and music to round out the experience. Just a fantastic job. Congrats!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thank you so much for the kind words Cody!

And yeah I failed at explaining myself this time harder than ever before 😂

Here’s hope the new video trailer thing helps to rectify this a little bit.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: incredibly polished and playable! wow, this is so fun. I just played for SOOOOOO long! I adore the graphics; simple yet very elegant, with nice shine and shadow - all the sprites are really nice to look at. the strategies, the slow way newer pieces appear on the battlefield, and the familiar mix of two cool things is a HIT!!! I felt a bit lost at times when suddenly something would happen seemingly randomly and I had no idea what it did. that’s on me for not quite getting the red stripes evolution stages or reasons a bishop appears exactly… could some sort of explanatory helper tooltips or mouse-hover info help me know for sure what would happen if I made a move? I’d love that. I suppose I should have used UNDO to preview moves, but that never occurred to me (feels almost like cheating). The only other thing I did not love was the sound - I wish it was all more soft and chill and zen. In any case, this game is phenomenal, the ideal touchscreen game you could play for a thousand hours. wonderful work.

comments.author: Tom Jackson
comments.text: Brilliant context, executed very well. It’s a bit unclear when the king captures, though. I’d like to know what the rules are.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text: This is so slick. I love the way that I didn’t really know what was going on until I did. That is one beautiful learning curve. Amazing work!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
At first I didn’t know what to do, but as I played, the game rules became more evident, though this is one of the only thing I think could be improved: the rules could be more explicit, especially how to win the game.

Another thing I would consider doing is to allow disabling the sound effects. I found how to disable music, but could mute effects.

The game is very interesting and grab your attention. Very nice work!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Excellent game! reall fun gameplay and original. I went up to a 1024 tile, don’t know if 2048 is a win but I can’t play longer if I want to test more games. Really addictive. Just 2 little points: explain somewhere than a piece can be taken by another piece of a different type. At the beginning I didn’t merge bishop with knight. And maybe disable a tile once taken, even if still playable, or maybe a better visual feedback to notice that tile is stile active. Multiple time I want to select another tile and it was eaten by previous one, which was not the move I wanted

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: You had me at ‘King Godric XIII’! Nice writing. I had a lot of fun playing this game. I ended up getting defeated because I got lazy, but I plan on giving it another shot once I play some other entries. The game feels finished, and has good music. Also, love the thumbnail :) Excellent work!!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Super cool spin of 2048! Not sure how it fits with the overall theme but it’s a very nice addicting game :)

comments.author: Logan Tempez
comments.text:
Oh wow, well done! Congratulation for the game. The visual, the feeling, the music, it’s a very high quality work! Very good mix between 2048 and chess game. I would have like a better link to the theme tho.

GG!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Thanks a lot for the kind words!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: Super polished, but honestly I didn’t get it. Seems like everyone but me knows what 2048 is? I get Chess, but I didn’t know what the point of the game was aside from being able to combine my pieces, so some instructions would have been great. As soon as the Music started I knew whose game it was - I love the tune and I think it fits the game well, but you could consider branching out.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Absolutely, I pledge to making some entirely new audio the next time I participate! Just had no willpower left this year, so reused the theme from the last gaem.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The clean and minimalistic design of the chess-like pieces is visually appealing and fits the strategic gameplay very well. The use of warm colors creates a cozy, vintage atmosphere, which works great for a game centered around a medieval king. The stylized chess pieces are distinct and immediately recognizable, making it easy for players to grasp the mechanics visually. While the overall design is effective, it might benefit from a bit more visual feedback during gameplay. Adding animations or subtle effects when merging pieces or achieving specific game goals would enhance the player’s sense of progression. The artwork is well-executed, and the chess theme is perfectly integrated into the game. The simple yet elegant design makes it easy to focus on the strategy aspect while maintaining a pleasant visual experience. With just a few additional effects, this game could truly stand out even more. Excellent job!

api.name: Tredici Malefici
api.description:
Tredici Malefici (13 evils) is a missile defense 🚀 javascript game where aliens 👽 have discovered your phobia of the number 13. To exploit your fear, they drop 13 bombs adorned with 13 spikes. Turn the tables and use this fear to your advantage.

  • Controls: Click/Tap
  • Tested on: Android phone/tablet (Chrome/Firefox) in portrait mode.
  • Download: The game is one monolithic index.html file with no external dependencies, so it is easy to download and run offline from your file manager in your browser.
  • 💃 Live
  • 🍔 YouTube
  • 🍕 Code
  • 🍨 Post-mortem
  • 🍩 Developer Notes

Note:

  • You may have to play the game a long time before encountering difficult speeds, bosses and voices 🐲

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Works well, nice sound FX on the explosions. I like the idea of limited ammo. but if you shoot too quickly its a long round to wait out.

Interesting enemy shapes, looks cool.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Nice. I think when you have depleted your ammo the enemies should speed up a lot so the round can end faster as there’s nothing to do but watch then roll down the screen slowly.

I love the little procgen tune at the start!
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I really like the limited shots! The accuracy that you need make this a very enjoyable game. Great work!

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
Nice and enjoyable missile defense game. At first I didn’t realize that the missiles explode exactly where I clicked—I thought the mouse cursor only set the direction and the missiles exploded on impact. Once I discovered this detail, the gameplay clicked for me, and so did the ammo limit. I would try to wait certain enemies out based on their trajectories and only launch a missile when I had a chance to take out a few of them in one blow.

I enjoyed the poetic intro screen, as well as the vector-like shapes of the aliens. For some reason, they made me think of the winter holidays :)

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Nice game, very fun and addictive! I heard great job!!

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Missile Command redone, would need a bit of game balancing. Missiles are scarce and enemies at the far side take a long time to reach – if your first shot fails, there is not enough time left for a second one. Reducing the screen actually makes the game easier !

Gfx are reminiscent of the Vectrex console, you may want to go for full retro look. Sfx get repetitive quickly, the beatbyte at the beginning is nicely done but could be improved with a longer melody. Nice use of speech API, how about announcing levels too ?

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Fun little game. It reminds me a bit of Math Blaster :) I enjoyed your space poem/music, but it made me want a game track! Solid entry.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Simple yet very well mastered!

As you noted, it’s too bad the difficulty doesn’t ramp up faster. You will lose players because of this :)

The fact you handled mobile is great.

I have to admit the shoot/explosion sounds didn’t really sound great to my hear haha.

Well done!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun little missile command type game

Easy to play. Cool little shapes, good sound effects

At first on mobile nothing happened until I pressed the screen. I thought it had crashed at first. Maybe it was waiting for user interaction to make the audiocontext. But if this was the case something on screen to indicate this would be good

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: This is a missile command variant at heart. 13 spikey stars will descend slowly down and you need to perfectly time the shot to take them out. Unlike the Atari original, when your missile explodes that’s it - there’s no expanding radius of explosion in which to capture the baddies, ideally capturing multiple ones at once. So in this game you have to be absolutely utterly perfect in your aim, otherwise you’re out of missiles within seconds and then have to sit and watch as the baddies slowly float to the bottom of the screen. I liked the intro message and music! It has a nice beat and pace to it, which seems to be lost when the game starts. It feels like some tweaks here and there would really have elevated this: maybe you don’t run out of missiles? Maybe a hit gets you some more? Or if you do run out, the remaining spikes should fall much much faster so the next level starts. Ideally, though, when the missile explodes it should expand so you can capture other enemies with a single shot. Strangely, the thumbnail for this game on js13k implies that may actually happen in later levels? But sadly I didn’t get past level 3 because I just wasn’t engaged enough. If the game does introduce that later, then I really wish it had done so from the start.

api.name: Kyabetsu
api.description:
Kyabetsu is an adventure game inspired by graphical adventures like Monkey Island

Controls

Click on the scene to move around.

Select an action on the bottom and click on objects in the scene to interact with them.

Actions

  • Look at 👀 look an object to inspect it
  • Talk to 💬 start a dialogue with someone
  • Pick up 🫳 try to pick up an object, if it works it will be placed in the inventory
  • Hit 👊 hit someone/something with force
  • Read 📖 read something, eg: a book, a paper
  • Use ⚙️ use an object in the scene
    • if an object in the inventory is selected, use that object with something else in the scene
    • note: you can unselect an object in the invetory by clicking it again

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Implemented the point and click very well. The labels on items and directions are a nice touch (I was a bit lost on the earlier version).

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: #playtest on the slack channel was helpful ^^ thanks for your feedback!

comments.author: ZZ
comments.text: 2nd game I finished! Only thing i would have liked to have seen is an X to escape text loops I get caught in when checking stuff. But that was a fun experience! Thanks

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: I thought to add that but skipped to save some space, I will add the skip dialogue in the second episode, thanks for your feedback!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Welcome to the 13k point&click club, this one is very cute.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Nice point’n’click adventure. Being able to skip through some of the dialogue would be great. Excellent little game.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very well done, nice story and inventory system. I enjoy these styles games and this is done well. Great work!

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: Nice chill feeling playing this and I appreciated the humor. “Why do you hate this duck?” Haha.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: SVG animation can go alot when right, well done.

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Lovely loading screen with animation and sound effect; great job. Nice work building narrative and story. Cute as graphics. In-game sound effects are great, add a little bit of music for more immersion. The squeaky sound effect gets old and annoying very quickly 😉.

Got stuck at the rock - but great game concept. Nice work, and love the idea, the character animations, and the storyline.

Love that you can use the mouse for the whole game; would be cool to have keyboard implementation too.

Nice GitHub README. Love how you talked about inspiration etc.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Very cute game. Simple and intuitive gameplay and controls.

Doesn’t really match the theme, but was really fun.

Great job.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I escaped to the moon! Super cute graphics, very wholesome writing. Several hilarious moments such as the spider scaring itself and Coso just jumping onto the moon. The puzzles were well balanced, I never felt stuck or annoyed (which great - most point and click games are way too frustrating). Impressive work, great job!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I did it ! The adventure was very interesting and the difficulty of the puzzles was perfect. I lso liked the little sounds of the character when they are speaking!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Really cool adventure. Many diaglos are adjusted to context, it’s really lovely Cool graphics and art, nice puzzles. Great game, even if not in the theme

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Very cute and approachable! I got stuck at the rock in the cave, but was fun exploring the area. Loved the graphics, nice job!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Nice point and click adventure! cool artstyle!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: What a great little point n click adventure game. I enjoyed it quite a bit. The sound effects are a bit annoying and I’d try to vary them a bit more.

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I quite liked the alien person and the little story. The game itself is simple but well made - I enjoyed it very much. It’s a fun puzzle game!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Such a fun little point and click game! A lot of different interactions, nice map and characters. Great job!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Ah, that was so cute. The story was pretty wacky, but felt cohesive. I was a bit lost at times, but was able to finish. The way you implemented the voices was adorable. Good use of the theme around superstition and the day 13.

I left like the buttons were a bit annoying to use. I would personally prefer if the actions would appear as a tooltip when clicking an object, instead of having to go down to the toolbar to change the action each time.

api.name: TriskaidekaPool
api.description: Game of pool with “side effects” If sum of values of two colliding balls is equal to 13, runes will appear on the table. Each rune has its own effect, making game… unpredictable Balls should be put in sockets in ascending order, otherwise zero points acquired for each ball not in order.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Interesting twist. Would be better if sign’s meaning would be visible during the game.

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Thanks. I tried to make the sign’s images meaningful :D

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
A different take on the pool game.

I really liked the pseudo 3d effects on the balls and the shadows, well done.

It would have been even better if the balls rotated as well :)
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Oh ye, that would be fabulous :D I had some thoughts on making them look like they roll, but did not implement , maybe next time >_o

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Enjoyed the game!

I feel I need more feedback, like to know when the ball is ready to kick or when I put a ball in a whole in the wrong order.

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: THanks for feedback. Ye, kinda agree, it is probably not clear when all balls are stopped and it is OK to hit the white ball

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very nice pool game with the 13 theme! The physics were very well done and it felt smooth. Great work!

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Thanks, the physics is very basic :D

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Love the look and play of this game, feels quite atmospheric.

Really hard to pot the balls in the specific order unless the break falls on your side. Especially when the symbols appear which serves only to make it harder.

I’ll have to play it again to see if I can beat the game.

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Thank you. Sometimes its very easy to play, and sometimes its almost impossible If this would be “real”, commercial game, of course this kind of situations should be avoided, user should have a way to win in any situation, but for friendly js competition its okay :D

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
Simple controls, nice graphics (I like the lighting and dynamic shadows) but most of all I really like the theme element with the runes (which do actually make you quite fearful of ball collisions!).

The runes can really mix up a game, and adds a lot to replayability as every game ends up with different runes in different places. My main criticism with this one is the time it takes for the balls to settle - you end up waiting a bit too long after each shot. But also perhaps thats just me having limited patience!

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Thanks! Well, I guess in “real” pool you would also have to wait :D

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I do like pool game and really liked this. Took a few games to understand whats going on but really playable game. Pockets are a bit tight perhaps and maybe I would add a bit more friction. Would love to borrrow some of the code If I may, to learn how to do a pool game.

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Thank you! I played real pool only once in my life, maybe this is why pockets are “a bit tight” :D Code for this game might be not the best, but it should be enough for simple pool

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: I’m so used to pool games letting you control the cue that I kept sending the white ball backwards :( The concept is neat, rune effects well varied. Quite hard to complete a game under these circumstances!

comments.author: mooookar
comments.text: Haha, at first I made it exactly like this - you should be “dragging” the line in opposite direction, same as in AngryBirds etc. But then I realized - if ball is near the table’s edge, it is not really visible to player, so I switched it around )

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk

comments.author: Björn Ritzl

comments.author: Noël Meudec

api.name: PicoBuddy
api.description:
PicoBuddy is a game/interactive story, in which you take care of a digital pet.

Instructions

  • Hover the purple buttons to see their function
  • Click the purple buttons to satisfy your PicoBuddy’s demands
  • Don’t worry if some things start acting strange or malfunctioning; everything will be fine after 13 days

Considerations

  • PicoBuddy takes about 10 minutes to play. There is no save function, so you have to play it all at once
  • PicoBuddy requires a lot of reading in English

Credits

Sound effects were created using ZzFX: https://github.com/KilledByAPixel/ZzFX

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Pretty neat little sim.

I think the UI could do with some polish, it seems a bit disjointed.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thanks for the comment!

Yeah, fair point! Your eyes have to jump around the screen a bit. I was hoping to add some CSS transitions (maybe?) to help make it more clear what’s important on screen, but… you know… I didn’t do that

But anyway, thanks for playing!

comments.author: Luke Nickerson
comments.text: That was quite the experience. I appreciate the dark humor and the writing, and the odd juxtaposition between pet sim and “the unbecoming”.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thank you for playing!

Yeah, this ended up being more of a writing exercise than I expected!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Ha! I was torn back and forth between smiling because of the narration and the stress of finding (!) the (correct) buttons :-) But i did well in the end (100% happiness) Also, there was a hint of triskadekaphobia, when finally the “proceed to day 13” was to be clicked… (“should i really?”) Nice game!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thank you for playing!!

Nice job with 100%, but… is that really what your PicoBuddy wanted..?

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
At first I thought this wont be a lot of fun, but when the story kicked in and the issues started happening; was really enjoyable.

Simple but fun issues made all the difference, the humour had me smiling too.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Yeah, the game starts a bit slow, I think; in the beginning, the narrator has to do a lot of the heavy lifting.

Thanks for sticking it out and getting to the good parts..!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Really nice once you give a chance to stop and play the game! My pet finished with 97% of happiness!

At first I thought it was too easy, but I can see a kid playing the game and feeling it more challenging. Maybe a level selection could make the game more difficult.

I didn’t feel too concerned about triskaidekaphobia. The plot is good! I have 13 days to raise the buddy, but as a father of 2 kids and 8 dogs, I would feel more pressured with some message like: will you hold down the fort until your pet reaches maturity and calms down after 13 days? (I hope this sentence makes sense, I’m not a native English speaker).

Really fun! Congrats on your work!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thank you! Nice job with 97% :D

Yes, I didn’t spend any time on managing difficulty, haha. You get 13 seconds to complete each demand, which is probably way too long/slow. (But I refused to change it to a different number in respect of the competition)

And yeah, this game would be especially easy for someone with 8 dogs! Woah!

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text: Wow, ok…. I think it’s the best one so far for me this year. I love the narrative, it reminds me Undertale a bit. I will try to have a second ending. Haven’t expected this type of game in the jam. Great work.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text: Hey, thank you for playing! Yep, you can use the meter in the middle to estimate which ending you get. Looking forward to your entry next year ;)

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text:
Wow, laugh out loud writing! This got surprisingly philosophical!

“In the end, your PicoBuddy had a happiness level of 100.0%!” - I bet you say that to everyone, ha.

“Your PicoBuddy sees this as a sign of weakness.” - 😮!!

This was a great, fun experience. Well done!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thank you for trying it out! Glad you enjoyed it!

Oh, you got 100%? Nice! There are two other endings if you get lower percentages.

Thanks again!

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Lols, it’s a digital version of an OG Tamagotchi!

Great storytelling and narrative. Fun sound effects, add some music for that extra bit of immersion. Love all the use of emojis as well.

It’d be cool if there was a penalty for clicking the wrong button (by day 12, I figured out this was the emoji counter on the side but didn’t really seem to make much difference), because at this stage, you can just spam all the buttons until you get the right one. Also not sure what the numbers next to the things meant, eg. 1. hydrate - 12 (what does the 12 mean or do, since this didn’t seem to have any real use/consequence).

At day 5, the UI flipped and was totally inverted, have no idea how it happened or how to fix it. Great fun game though. Day 6 went back to normal… but was in Japanese or Chinese? But by day 7, I understood what was happening… fun concept and really well executed.

The eye/s looked like a cute bee to me 😛.

After the screen fade at the end, if you had an ending slide that’d be really cool; maybe something like game credits or similar.

Nice GitHub README with graphics included.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thanks for playing, and for all of the notes!

Right, if you hit the wrong button, your buddy’s “happiness” goes down slightly. But it’s still really easy to have 100% happiness by the end.

The numbers next to the demands are how much time you have left to complete the demand. But you’re right, the numbers don’t really matter (unless it hits 0, which means you’ve “failed” the demand)

And yes, good point about the ending! It’s a little bit awkward with the black screen, haha.

Anyway, thanks again for playing!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Wow, this game rules. Amazing concept, the idea that evolving your tomogachi would create a being that ends the world, the way the narrator leads up to it, the whole thing is just wonderfully executed. The initial presentation and description make you think it’s…well…not going to be very fun. This is unfortunate in some ways as some people might not try it, but it adds to the element of surprise when things start unraveling. Also thought it was fun to get some extra surprise japanese practice, I’ve been learning for like 8 months now, I could read everything. But it takes longer to read japanese for me so I fell behind on my pets demands while reading it ha!

Anyway, wonderful game, just one thing I think that could make it better would be a bigger effect at the end. The game has so many fun effects throughout that I hoped for something more to represent the ending. Like skew and rotate the screen (even with just css transforms + animation), flash the pet at a larger size while everything gets sucked into a vortex. Something like that.

Amazing game though, great work.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Hey, thank you for playing! Glad you liked it!

Yeah, agreed about the ending! I was planning on adding some “glitch” text CSS in the last few days, and also do on the final conclusion screen, but… never did it, haha.

Thanks again for playing!

PS: Started playing your game, but decided I needed to play it at night with headphones on to get the true experience. So I’ll circle back and play it later!

comments.author: Adrien Guéret

I’ve finished at 100% of happiness, and the game told me it was the “decent” ending :o I would love to check the other endings, but it would be great to allow players to skip narrative after finishing the game at least one. Very solid entry, it’s so cool to be surprised like that when playing :D

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin

Agreed– it takes a long time to finish the game, so it’s probably unlikely to see the other endings.

If you’re curious, you can read the other ending text starting on line 962 on the source code, haha (please ignore all surrounding bad code)

comments.author: Lewis Nakao

Wow, that was fun! It’s a awesome unique experience with twists. It reminds me of Doki Doki Literature Club where things get weird and dark. In your game, it doesn’t turn into a psychological horror though, hehe.

I appreciate you creating this experience!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Oh, I can see what you mean with Doki Doki Literature Club, with a “cute” presentation but darker themes. (Man, that game gets dark…)

Thank you for playing!!

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
This is postmodernism in a 13KB zip. I love it.

A very well executed idea, I had a ton of fun playing it! It’s amazing how much character and humor you conveyed using a simple log of messages, a few emojis, and an array of well-designed glitches.

I appreciate the narrative twist at the end. Toys like tamagotchi or furby can, in fact, be thought of as being about building relationships in which the power is skewed towards the player who has the absolute control and the ability to turn the toy off at a whim. I don’t know if that’s the interpretation you were going for, but it does seem appropriate for the EyeGuy to seek retributi👁️n.

I got 100% which I guess means I tried too hard to deserve to live past the unbecoming. Oh well, at least I’ll make a lot of new friends! Btw. after I finished, I waited for a few more minutes at the black screen expecting a post-credits scene. The story-telling is too good to not have one!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Thank you for playing and for writing that great interpretation of the game!

About the ending: yes, I told myself that the ending was fine (because everything fades to “nothingness” in the story), but it’s a bit awkward from the player’s perspective. Adding something else after the fadeout would be an improvement for sure

PS: I am having a lot of fun with “Millennials”!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo

Innovation is definitely a 5/5! At first, I thought it was just a simple clicker game, but it turned out to have so many clever elements!

The game even includes some small puzzle aspects, which keeps me eager to find out what the next day’s challenge will be, haha. It reminds me of “WHAT THE GOLF?”. Great job!

api.name: The Society of Multiphobics
api.description:
In this puzzle game you have the role of an Event Planner for the dinner of the Society of Multiphobics.

Your task is to assign all the guests to chairs at the tables in such a way that no phobia is triggered.

Info & Controls

All levels are solvable.

All the required information is displayed within the game, but here are some backup infos just in case:

  • the guests are represented by emojis

  • the phobias of each guest are represented below the emoji (again emojis)

  • everyone suffers from triskaidekaphobia

  • select an emoji to get more information about the phobias

  • change the position by using drag and drop or clicking an empty chair

  • you can also use the empty fields as intermediate move

  • the game is won when all guests are seated correctly

  • note: some guests are represented by object emojis, treat them as persons nevertheless :)

About the score calculation

The score is based on the amount of moves.

You get the highest score (9999⭐️) if you solve the level in the least amount of moves possible (par).

When you need as much moves as the level has guests, you still get 2000⭐️. Then it slowly decreases to 0⭐️

Note: Calculating the optimal par was one of the hardest parts. The final solution is not perfect, but works for most cases.

Stats & Fun Facts

  • this is my 4th time participating
  • I worked ~90 hours on this game (longest time so far), but I had a lot of fun 😁
  • My husband supported me an extra ~10 hours (DevOps + the drag&drop feature)
  • 10% of the coding happened on a plane over the Atlantic ✈️ and another ~40% while travelling the US 🇺🇸
