  • I spent around 4 hours on the music using SoundBox. I used the only song I know how to play on the piano 🎹 and added some drums and bass
  • I did around 15 iterations with Poki playtests, which gave me some good insights, especially to improve the tutorial

Next steps if I had more time and space

  • improve scoring system
  • additional modes, e.g. time-based or gambling
  • add custom svgs
  • add sounds

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: very nice theme usage and game idea, played it a lot already, the levels are fun and challenging!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: The game fits the theme perfectly. It’s fun and makes you think, which I really like. The controls are smooth, and the design looks good on both mobile and laptop.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Polished production. Great use of emoji and overall smooth graphics and nice music.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Full marks for the theme!

Fun puzzlegame, i could almost “hear” the constant complaints of the guests when i again seated a horse-o-phobic frog opposite of the horse, that could not stand newspapers,.. :-)

i also like the music, matches the theme, good job with soundbox.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Sudoku for dinner planning. It’s pretty interesting and challenging, but fun game. The music is pretty enjoyable too! Some sound effects would have fit perfectly too.

The theme is on point. While I understand the point of the phobias, lore-wise it’s confusing why a book would feel fear or something.

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text: True. It was an inner conflict between having the weirdest phobias possible and having living things. I ended up enforcing living emojis only in the tutorial, hoping the mechanics are clear enough after those levels.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Matches the theme very well.

Super creative idea.

Awesome job.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun game of shuffling seated phobias.

I’m glad the hints were there, and the shaking red borders to know what needed moving or satisfying

The graphics and animations were good

My only criticism was that it was over too soon

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
Thanks for your feedback!

Over too soon? Did you achieve 9999 ⭐ for all difficulties? 🙃 But I guess, you mean more predefined levels and/or variations?

comments.author: NoNiMad
comments.text: Simple but really good puzzle game where it can quickly get quite complicated! Audio and graphics were nice, got me hooked for some time!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Intresting mechanics and fits the theme. Very good execution. More variety would be welcome - more tables, things like fobias of particular color, or animals in general etc.,

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text: Nice puzzle! Consider making educational games. I’ll show this to my 5yo as sun as he come back home from school. There is a true potential.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
This game was pretty interesting. I liked how I felt like I beat it and then was shown that I did not, in fact, beat it, and there were actually more difficulty levels!

I liked the mechanics of fear. It was challenging to try to keep track of everyone’s fears at the bigger tables.

The graphics were clear enough. I think this was a great use of emoji to save space, and I felt that the fear animation gave each character a bit of personality.

In the end, the game beat me, but I guess it’s good that the game has challenges for event planners more skilled than I.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Really cool idea with a cool twist on this years theme! Having to seat everybody in the right seats without triggering any phobias is a really fun challenge. Great job!

api.name: Coup Ahoo
api.description:
You’re finally fed up with your fleet and their incompetence. Take down all 13 of them one by one and claim your spot at the top. Along the way, you’ll discover precious cargo dice that represent your ship’s vitality and firepower. You may also encounter friendly merchants, carpenters, and potential crew members eager to join and assist you on your journey.

The game is played entirely with a mouse (or touch). You can mute the game by pressing M.

Your ship’s cargo consists of dice, and their total pip values represent your ship’s total HP. These dice also influence the number of damage dice you roll in combat. Be careful not to let your cargo’s total value reach the dreaded number, as even rolling that in combat can have unfavorable consequences.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
I made it to x4, felt like I would never lose until that point, but I did make a few mad decisions so maybe could have made it further. Played for quite some time!

Great graphics and game play, quite addictive! I imagine with more KB of space you could add a lot more features and add more variety to the game. Really great entry!

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: I actually already did an enhanced edition which I released on my itch at the same time as this one. I hit the size limit after the first week indeed had some more feature/event ideas to give it more variety so I kept working on it for the remaining three weeks of the jam.

comments.author: Santi Herranz
comments.text: Very fun, I like the music and the sound effects. Easy to play in turns but difficult to win, unfortunately for me.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
What a great and fun entry. I love the animations, the style and the gameplay. It gradually becomes more and more difficult, and we get some random rolls to get lucky (or not.)

I don’t know the difference between the orange die and the regular die. Maybe there are no differences. I was also wondering what the “14” on the boat meant. I thought it was my HP at first, but I think it only means I’m the number 14 boat. Maybe my HP could have been the indication instead? Just a thought.

Overall a good entry, the first I’m voting on, and it’s a great start, what a gem. Will play more later.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
The orange dice are spice and can be traded with merchants. I kinda wanted to keep it somewhat vague as players would most likely figure it out when they encounter a merchant. But I guess it wasn’t a great decision as quite a few people have been confused about them.

The number on the boat is indeed just the number of it. And the dice you have are actually your HP.

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Really, really fun and clean. The music, the SFX and the graphics really meld together in a nice way. The gameplay itself is fast and fun, and I played for a good while. Never made it to the end as it seems the dice started rolling only zeroes and ones after a while (even though I had high value cargo). I might have misunderstood something. Really fun anyway.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: The damage dice always roll 0-2, the amount of cargo only reflect on how many of them you get to roll.

comments.author: Rustie
comments.text:
This was really enjoyable, I’ve played it a couple of times now as it took me a couple of goes to understand the strategy of preserving the amount of dice you have on your boat and also when to have a big enemy hit be “tanked” into one of your dice with a low number.

The theme was subtly in the game play as well; I think when I scored 13 hits on an enemy boat it always failed.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: A kind of game I hadn’t seen before, cute and nice music

comments.author: Antonio Salvati
comments.text: lovely graphic and animations, the gameplay is nice, innovative and challenging!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
I really liked how the dice worked in the game, both the number and pips. Trying to decide which die to take the hit so you could keep as many dice as possible was fun. I also liked how 13 played into it by making the dice unlucky and losing a turn. Also not having the damage pass through to other dice was a nice touch. That ensures you get at least a certain number of rounds before defeating the enemy (rather than doing so much damage you kill multiple dice in one go).

I don’t understand what the orange dice do though. I also never figured out how to accept dice from the trader for spice (it only let me move on without doing anything). The shipwright might be too powerful as I figured out you could reroll every round and get 2 free repairs. With enough dice this would still do enough damage to 1 shot any of the opponents dice. Combine this with steel dice that only take 1 hit a round, and it’s a really powerful combo.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: The orange dice are the spice you trade with. You just click one in your side and get dice based on the value of it. And you’re thinking of quartermaster, not shipwright. Now that I think about it, this version doesn’t even have shipwright. I also released and “enhanced edition” on my itch which I spent 3 of the 4 jam weeks working on after hitting the size limit. To top off that combo, having navigator be there to give you extra rerolls makes it even more busted.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Very nice entry. All of the animation is so good, and the music is nice. Interesting mechanics also.

I never got very far as all of my rolls were missing or so low.

I think you should be able to change your mind when taking loot as well, I clicked a spice but wanted another but it was already set, grr.

Really cool though, lots of variation in play.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Not sure if it wasn’t clear but damage dice always just roll 0-2. Allowing you to change your mind is a bit tricky one. Can’t read the mind of the player so then it would need to be an extra confirmation which might be annoying for the 90% of times when you do not want to do it. And even that doesn’t fully fix the issue. Just gotta have some restraint and not be so trigger happy. I’ve fallen to that same pit several times too being all “oo shiny, gimme”.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
The integration of the dice as damage and hitpoints is really great!

I played it 6 or 7 times, my best game was when I lost against the #1

Every play was different, thanks to the different stages inbetween of the battles, that adds a huge advantage for re-playability!

Nice, cozy music, lovely gfx-style!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text:
Presentation is lovely and kept me playing. Really wanted to complete but got as far as boat 4. The middle game got to the point where i couldnt lose but the cpu soon caught up and I was no match for number 4 :D

Will come back to this one.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: If you do come back to it, there is also a post jam (or started it after the first week really) version on my itch with a lot more content and variety. A lot more different crew members, other trinkets, special dice and a ton of different kinds of events between encounters.

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: I love the jolly crew bopping to the music. The graphics and feeling of the game are great. The idea of using dice for hitpoints is quite clever. This is one of the top entries for me.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: This is one of my favorite games this year! Thank you!! Well designed, a lot of fun and great style.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: The graphics were really fun! Also enjoyed the music. The combat got a little repetitive, but the different friendly interlude people were a nice way to spice (pun intended) things up.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yeah it does get repetitive. That’s exactly why I spent 3/4 of the month making an enhanced edition of the game with a lot more event variety and different kinds of crew members & effects spicing up the combat too.

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Cool that you can play entirely with a mouse.

Fun graphics, and nice music. Love the bopping characters. It’s really makes the gameplay fun. The use of dice as cargo is fun and interesting. I like that you can recruit people along the way (and they are named things like “CSS”). Funny that the ship gets bigger when you add more dice!

Would be good to have a “ship” counter at the top so you know which number ship you are up to. I lost track after like the second ship, and made it to the end, but along the way, had no idea how far we were in. Super fun, and defeated all 13 on the first try!

Love the ending animation. Then was a little confused; does the game keep going?

In your README you have “copyright Antti Haavikko 2023”. Do you mean 2024?

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
The ships are numbered 13-1 so technically there is a counter. It is indeed infinite post-win while enemies keep getting tougher and you don’t get any new events anymore.

Ah yes, took the same code base as last year and didn’t update the readme…

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Great entry!! Amazing presentation and fun gameplay. I would had played it for hours if there were more progression elements like some form of boss, islands, etc.

On my top 5 this year.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yup, the size limiting was indeed a bit limiting. I released an enhanced edition of the game on my itch at the same time with a lot more variety & content.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This game was really fun! The music was perfect, and the art style was so fricking adorable. The game is like a mini Risk on the seas. It was perhaps a bit too random and luck based. I felt like there was not much I could have done to prevent my ship from sinking. I don’t like when my life depends on getting a lucky break.

It wasn’t clear what the orange cubes were for, until I read the other comments. It would have been better to make them more obvious. For example, the merchant ship should have all orange dice. Also, the whole time I got a single merchant ship, so I don’t see the point in hoarding orange dice.

When the ship grows very big, you the dice go on a second row that isn’t visible anymore. Also the action buttons are on top of the floating dice.

I didn’t understand what the “unlucky 13” was about or what the effect was.

The idea is really fun, but I think some game balance improvements could take it to new heights.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
Unlucky 13 is when your cargo totals up to 13 in which case you miss a turn or when your damage dice total up to 13 when you do no damage at all.

The merchant can’t really have all orange dice as he needs normal dice in stock that he sells you. For example, giving 3 spice dice for a 3 pip value spice dice of your own would be a bit too good of a deal. But yeah, guess I need to figure out something to make it clearer as people aren’t quite as explorative with the game mechanics as I thought.

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text:
This was great fun! Love the way the characters move in time with the music. Cool style of graphics too!

I thought I would go on for ever but ended up in Davy Jones’ locker! Great game.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Loved the game, played for waaay too long haha. Made it to infinite mode x5, but got into a stalemate where I fixed my plated cargo and the enemy fixed their cargo and we shot back and forth for a long time before I just gave up. Amazing game, nice work!

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: This was a blast to play and I hope you release this on mobile someday! I enjoyed how lively everything felt!

comments.author: ckolin
comments.text: That was awesome! Very cute graphics and a cool soundtrack. I’m not sure I understood what’s special about the orange crates. The game feels very polished, even the pirates’ arms have little animations!

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Insanely awesome graphics, audio, and gameplay.

This game is so polished and fun.

Ended up playing it for hours.

Amazing job.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Funky music, cute graphics, and fun to play.

The canons on boats was fun.

I liked it when other sailors joined my boat, nice dynamic.

comments.author: Lucas Drummond
comments.text: Fun! Really creative use of simple graphics and mechanics that build really nicely. The damage output mechanic is a it confusing at first.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Parley?

I don’t really know how long it takes to finish, but everything else is cool!

Congratulations!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I had a lot of fun playing this game! The animations, graphics and camera movements are impressive. The music is good too, but can be a bit repetitive. Very good entry, congratulations!

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
A very neat little game! Great graphics and music, I like how the little characters are bobbing around to the music. Some really nice details in here too, to water sfx great - and I really like the animated camera.

Lots of good things here to appreciate, well done!

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: The most fun game I played so far in this competition.

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: What an adorable happy bouncy… sea sausage? The game concept is original and the animation & music a delight.

comments.author: Mezriss
comments.text: Great presentation - I especially love this cloudy background, fun gameplay. Not much to add to what was already said in other comments - trading needed a bit more explanation, getting fixes after each reroll removes some of the “strategic depths”.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Such a vibe! Fun gameplay and a lovely cast of chars!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: the graphics are amazing and the animation is even more, well done on the game I’ve played a while and it does not get boring fast, ill write the review and play some more before moving to the other games. well done

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Super cool game! Managed to get to infinity +2, at that point I decided to surf the sea to play other games :)

In terms of feedback, I don’t think there are any areas of the game that could be improved - the mechanics, graphics (those camera animations wow!), audio - it’s all pretty perfect to me. Great game!

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Very cool game! Love the presentation and animations, great controls and fun gameplay!

I played a few times after my friend recommended me this one early in voting. Out of the 20 or 30 I have played this is def a top 5, if not top 2 or 3 so far.

Took a while to catch on to some of the mechanics, but after that it is quite good.

comments.author: Tom Copeland
comments.text:
A clever and addictive game! I played it probably like ten times. It seems very dependent on luck, sometimes I died in the first few rounds, other times, I got a good crew combo and made it into the infinite rounds. Once you start losing, it’s very hard to recover.

The music and animations are great. Very fun.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yeah, it is indeed a bit too reliant on luck. For the enhanced edition of it, I added a bunch more content and tried to give more choices the player can make to be not quite as luck based.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
That’s a solid entry. Very nice game concept! Really addictive!

I would consider making the types of cargo and their effect a little more explicit.

Congratulations!

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yeah I’ve already released an updated version of the game where this along with a bunch of other new stuff. I only spent the first week of the jam to create this version as I hit the size limit but kept on adding more stuff which I labeled “enchanced edition” and released at the same time.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hey Antti! Thanks for another gem of your submissions to js13k!

  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game under 13kb! Not to mention all the goodies you packed into this size.
    • Keeping a game complete, and adding the extra challenge for the brave ones.
  • I liked that you:
    • Had all these small animations of the characters. At some point in the game I was rowing with them and dancing while throwing dice.
    • Kept your style! I could recognize it was your game just from the thumbnail overall shapes and colors. It’s authentic and yours.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • On my first run I, without knowing, overpowered the whole thing – I had more than 40 dice at some point (the boat looked hilariously large). So I was defeating the later levels and the king without any problem. On the additional challenge, the lack of recouping dice from battles made me disappear in 5 battles. I think with some adjustments I would be going for much longer?
    • I later realized that I just had a very luck run, with many merchants coming my way, to a point I couldn’t reproduce in later plays, keeping around of 10 dice at maximum. So I assume there is some balance to reach the king with almost the right dice.
    • I could see this game as those auto battlers? The strategy for picking the best dice for damage is rather simple – I assume that’s what the AI is doing – so I didn’t see much fun in keeping up wih the clicking. The fun was taking roll decisions and getting to the next encounter.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback! Yeah, it is indeed quite heavily reliant on luck. I spent 3 of the jam weeks still building on the game even after hitting the size limit after the first week. In this “enhanced edition”, I tried to give more options to players to have more say on their runs and of course a load of different events, crew members and passive relics keeping the game from getting too repetitive and boring that quick. And there indeed is a way to autoplay the combat there too. But with some new special dice and crew member effects etc, the strategy isn’t always so boringly simple all the time anymore.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: Another excellent entry for this competition. And it is quite unique. The graphics are cute and the 8bit background music give it a nice atmosphere. The gameplay is quite based on luck (especially at the beginning), but when you get a nice combination of people to help you, it feels so good! And there is some “end game” things to do too, what a pleasant surprise. Overall, this is a fantastic little game. Thank you for submitting it!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
I think this is my favorite game so far, and I’ve played dozens of them by now!

The dice mechanics, from the idea of rerolling attacks, losing damage by swapping a dice face, and rerolling cargo, were really really fun.

I also enjoyed the modifiers from quartermasters and the gray dice thing that makes it only take 1 damage, because these made me change my thinking. If there’s a repair-quartermaster, I want to reroll more often. If there’s a 1-damage gray dice, I want to direct damage to it, but if there’s both, maybe I want other dice to die and make the repair-quartermaster more likely to repair my armor dice.

I might be wrong, but I think there would be some more interesting choices if the spice dice weren’t able to attack. Right now they’re just too beneficial to go without!

I loved the graphics and music, which make this feel like a fun adventure in the seas.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: I think having spice dice not being in attack might be too harsh. But I have indeed already added some more downsides to them on the post jam version, like for example event with pirates who in trading will demand you to give up all your spice or face a fight etc. So having loads of them isn’t always that much of a good idea. And obviously it has a lot more content in the form of event variety, more special dice, more unique crew members and special effect passive relics to vary up the runs and give more strategic choices to players.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I’ve not played many games this year but this is in my top 5, hands down.

The music is cute, the sound effects are great, the graphics are simple but gorgeous and the gameplay is interesting.

Obviously having to roll dice means there’s a lot of luck involved but I don’t mind that.

comments.author: Source Caster
comments.text: This game is so addicting… I’ve already beaten level ∞ x 20 and just can’t stop.

comments.author: Dayset
comments.text: Once you collect enough relics with the help of MIMIC and Pearls you become invincible with cannon-firing nukes (20k+ damage) So ∞ x … is just a matter of your consistency. I encountered a divine creature while having an infinite amount of pearls/cargo and wasn’t able to defeat it. It was a loop unless I reduced the count of my cargo to 4 (from 400+) The game freezes when 20k damage is on a scene. (5700x3d CPU) The game is fun but not fair at all. https://imgur.com/a/Lti1ITu

api.name: Templar's Final Stand
api.description:
It’s October 13, 1307. King Philip IV has sent his forces to hunt you down. As one of the last Knights Templar, it’s a battle for survival!

Move across the battlefield, take down enemies, and gather powerful items. But beware - if your total load reaches 13, your triskaidekaphobia kicks in, and every step will become a painful struggle.

Each move matters, and survival depends on balancing your strategy. Let’s see how long you can last!

Controls

  • Click/Tap to make your selections.
  • ↑↓←→ / WSAD to move up, down, left, or right on the keyboard.
  • Swipe up, down, left, or right on mobile devices to move.

Rules

  • Move toward enemies or items to attack or equip.
  • Combine same items to create stronger versions—except potions, which may either buff or debuff.
  • Equipments (except potions) add weight, which lasts until the item’s duration ends.
  • At a total weight of 13 or more, every step will cause damage.

Choose a Class

  • Knight: Brave and balanced, reliable in combat.
  • Wizard: Physically weak but able to use potions to attack enemies.
  • Defender: Reflecting damage back to enemies with his shield.

Know Your Enemies

  • Whirlstriker: Strikes surrounding opponents.
  • Guardian: High shield defense.
  • Counterstriker: Retaliates against incoming attacks.
  • Spearman: Penetrates through shields with attacks.
  • Crossblade: Attacks in a full cross-shaped pattern.
  • Lancepiercer: Penetrates shields and strikes nearby opponents.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: Is a new concept of the methodical chess, nice to play but it cost time understanding the gamification. The graphics are really nice in the flat design, clean and pretty original Color’s for the theme

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thank you for playing! Glad to hear that you like the graphics in the game. And I understand that the game takes some time to fully grasp, and that’s an area that I can continue to enhance.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Great entry. Love the take on 2048, and the graphics where very nice.

I feel adding some extra gameplay to battles could had made it more fun. Anyway, great work!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thank you so much for playing!! I agree that there’s room to improve the depth, particularly outside of the item combining system. The game is currently focused on breadth lol, like provide 3 different classes. (I highly recommend giving the Wizard a try!) Maybe I can add more “controllable” mechanisms to improve gameplay.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: Very well done and a great concept and a lot of fun shuffling around the enemies and upgrades. Great work!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thanks for playing and for your kind comment! I’m glad you enjoyed the game!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Original game, loved the inspiration of 2048. It made me think about Pyrene too, which was just released.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thanks for playing my entry! I’ll definitely check Pyrene out when I get the chance.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Enjoyed playing through, played mostly as the knight which seems easy at first but got difficult!

Was unsure I was using the wizard correctly as I found that difficult, also collected some negative cards a few times which did not help.

Great graphic style and well made!

Would be nice to have levels and move along a map rather than just have continious play.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Thank you so much for playing! The strategy with the Wizard is that if you encounter a negative potion, you can try combining it. The combination can deal damage, and there’s also a chance to turn into positive potion!

Having levels is a great suggestion - it could give players a clearer sense of progress and objectives. I’ll definitely consider that!

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: I like the gameplay. It takes a little time to get used to how the mechanics work but all in all it’s great!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thank you for playing! I’m glad you enjoyed the gameplay! You’re right! It can be a bit hard to understand the rules at first. There’s definitely room for improvement in that area!

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text: This is an interesting concept! And really well executed! I like it!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Glad to hear that you like it! Thanks

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is pretty cool! Gameplay is unique and makes for a fun, strategic puzzle. Graphics and animations are very slick!

Cool that there are three different classes to play as; each feels pretty unique and requires a different playstyle.

Also nice that there are different enemy types, although I felt like I wanted some more visual difference between each enemy? Like maybe different colors to show their relative strength or something

Also, props for (almost) tying in last year’s theme, too :D

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Thank you so much for playing! I’m really happy to hear that you noticed the distinct playstyles of the three classes! That’s exactly the kind of experience I wanted to bring to the players.

I also appreciate your feedback on the enemy visuals, and I’ll keep that in mind for future improvements. Thanks again for the thoughtful comments!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Funky sound track with drums.

I enjoyed playing, the whole shift tiles dynamic is good and really makes you take care what you click on and in which direction.

The graphics were good and stylised.

There’s a lot more to this game so I’ll have to come back to it after the voting to have some longer sessions

Great job

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Thank you so much for playing! Audio is my soft spot, I’m happy that you noticed it!

I’m excited to hear that you enjoyed the game and even more thrilled that you’re planning to come back for longer sessions after the voting. It means a lot! I hope you continue to have fun with it, and I appreciate your feedback.

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text:
One of my favourite games in the competition. A great mix of “2048” gameplay combined with RPG elements, three classes, six enemies and the “Reigns” look.

This is awesome!

CONGRATULATIONS

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thank you for such high praise!!! I’m really glad you enjoyed the game.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Wow!

What an incredible concept!! I was scared at the beginning that there would be too many rules, but ended up being so easy to play. Music is simple yet very good. Animations are just perfect. The idea of getting boosts and improving your character makes you wanna continue playing to be more powerful, I think it’s incredible.

The intro is very well mastered as well, I can jump in the game in a few seconds and not be lost.

Very very well done

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Thank you so much for your kind words! I agree, the rules can feel a bit overwhelming at first, and I see that as an area I can improve on to make things clearer from the beginning.

I’m really grateful for such high praise. It truly means a lot to me! I’m glad you enjoyed the game.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
wonderful almost full modification of the 2048 gameplay I love the RPG-elements in it.

The music gives it a good atmosphere

very good game!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Thank you! Great to hear that you love the RPG-elements in the game! and super happy that you feel the music helped create a good atmosphere! Your feedback means a lot, and I really appreciate it.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This game is really cool, and has really clever mechanics. A very complicated twist on 2048! However, I found it perhaps a bit unbalanced. I didn’t feel like I had good control of what was going on, and me winning felt a lot down to luck of the draw. The meaning of many of the symbols is not clear. Some of the stats text didn’t fit the container, which made it very hard to understand my standing.

The music, sound effects and art were pretty good, and the controls were dead simple. I liked the introduction of the 13 weight limit as the theme interpretation.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Thank you for playing and giving constructive feedback! Super agree that there are a room to improve on game balancing and how to make the symbol/text more clearer!

I’ll take this suggestions in the future. Thanks again for your input; it really helps!

api.name: Escape From Trece
api.description:
This game is about a character who wants to escape from the 13th floor using the stairs, avoiding some giant moths.

Idea

The idea was inspired from true events, I lived with my wife and kids on a 13th floor, and there was a time when both elevators were not working, so my wife had to use the stairs, also she doesn’t like moths.

Objective

The objective is to get to the ground floor, avoiding the moths. If you are hit by a moth you lose one life.

Controls

To play you can use on-screen buttons or keyboard up - down arrows.

Tech stack

Plain js, html and css. I’m using a 2D canvas library I’ve been writing for a couple of years. Code is minified using terser.

Tip: there are no moths at the edges of the stairs.

Thanks to

My wife and kids who helped me with ideas and beta testing.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Reminds me of those old handheld LCD games. The game gets easier with every floor the player reaches, so somehow thats the inverse Progression of a game :-) But it works, and kept me motivated until i finally reached the ground floor.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: It’s a very straightforward game. Everything makes sense and the challenge is there in a natural kind of way. I like that it requires decent user movement in order to get all the way to the bottom. I have to admit that I failed about 5 times before I won. Lol

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Ok this is a silly one, but entertaining. I thought white button shirts were falling from the sky, but now I know they were moths. The main reason I died was because I was too impatient :) I’m glad each floor had a safe haven or I would have died from anxiety. This game deserved a goofy background music.

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: Unusual choice to use up/down keys rather than left/right which felt more natural to me. Paradoxically, it’s harder at the beginning than at the end because I have little sight of the moths and little time to react on the top floor compared to the bottom ones.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Nice idea! funny game easy to learn gameplay. I reached to first floor after some attemps with only 1 life point remaining

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text: I tried this on mobile, unfortunately the screen stayed blank so couldn’t play

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Cool little idea. I think the moths fell at a really nice rate, and I’m glad there was some audio. Your wife is wrongo, moth’s are kewl!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I liking that this is a true story. I have same about moths also. It is most realistic game on theme i have play this year.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Nice game, really enjoyed playing.

api.name: FREEZOBIA
api.description:
Try to earn the most points without dying. Avoid enemies and stay close to fireplace so you don’t get freezed. The default time for getting freezed is 13 seconds, so that’s how long you have to find new campfire before it is too late.

W - moves up S - moves down A - moves left D - moves right Q - shoots grenade space - shoot bullets

Destroy colored rocks to get points and additional perks.

blue rock - next bullet deals 13 damage red rock - restore missing health black rock - one more grenade yellow rock - speed boost

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to move the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: K3PAA
comments.text: Thanks, will note that for feature game development.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Nice graphics and gameplay, other than the lack of audio, I think the only thing holding you back here is the controls. Hooking mouse click to fire as well would be a nice start, but I think really adding controller support would make this game a lot better! One analog stick to move, one to aim. The problem with using the mouse is that your character moves around within the frame (they aren’t always dead center), this means when you move your player, the direction you aim changes. This feels bad. If you used analog sticks this wouldn’t happen, or alternatively you could keep the player direct center of the screen at all time.

Still, I think this is a great foundation for a game, tweak controls and add sound and music and you’ve got something really good here. Nice work!

comments.author: K3PAA
comments.text: Thanks, for feedback. Noted the suggestions .

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
Great graphics and I really like how the arm holding the gun and aiming looked. I liked how the theme of staying by the fireplace was 13 seconds.

Notes:

  • It looks like the game generates the next fireplace only after the current one is out. It would be better to do this while you’re in the current one.
  • Generating the next fireplace sometimes leads to me getting stuck in a tree or object.
  • A indicator of your time to freeze when outside of the fireplace would help. Even just a number of seconds.
  • An indicator of your reload. Even just the word “reloading” appearing by the character would help.
  • Enemy deaths could use a particle effect or death animation. It just makes for more satisfying gameplay.

comments.author: K3PAA
comments.text: Sure, thanks for feedback. Will work on that for feature development.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
The splash screens says I should shoot with a mouse click, but it seems I should rely on a space instead. I would love if I could use mouse click :)

I like the graphics in general, and the game play isn’t bad, but I found it a little difficult.

There is potential to be a fun game though!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
i like the pixelart and the basis of the gameplay.

Music/sound-fx would add much to the experience.

When the player needs the mouse to aim, it would be great if also the mouse would be the trigger for the gun (left mousebutton) and the grenades (right mouse button)

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I understand trying to stick to the theme, but i think 13 seconds of game play before i have to find a new campfire, just felt too much. This game is really hard. The character is also kinda slow, so I always reach the next campfire just in time. I felt like there was little time to actually explore the game before the next campfire died as well.

I think the game could use a softer learning and difficulty curve.

The art was nice, but I missed the sound effects.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice pixel art and gameplay, all works great. Some sounds would have added to the feel of the game and maybe some particle affects / screen shake etc.

Love the grenades and the breakable environment.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Great game, I loved the pixel art! To me it would have been more natural to shoot with the mouse instead of the space bar, but it was a good experience. Music and sound effets woud certainly benefit the game. Good job :)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game looked good

The graphics were well made

It was over pretty quickly, how do I get more time?

For me the control method was a little disjointed - move with WASD but aim with mouse, but shoot with SPACE. Perhaps move with keyboard and aim+shoot with mouse could work better?

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • Great work on:
    • The graphics. The pixel art is consistent and charming for what you are going for here.
  • I liked that you:
    • Put together an interesting concept with the fireplace mechanics, slimes and shooting.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • If I understood correctly, the map is very small and when you walk to the border of it you get transported back to a location and some of the stuff resets (like a fireplace). That broke my experience with the game, because I couldn’t really explore the world and find the fireplaces, I was just going ahead and suddenly jumped into another location and had to adjust course.
    • You have some mechanics together but they need to be balanced. I didn’t feel the challenge was scaling up or anything. Because of the jumps I was at lost with trying to survive. Maybe 13s or the fireplace running out of fire are too tight for the amount of stuff the players need to handle. I can’t think of aiming, shooting at rocks, avoiding slimes and so on all at the same time. The 13s effectively made me be on the run at all times.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
I really like the idea of this one. Twin stick shooters are always fun, and I think this could have been one of my favorites, but here are a few notes for next year that I think are holding it back:

  • if I’m aiming with the mouse, I should be able to shoot by clicking. You can always keep the space bar too but for most people it won’t feel natural. It also caused me to restart the game too quickly after dying.
  • the goal of the game wasn’t super obvious. I’d add a giant label saying “Find the next campfire” or similar. You also have a few cool mechanics but it’s hard to figure them out within the game.
  • it felt a bit slow. Not sure if my laptop is too weak but I think this game could easily run at 60 FPS. There’s probably a few optimization tweaks you can make
  • difficulty could be lower at the beginning. Show me a campfire closer so I know where to look, and then I can figure it out for the next one

Hope you can come back with another kickass shooter next year!

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Very cool and interesting game! The graphics are impressive and the gameplay is very smooth. It would be nice to show players what they need to do when the first campfire goes out and show the timer somewhere more central to remind players.

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I wish I had more direction, as everything feels really challenging! I do like the graphics and the game feels good.

api.name: 13 The Damned
api.description:
In 13 the Damned, a card game inspired by triskaidekaphobia—the fear of the number 13—every decision is a gamble with fate. Flip cards, accumulate points, and push your luck. But beware, revealing a King (worth 13) spells doom, ending the game in an instant. Will you risk it all or walk away before it’s too late? With three levels of difficulty and a rising score multiplier for every card revealed, every choice could lead you to victory or disaster.

How to Play

  1. 12 cards are randomly picked from a standard 54-card deck and placed face-down on the board.
  2. Reveal cards one by one, each with a value from 1 to 13 (King = 13).
  3. Accumulate the values of the revealed cards, but don’t let the total exceed 13 points, or you’ll lose those cards.
  4. For each card you reveal without clicking Keep Cards, your score multiplier increases by 1, boosting your potential score.
  5. You can lock in your score at any time by pressing Keep Cards, or end the game early with End Game to save your total.
  6. Be cautious! Revealing a King will instantly end the game, marking you as damned.

Difficulty Levels

  • Easy: No Kings will appear, but your final score is multiplied by 0.75.
  • Normal: One King may appear, with a score multiplier of 1x.
  • Hard: Two Kings could appear, with a score multiplier of 2x.

Track your progress and compete for the top spot on the highscore board !

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Interesting luck game. There’s not a lot of skill involved, since the ideal strategy seems to just always pick 1 card and keep it. The risk of loosing all cards seems to be too high to be worth it to pick more than one card.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text: Thank you for your feedback. I’m thinking to a way to adapt cards selection by their value according to the difficulty selected. I think also to integrate the possibilité to pick a jocker that could be use to skip a king.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Nice overall implementation, all worked to the rules you explained. I did sturggle to get 2 cards without going bust, seemed the best strategy was to keep on keeping!

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hello

Thank you for your feedback. I’m thinking to a way to adapt cards selection by their value according to the difficulty selected. I think also to integrate the possibility to pick a jocker that could be use to skip a king.

regards

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text:
Highest easy: 78 Highest medium: 98 Highest hard: 194

Nice little gambling game. Fun!

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hello

Thanks for your feedback.

regards

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Interesting idea, though mostly luck based. For the most part I just would pick 2 cards and then keep since they were almost always going to total to around 10 (the average of adding 2 numbers from 1-13 is about 14). I would keep most cards and only would lose about 1 or 2 pairs.

Selecting a difficulty didn’t appear to work at first clicking on a setting doesn’t look like it updates anything nor does it close the menu (you have to press [r] for that). Once I started a new game did I realize it worked.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hello @straker

Thank you to take time to test my game.

I’m thinking to a way to adapt cards selection by their value according to the difficulty selected. I think also to integrate the possibility to pick a jocker that could be use to skip a king.

I’m not satisfy by the difficulty menu. I will do something to make the difficulty selection clearer.

regards

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
I think I found bug that you can click twice or more time on the same card. But I haven’t used it to have a pure experience.

It’s pretty simple game and remind me BlackJack. But with 13 instead of 21+. Took me 10 sec to understand the rules. As a game of luck it simple but works.

I like that in the end you can put your name and see a local hall of fame. Nice little touch.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hello Alexey

Thank you for your feedback. I will check the case of clicking same card several times.

regards

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Simple gambling game, but no real objective except high score. You should add some score to reach after a certain amount of game, with cards picked in the same set, so we should remember what was already picked to try playing with probability instead of just randomness :)

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hi Jonathan Thanks for your feedback. Your idea is really interesting. I will see how to implement it to the game.

regards

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: It’s kind of like playing a repeating game of blackjack, except you are trying to avoid going bust at 13 points instead. Click a card then decide if you want to risk clicking another. If not, click “Keep Cards” and the counter resets so you can try again - the aim being to clear all 12 cards from the grid. Obviously, you could just click “Keep Cards” after every single card, but you’ll end up with a rubbish score if you do. I feel given how simple the game is, it could have explained how to play all by itself - as it stands, you really need to read the instructions before even starting it or you won’t have a clue what’s going on! Aside from this, it’s a nice twist on the concept and definitely on-theme.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
@photonstorm

I really appreciate your comments. Thank you so much for taking time to write it.

regards

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game worked well.

The layout was good, and the game was easy to pick up.

Pretty much a game of completed chance. I played a few times and didn’t see a king card. Were the difficulty levels just picking slightly more cards from a particular range? e.g. easy was mostly low value cards?

Perhaps decorate the cards like traditional playing cards, or have an animation when cards are revealed?

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text: So basically blackjack, played as a solitaire, but with 13 being the limit instead of 21 ? I wished the cards were a bit more realistic – number in the corner with as many symbols on the card, and simple gfx for the figures. You probably can pack this much tighter than 13 kb, freeing room for more details. For instance, graying out the cards from the former turns so those from the current one stand out.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hi @Siorki

Thank you for your feedback. To be honest, it is my first game. I do my best to keep in line with my plan to build a game fully playable. So I made choice and let graphical part away. I hope to improve my skills for next challenges. :p

comments.author: Tom Copeland
comments.text: Interesting idea. I got a score of 1313 (in keeping with the theme :D) after discovering that you can reuse cards that are already revealed, allowing to you get a perfect 144 every time.

comments.author: sdeseille
comments.text:
Hi @thcopeland

Thank for your feedback. :) @rndD have discovered this bug also and I corrected it but I will push to my main branch after the vote finished. regards

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: Good concept. I could see this being a reasonable game to play with a real deck. Might just be me, but I wish the numbers were actually numbers instead of text, it would be easier for me to do math on them. I don’t think the difficulty screen worked? At least, I couldn’t select difficulty, I had to restart every time. Unfortunately I was able to bug the game by clicking on cards I had already played, but I could play the game without doing that.
games: XIII Days To Save The Kingdom

api.name: Cry in The 13th
api.description: Your friends decide to go camping on the 13th of the month, but something very bad happens. Now you need to play hide and seek with a psychopath. Use the mouse to hide while you can and pray that he wont find your spot.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: The game doesn’t work well on the site. It places itself over the voting field for some reason.. Anyways, it took me a while to understand how to play. When I did, I passed a few levels, but I reached a point where I hid and another buddy chose the same spot. The killer then came, attacked that spot and the game hanged. Nice music thou

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I was the only survivor! Nice job, I see that you implemented full path finding for the various characters, which is impressive. Congrats on submitting!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: It looks and sounds kind of creepy and scary but I had trouble figuring out what was happening or how to do stuff - much like how a victim of a deadly massacre might feel, in a panic and not sure where to run! 🔪🩸 😱 so in a way it succeeded and the killer got me every time!!!lol but I kept thinking I wasn’t quite getting it. I had some troubles with the way it uses a popup - perhaps my adblocker was interfering with your canvas implementation being able to detect my mouse movements? alternately, it really could be just that I didn’t quite understand what I was doing, but either way I am glad you joined js13k!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Simple idea, works ok, I did not survive :(

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I liked the killer picture when he finds you!

It took me a while to understand how to play. Once I did, I could pass a few levels, but up to a point, I was caught even that it was another character that was found.

Interesting idea though! Congrats!

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: I liked the sound and the creepy picture when you get caught! But I really struggled to play this. It took me several goes before I figured out how to move. Then I managed to hide (I think) behind a tent. The baddie didn’t find me, so on the next level I hid in the tent again and he ran to a bush and the game ended.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The graphics were good.

However the playfield on the screen was too small - but the text didn’t fit on it.

I liked the music and the scary character.

The mouse controls were a little slow and unresponsive.

I only survived a couple of nights.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
It was a bit difficult to figure out what to do at start, or if the game was responding to my clicks. I’d have loved it if the playing screen was a bit bigger as well.

However, the game manages to keep you guessing if you’ve hidden successfully, especially since the sound gives a sense of urgency to the game. Great touch with the creepy picture that appears when you get caught!

Even though I survived a bit, in the end I didn’t manage to avoid the killer… ☠️

api.name: F-Stop
api.description: I was not able to come up with a game concept in time, so this is just a tech demo.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Clever tech demo, hope you are able to continue, am sure there are uses for this!

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you! I hope to continue the project soon.

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text: Oooh, this is beautiful. Learning how to implement raytracing with the depth of field in WebGL is still on my bucket list. I’m looking forward to checking out the source. Thank you for submitting it even if it’s unfinished!

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Hey, thanks so much for the comment! I really appreciate it.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Cool tech demo. I wanted to use SDFs also for my game but felt I would waste too much time fighting it so I ended up using regular rendering.

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you! I really appreciate it.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text: Very interesting work overall here, I was not expecting to see a raytraced entry into js13k. Definitely hope you enter again in coming years! I’d like to see a game based on this kind of direction for sure.

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thanks! One of the things I’ve learned from this year’s js13k, is the importance of having an idea and prototype as quickly as possible. That way you can iterate and pivot if needed. Game design is hard! haha.

comments.author: aselitto
comments.text: I felt like this + where’s Waldo would be AMAZING!!! Great work!

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you! Haha nice idea for where’s Waldo.

comments.author: Luke Nickerson
comments.text: Very beautiful, and I appreciate your blog explanation! (https://idkwhatt0callthis.blogspot.com/2024/09/raytracing-187500-voxels-in-browser.html) Thanks for making this.

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you so much for reading! I’m glad you enjoyed it.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Very interested to see what you’ll make of this, if you decide to continue in the next year’s js13k.

The demo works shockingly quick for real-time raytracing, too. I always though of this tech as more of a static scene rendering thing.

Great job! :)

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text:
Thanks so much! I will definitely be continuing the project to make a larger game. I might use the tech for next year’s js13k as well.

Yes, you are correct, for a long time this tech was only used for static scene rendering. Also animated Pixar films and stuff like that for example. Each single frame would be static rendered using this kind of tech and then sequenced all together to make the whole animated movie.

I believe this is still how they do it now, but with an even more advanced type of ray tracing called path tracing.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Super pretty! It’s definitely difficult coming up with a game idea for a tech like this. Perhaps you are a photographer that gets stranded in a weird world full of fractal objects and you just chill out taking pictures of those weird mathematical entities? It’s the best idea my brain can come up right now :)

Funnily enough, a couple weeks ago I was checking how Teardown works - your code will be a great source of inspiration. Well done!

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you so much! I had a bunch of photographer ideas for a game, but all of them came to me too late. Next year, my focus is going to be getting a working prototype within the first couple of days. I did not do that well with time management this year, haha.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
I have no useful feedback, but this looks really cool and handles really well.

Hopefully you can build something out of this; if not, it’s cool as it is, too..!

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text: Thank you! I have decided to continue the project and make a game separate from js13k.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Looks good, I’d like to see if you do develop this further what you’re able to make with it.

Despite being really grainy the concept was good, and the whole focus mechanic could be used to good effect in a richer experience.

api.name: Shoot 13 Nomsters
api.description:
I came in to the office early and switched as many M and N keys on keyboards as I could. Some might say I’m a monster, but others will say nomster.

Works fine on desktop, too. More fun in VR.

Nomsters are legally considered to be NPCs for the Decentralized OP Guild Challenge.

comments.author: Chui Hin Wah
comments.text:
cute little game, i played it in vr.

nice of you to include a mute button, kudos to you.

the gun is strangely shot on trigger release, rather than “on trigger down”. the demo projects out there usually set a down threshold to 50% trigger pressed, and then wait until it returns to below 20% or so. that way you can detect the down event without false triggers.

the bullets travel slow, and the “birds” movement are very unpredictable. i basically shoot them while they are to the side.

first playthrough i got 9% hit rate, 2nd playthrough was 8%.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Those nomsters look almost too cute to shoot at them… Probably thats the reason why the player only has this lame soft ball thrower… where is my freakin’ railgun?! Or a BFG 9000! Yes, that would fit. :-P

Pretty hard to hit them. I got only 27% accuracy at my best try. Its an entertaining game. Nice music, btw.

(played on desktop, i have no matching VR equipment, only a PSVR)

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Cute monsters! They are very quick, I had to shoot them in the corners of the screen in the end as I just kept missing.

Nice simple graphics, I know VR is tough so well done!

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
I don’t have VR, but I gave it a shot =)

The shot evasion mechanic is super nicely done. When I had only 3 last nomsters it was hard to trick them to have a successful shot.

Cute mobs, or should I say nobs? And music. Definitely worth trying if you have VR or just want to practice FPS accuracy.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Nice job. Played on desktop, I don’t have VR. On my first playthrough, I assumed the goal was to optimize for time, so I just clicked frantically and got 6% accuracy. Then I realized the goal was accuracy, so 2nd playthrough 36% and last playthrough 50%. It seems like optimizing for accuracy is mostly a matter of waiting patiently for them to go to the corners? I think the projectile hit box could be a little bit bigger. Overall, this was fun. Nice graphics, cute enemies. Nice job!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text: Those nomsters sure are fast! Played it on desktop. I was mostly just shooting in one spot and hoping they would cross my path, then noticed they got stuck on the edge so targeted them there :D.

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
I was able to shoot most of them by just firing at the lower-left corner since they move randomly.

Gameplay was decent, but I wish there were more factors to it.

Nice job.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Very small game, which feels like you could have gotten more out of your 13Kb. That being said, it worked well, the shooting, the guns and music were all well executed.

Best part of the game execution were the nomsters, on the one hand very simple, but the the way their faces rotated while jumping and moving left and right just instantly brought them to life!

Well done!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thanks! I’m glad you liked the little dudes.

And you’re completely right, had I had more time, I could probably make it into a more fleshed out experience. This was my second submission this year, so had to cut all of the corners :)

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text:
Cool little gane. I didm’t play im VR. Really mice nusic. Did nomsters got nultiple life poimt? or was I just a terrible shooter?

Danm, what happemmed to ny keyboard?

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: 7% accuracy. I’m not that good at this game, haha.

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: Simple pew pew! The front and rear sights on the guns are a nice touch. I recognized your signature song from The Neatness ;p

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Accuracy 9% (played on desktop)

Jesus christ was this a frustrating game. But not in a bad way, I had fun. Those bastards are hard to hit. But also it kinda felt like the bullets were too slow or at least took too long to hit the nomsters.

The game is cute and the music too. The surrounding scenario was a bit odd; the game could be a bit more immersive instead of making me feel like i’m in a VR.

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text:
Played in VR. I think this game teaches us something about life and I’m trying to put to words what that is. To start from the top, I played it a couple of times, and the more I tried the worse my accuracy was. First time I played, blasting like there’s no tomorrow, 13% accuracy. I said to myself, Jure, that’s bad, you wasted so much ammo. So I said I’ll focus and I played again… 10% accuracy. Then I was upset, one in 10 shots making it, that’s terrible! And for the next round, I really focused, locked in, just you wait, nomsters, it’s shooting time!! … 7% accuracy. @$!#!

“Be water, my friend. Empty your mind.”

Lovely little game!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Really fun game on mobile too using motion controls

The music was fun and kinda recognisable.

Hitting the bouncing balls got much harder the fewer there were.

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Good shooting gallery, do I win a teddy bear after downing all nomsters ?

Great music, a good point for having made WebXR optional so you can still play with a non-VR setup. I wish there were more features, extra levels, extra nomsters ?

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I got 23% accuracy on desktop! I found that I can wait them to be at the corners :D

Nice entry! Congrats!

comments.author: Akbar Sultanov
comments.text:
Theme: The game effectively captures this year’s theme of fear surrounding the number 13.

Gameplay: The accuracy of the mechanics is commendable. However, it would be beneficial to display the number of shots fired. Shooting at corners seems to be more effective when the monsters are in motion. Additionally, having game-related buttons in a more visible area would improve usability; the UI could use some refinement.

Audio: The audio is good and contributes to a specific mood.

Innovation: The game appears to be a straightforward shooter, lacking distinctive innovative elements.

Graphics: The UI could benefit from more visually appealing elements, and incorporating atmospheric backgrounds would enhance the overall experience.

Controls: The controls are intuitive and easy to use, with no issues encountered.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The use of colorful, playful “Nomsters” is an excellent choice, making the game visually appealing and fun. The minimalistic design with simple shapes and bold colors keeps the focus on the action without overwhelming the player. The soft, rounded designs of the Nomsters give the game a friendly and inviting feel, which contrasts nicely with the shooter mechanics. Maybe some variety in the background or environment could add depth to the visual experience. The simplicity of the design is very effective, allowing players to focus on the gameplay while enjoying the colorful and quirky characters. The Nomsters are charming, and the overall visual aesthetic is fun and lighthearted. Great job creating a visually engaging and approachable game!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Theme: Great match to the theme even though the nomsters are way too cute to be scary :-)

Gameplay: The game was enjoyable and intuitive to play.

Audio: Amazing how it reminds me of Wonder Boy and I am a huge retro fan.

Innovation: Nice shooting game in XR. I wonder how much more the XR space could have been leveraged.

Graphics: Really cute and polished despite the simplicity.

Controls: Intuitive and reactive.

comments.author: Kieran Farr
comments.text:
Gameplay: The gameplay is straightforward – there isn’t much more than shooting 13 bouncing balls. The bouncing movement of the balls can make it frustrating to get the final ball or two until you find a trick. Showing accuracy % at the end adds a fun additional flare.

Graphics: The models are simple but effective with a touch of hand-made uniqueness

Controls: The controls are straightforward and work as expected

Theme: It met the conditions for using the 13 theme but aside from the number of balls there wasn’t a core tie-in

Audio: Good basic sfx and nice touch to have music

Innovation: Shooting balls

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text: I really liked the aesthetics—simple but very beautiful. And the effects when the enemies explode are great. I would perhaps fine-tune the collision detection a bit, as sometimes I fired multiple shots at a stationary enemy, and it seemed like they weren’t hitting. As for ideas, I’d love to see a time-based mode where you have to eliminate all enemies within a certain time limit. Great work overall!

api.name: Triskatopia
api.description: Welcome to the parrallel universe Triskatopia! No 13, no atomic 13, no Aluminium, no air planes or cars. Transport only by hot air balloon. Congratulations! Due to balloon driver shortages, you are now the new recruit. Buckle up and try to land on the target.

comments.author: Chui Hin Wah
comments.text:
beautiful scenery, confusing controls.

what i like the most is being able to get high in a balloon and look at the low-poly but carefully arranged landscape that is done in this game.

the gameplay itself, is just fiddling with the menu until my prediction line points at the X.

prehaps a short of time or something? i really wish it can become a more polished game with hands-on balloon controls. it must be quite fun fiddling with knobs and bobs to fly this giant hot thing.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Very pleasing scene setting. Ground, balloons and clouds, sky, everything looked great!

A few minor things that could have really made this awesome:

  • I’m not to concerned about theme myself, but you will get judged on that as well, so could have had more of a tie in.
  • Needed a bit more polishing, there were some bugs still, nothing game breaking though.
  • The game itself was a bit simple. I would have removed the down button and only allowed for up bursts with the balloon descending automatically so timing of the launch would become more important.
  • I would have liked to see it work on desktop. I played it in VR, but since most people don’t have a VR Headset, desktop is important if you want people to play.

Very good overall!

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text: I can’t start without VR headset :(

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: The graphics are whimsical and the idea original. However, the trip to the target is a bit too sudden, and caused me nausea in VR (because the view moves suddenly while my body register no motion).

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text:
I love the visuals, very pretty and scenic levels! Looking around and seeing those colorful balloons floating around leisurely was very nice.

As some mention the quick trip from the starting point to the target is a recipe for nausea, however developing in VR for years, often in situations where one eye is seeing something and the other eye is seeing the camera turned on it’s head or with the wrong perspective and so on, has prepared me well for this - this is to say I did not have issues with that.

I did have a few issues where I couldn’t see past the balloon and had to crouch in real life, but that’s a common thing that’s hard to address completely.

I wondered if the game could have a twist to it, maybe directing the flight as you go, instead of playing the balloons like a golf ball.

Overall a nice entry, but could have more developed gameplay, as well as a better connection to the theme.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Reallt nice VR experience, I wished I had to control the balloon myself rather than using the aim and shoot method as moving up high was really fun but the route was over so quickly.

Great graphics and easy to control.

comments.author: Akbar Sultanov
comments.text:
Theme: The game lacks a clear association with the fear of the number 13, aside from the description provided by the author. It would benefit from integrating this theme more effectively into the gameplay.

Gameplay: The gameplay is enjoyable, encouraging players to think strategically about guiding the balloon to a landing point. However, the current difficulty level feels low. Reducing the clarity of trajectory hints—perhaps by only revealing a small portion—could enhance the challenge. Additionally, some buttons, like the “up” button, were not always visible. Integrating these controls into the balloon’s interface would improve accessibility.

Audio: While the audio is generally pleasant, the sound effect for the balloon rising is a bit jarring and could be improved. Overall, a few tweaks would enhance the auditory experience.

Innovation: This game is relatively simple and reminiscent of trajectory shooters. Introducing unique mechanics could help it stand out.

Graphics: The graphics are visually appealing, and each level provides a diverse visual experience, adding to the game’s charm.

Controls: The controls are solid, functioning smoothly without any issues.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I tried the game, and it almost seemed to work on desktop mode, but then I couldn’t interact.

Thought it was worth a try without VR, as some of the games worked with either.

Looks really good though

comments.author: Kieran Farr
comments.text:
Audio: The audio design is a highlight, featuring good and responsive sound elements. This adds to the overall experience and helps to create a more immersive environment, partially compensating for weaknesses in other areas.

Innovation: While the core concept is interesting, the implementation falls short in terms of control immersiveness due to control scheme. There’s potential here, but it’s not fully realized in a way that makes the game stand out from others in its genre.

Graphics: The visuals are nice and crisp, great terrain generation suggesting clean and clear graphics that contribute positively to the overall presentation of the game.

Theme: The game’s connection to the overarching concept is somewhat weak. While it may have some thematic elements, they don’t strongly tie into the main idea or stand out significantly.

Gameplay: While unique, the game suffers from a lack of variety and replayability. With minimal skill requirements, it doesn’t offer much depth or challenge to keep players engaged over time.

Controls: The control scheme is minimal, which isn’t necessarily a problem, but it took several attempts to figure out how to use them and they don’t seem to fit with the style and theme of the game

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Theme: I feel like the link to the fear of number 13 is not fully clear in the description or throughout the game.

Gameplay: I think the difficulty level should be cranked up to make the game even more enjoyable.

Audio: I wished there would have been a bit more atmospheric music to immerse more within the experience, but the sounds effects were really nice.

Innovation: I liked the overall air balloon story which I did not see a lot in VR/games.

Graphics: This is really beautiful and polished, a pleasure to be immersed in.

Controls: Amazingly functional but I wish I could use more the controllers.

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text:
I really liked the idea and I see potential in it, although there are two things I might change. On one hand, the sound when you press the button and go up felt a bit uncomfortable to me, and it would be nice to be able to press and hold to go up, rather than having to press with each jump, as sometimes it was hard to get it to move the way I expected.

Other than that, I enjoyed it a lot, and I think it could be a very fun experience with just a few tweaks.

api.name: Escape from Planet XIII
api.description:
It’s finally your chance to escape this hideous planetary system! You are sick of it’s lush valleys and dense forests! There is no easy way out though. The only reliable escape waypoint is in the middle of the system, through a warphole. To reach it, you need to win races on each planet to be allowed to jump to the next, one closer to the core and your liberty. You can make things happen faster though: after each race you are given the opportunity of skipping one or two planets. For each planet skipped one random element is added that might affect it’s difficulty! You will have to play to see which effects can happen and which ones are troublesome for you and not worth taking or which ones look like a free ticket to a quicker escape.

You have two car options:

  • Easy Rider: With it’s moderated max speed and gripping tires, it might be best suited for your first escape attempts!

  • Tofu Drifter: High speed, low grip, 100% drifting. This is your choice if you want the best adrenaline experience, but you might need to practice to take those corners properly!

It was really fun to make this one! I overcomplicated it for myself making it happen on a sphere surface but in the end I was able to make it work and I think that it gives it a pretty cute look and feel! I hope you enjoy it!

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Driving on the spheres is so cool. I’ve gotten to the rainbow road twice now, and both times like immediately ran right off the edge. If you keep working on this, it would be cool to have a retry option.

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thank you for playing it!! I know the last level is a bit frustrating but I left it like that to add an extra challenge to the game. If I had more time I would have make it so it returns you to the track and give a bit more time for completion to take into account the times you fall. At least on the easy mode. Thanks!!!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Super impressive graphics and tech.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Great concept, solid implementation, adding effects is a neat idea. A lovely racing game.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Nice spherical driving sim, cool stuff.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: If this had controller support I’d play it a lot more. Still enjoyed it a lot though!

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thanks! I left controller support at the bottom of the todo list because I thought it was going to be difficult to tune the analog input but, as the most famous js13k phrase says: I ran out of time.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Funny game, I feel the game needs more items in the road to make it more challenging.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text: Really fun to play and looks really great. The music was fast and matched the speeding around a racetrack feel. I wasn’t sure how the 13 came into it. I enjoyed watching your progress updates on twitter.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: This is one of my favorite games this year! The 3D is impressive, the game is really fun to play, the music is punchy and I love the concept. Congratulations!

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Nice 3D rendering with models, textures, lights etc. And the road on planet is fun to look at, well done!

comments.author: Eoin McGrath
comments.text: Wow! This is visually super impressive! That last level is too tricky, though! Great game.

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
