comments.text: Thank you, I appreciated that you have spend a while figure out what’s happening during the game :) definitely I’ll low the level to figure out

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Congrats on the the submission.

Unfortunately I couldn’t figure out what I was suppose to do. I clicked on cities and my plane flew there, but I’m not sure what I was suppose to be doing at the different cities. I’m not sure what Box is or what it does, nor am I sure what my objective is.

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
A tutorial would come in handy. The game looks like some tycoon simulation, but I am still puzzled at how to achieve them.

After a two-way flight to Stockholm or London the screens turns off, replaced by the message « refueling for onboarding », then the game seems to restart from the beginning.

What about that second plane that moves on its own, is it a competing company ? The UI seems polished, but the gameplay is still a mystery to me.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: It’s a begging of strategy game, using the boxes, fuel and people, and making trades on the main airport. I’ll definitely add a kind of how to play soon :)

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hmm, this feels like there is a great strategy game hidden somewhere, but I had a hard time to find it. I flew around a couple of times could not figure out how to get more fuel or why Bogota had a capacity problem.

It feels like a game I would have liked if I understood it.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: Thank you for the comment, yes, the strategy is behind calculating people, box and fuel. There is trades with # of boxes on the main point!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
I like the presentation (HTML DIVs and CSS with transitions)

It looks like the promising beginning or the core concept of a bigger game!

I really tried to understand the task, but was not able to do anything than flying between airports, then suddenly my plane began to transport passengers to the main airport, then i ran out of trades… The next try, my plane would not fly to the airport because of “checking capacity”

I think, you’ll just have to display better whats going on, and what the current objectives are.

Keep up gamemaking, i think you are on a good way!

Ah, and the HTML includes a stylesheet (src/styles.css) which is missing. But it has no effect. I downloaded your code, and fixed the missing file, but it looked the same :-)

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: Hi, thank you for the appreciation on the game. There are some improvements gamification guide appearing soon in the release and I’ll invite you to play

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I feel somewhat honored when Oslo is in the game. The use of emoji is nice, and the animations of the airports makes it easy to understand we can interact with them.

I did struggle trying to understand what to do. Where am I supposed to go? I want to give this game another try once I know what to do.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: Hi John!, I love Norway and I would love to live there, it’s native people is just amazing despite I just know few. Recently I knew Oslo and was my fav trip till now. About the game, you can make strategy checking how to move between the airports, checkin their red F (fuel) required fuel to arrive. B (box) that you exchange in main airport for fuel, and P (people) to complete the main goal carrying them from airports to the Main. Some of them has more box than people, another’s opposite, at least you need one unit of fuel to move around. The planes around make busy the stations making it a bit difficult to point, you need consider make a itinerary :) best regards

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Very original idea! The graphics leave a bit to be desired in my opinion. With maybe a nice background and some more animations on the plane, the game could have a stronger presence. I’m also not seeing as strong of a connection to this years theme. But all in all, a really fun core idea with some real potential strategy behind it!

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: It’s pretty interesting that the name theme is base of your judgement, despite nobody knows what that heck means. Could you tell me in what I have to made my game to accomplish your brain speciation? F.a

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: The description claims the game to be intuitive, but as a new player I don’t really agree. What are the specific goals for the level? How am I planning my route? The game needs much more handholding to be fun to play.

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: Im agree that the game is not for everyone’s and it takes some times to make you think ;)

api.name: 13 Order
api.description:
Welcome to “13 Order”!

In this number puzzle challenge, your goal is to arrange the numbers on grids of different sizes, from 3x3 up to 13x13 🤯. Each level presents a unique and increasingly complex puzzle that tests your logic and patience.

The real challenge is in the final level: the 13x13 grid! This level is one of the most difficult and will test all your skills. But don’t worry—if you get stuck, you can use the special button to find the solution for you, if one exists.

Are you ready to tackle these puzzles and conquer the final board?

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Very clean, maybe a little bit basic. But the presentation was suberb.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: I wondered what might be the real Triskaidekaphobia for quite some time, well now I know! After solving an 8x8 stage, I don’t want to even think of solving a 13x13 one! Jokes aside, this is a really polished and well made mobile game. Perhaps you could squeeze a nice and calm music track in there as well to help with player’s patience.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
A clean implementation of these classic puzzle games. Nothing to crit regarding the presentation or the controls, maybe it would be nice to have some music playing?

Only some suggestions: maybe (to spice it up) add a ton of graphical effects to it? Like making the pieces wobbling while moving, or add dust as movement effect, or “crash” them into the new place? Just to make the simple movement from A to B interesting to watch.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Very nice submission. I think the UI is really solid. It’s not my kind of game at all, but I was still able to finish the smallest level. I could totally imagine this becoming a popular mobile game with people that like this sort of patience games. The 13x13 board definitely causes me triskaidekaphobia…

Apart from not making the best use of the screen on desktop, the game seems to scale weirdly on mobile too, showing big margins all around. I would try to improve the responsiveness of the UI if you decide to continue working on the game.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Good game! I’m not sure how the game is linked to triskaidekaphobia.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An enjoyable puzzle game of shifting numbers.

You did a great job, worked well on mobile and desktop.

I appreciated being able to click the end of a row to move it instead of each individual tile - ie like a real slide puzzle.

Fun to watch the game solve the board, with them all shuffling around.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Cool graphics, reminds me old game I played whan I was a kid. Some challenges was undoable due to randomness. The algorithm should be improved to shuffle pieces, it was a bit frustrating to almostcomplete a level and discover it was not possible the finish it.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice graphics and gameplay really enjoyed it loved how u can move not only one but entier selection in a row/col, sound is good but such game need more music, the animation is well worked on. the > for some reason not always solve the puzzle which confuses me if it solver or something else, if solved it say it solved which is funny. for a mobile game this will work really well. the link for theme is weak and for audio you are missing a music track try to cosider these to improve.

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: Nice. Very smooth and polished implementation, with a hint button! These are the types of games I find while browsing the internet on a long train ride and I spend the whole time trying to figure it out.
games: XIII Days To Save The Kingdom

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: This is really the type of game I am really bad at, so level 13 is definitely scary to me. The gameplay is nothing new, but it does the job. The controls work well. The sound effects are good but a background music would have been great too. No complaint about the graphics neither, it is pleasant to look at. Good job!

api.name: Salvadoran Reclamation: MS-13
api.description:
MS-13 Gang Members roam the streets of El Salvador, terrorizing the people and committing various crimes without consequence.

Under President Nayib Bukele’s command, you’re tasked with taking out the MS-13 gangs scattered throughout the country.

Controls

  • WASD or Arrow Keys to Move
  • Mouse to aim, hold Left mouse button to attack
  • Shift to dash
  • E to pick up ammo and/or weapons from defeated enemies
  • 1, 2, 3 to switch between weapons
  • Right-click to quickly switch to and strike with your meelee weapon

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Sounds and plays well, lots of weapon variety, I like to play these games like Rambo, not much of a tactical player, so I always fail the prisoner mission!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
I enjoyed the presentation.

Maybe the gameloop felt too repetitive after three levels. I would had love more random weapons or different enemies.

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: Very cool game I enjoyed the combat system also the ui is very good.It would be very nice for the line pointer of the gun to be green or red like a laser.

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text: Nice shooting and satisfying combat. I like that I can take weapons from enemies. All those animations, weapons and UI in 13kb… Amazing work.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
I reached the last level, but I still can’t win, it’s possible, but it requires much more time and patience :).

I enjoyed shooting different weapons as well as the training level. I also like the story itself, at some point I started to think that I am Tom Cruise :). I also liked the slow down effect at the end of the level - looks cool!

I guess I’d like to be able to change weapons so that mine doesn’t disappear so I can change back. As a possible improvement - reloading weapons.

I noticed several bugs: if you move the mouse to the black frame, the shooting not stops and you need to move the sight to stop shooting (mouseout will probably fix it as additional listener to mouseup). Also the AI ​​shoots before I see it on the map, I guess if I shoot at a distance that I can’t see and the AI ​​is there I can kill it, it would be good to limit the hit area to the visible part of the screen. I also noticed that I can melee through walls, not sure if it’s a bug or a feature :).

As for controls, I’m assuming you’re using e.key to find the key. In this case, if my OS language is not English, then this code will not work for me. I recommend using e.code instead to avoid this issue.

Anyway, this is a very nice game, I enjoyed it (except the last level, too hard for me :)), congrats!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Awesome. Strong entry, with lots of potential. The game is tough though, especially when enemies get up close. The melee weapons push baddies away, but I wish it did a little more damage. Overall, this is a pretty polished entry. Thank you for sharing your progress on Slack and Twitter over the competition, it was fun to see it all come together. Congrats!

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to do anything the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: The game progresses nicely, and it gets more and more difficult over time. I’m impressed with the arsenal of weapons you’ve added.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Interesting entry!

I liked the weapons variation and the fact my progress is persisted so I don’t need to start from scratch after a few days.

Sometimes I got the character locked on moving left or right, or the controls would freeze shooting, or not shooting at all. Nothing that really impacts playing as the level restarts with the same ammo as far as I could see.

Fun game! Great job!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Despite the fact some dudes seem to be bullet sponges, it’s a very well made game :)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun game, although quite graphic so not one for kids.

The tutorial was good.

I liked the graphics style. The sound effects were also well done.

The WASD mouse combination worked well. Although it would be good if you picked up the weapons or ammo just by walking over the defeated enemies.

Perhaps not switching to a picked up weapon would be good, unless it was better than the one currently in use?

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: The game is pretty cool. Reminds me of old flash games. The character animations are very well executed. The sound effects are great, but i’m missing some music. These mofos are way too sturdy though, and can withstand several headshots. The AI seems pretty well implemented too. Good one. The theme fit is a stretch.

api.name: The King's Crowns
api.description:
After centuries of reverence and grave robbing, By bandits, emperors and pharao’s alike, The Tomb of Alexander the Great was destroyed, Presumed lost for ever.

Unbeknownst to all, the body of the King was re-taken, To be interred in his home country. Now, there in the ancient mountains of Macedonia, Alexander rests for ever, in the Valley of the King.

But even now his tomb is not safe, not safe from you! After years of travel and research, You have located the Valley of the King, Where now you must solve the final riddle;

    The King's Crowns

    13 crowns King Alexander possessed
    13 crowns by the Devil oppressed
    13 crowns for Day and Night
    13 crowns that show the King’s might
    13 crowns with a cursed spell
    13 crowns that lead to Hell!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Incredible graphics and ambiance

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks for your feedback! I did definitely try to find out how far I could take this in the constraints (including my own skills of course)

comments.author: Luke Cann
comments.text: Great visuals though i couldn’t tell which crowns were the devils

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Thanks for playing!

I was / am sort of worried if I made that part of the puzzle not too hard to figure out. Without giving it away, perhaps a small hint; you are right, you can’t tell which crowns are the Devil’s, but you can definitely find the crowns which are not from the Devil :-)

comments.author: hybr85
comments.text: Very nice looking! Fun ending. Took me a bit to figure out how to switch day and night. Collisions would be better if they slid around obstacles, especially on desktop.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Thanks for your feedback!

The collisions were somewhere on my ToDo list, but I never got around to it.

The switching sounds familiar. Even I had sometimes problems finding the gate during play testing. That is why I added the sky beam on the gate because I was afraid people would miss the day/night switch all together.

Thanks for playing to the end! :-)

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: The gameplay seems to rely heavily on trial-and-errors, which feels quite punitive. The landscape is very nicely detailed, but the thumb-stick motion caused me instant nausea (because the view changes rapidly which contradicts my body feeling no motion). Nice job extending the default hand models ;p

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Jerome,

Thanks for playing!

Like I mentioned in one of the other comments, it is not supposed to be trial-and-error, but you definitely need to figure out the trick to finding the right crowns. I was a bit afraid people would miss the significance of the day/night switching. I hope you did figure it out in the end, otherwise you have experienced hell quite literally :-P

Getting teleport to working was just to much work in the time and space available. I’ve done that before so it would definitely be something to put in. I don’t get nauseated myself in VR otherwise I would probably have started with teleportation :-)

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This game looks amazing. But I felt completely lost. How can I distinguish the crowns? They all look the same to me.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi João,

I feared that people would have that problem :-(

If you walk around the ‘good’ crowns are very obviously different (unless you are color blind, but even then there is a clear difference). So just walk around until you spot a different crown.

But even then, you can only safely get the crowns at the right time of day. Go to the sky beam to change the time of day.

Good luck, and thanks for playing!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really nice game, love the changing of night to day and back by walking through the portal. Had fun running about swinging those arms as if I was running!

Lots of effort has gone into the environment!

I was stumped on what to do with the crowns, I collected a few in the day and died, collected a few in the night and died, I did enjoy the environment but never got more than 2 crowns after 15 minutes.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Ryan, thanks for playing, and thanks for you kind remarks!

I love your remark about swinging your arms. A while back I played Happy Run VR, which was definitely on my mind when creating The King’s Crowns!

As I gather from the other comments, you are not alone in not figuring out the puzzle.

The tricky thing is there a 26 crowns, of which only 6 or 7 (later fewer) are highlighted at any time so you already have to really search for them. But the ones that you do find you can’t grab because it is the wrong time of day, so you have to remember where they are for after the day/night switch.

Even when you do everything right, finding those last one or two crowns usually takes me a while too :-)

I hope you’ll give it another try!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Collected all the crowns! In fact, when there were no daytime crowns left, I took a big risk as to which crowns to collect next, but I made a guess and it turned out to be true - I felt really adventurous :).

Although I was actually a bit confused until I read your color tip and that’s when it started.

I played on Desktop, not through WebXR, but the world was great anyway! Share a few words about how you did it :).

The noise was also nice, more like the sound of water than wind, but it was like there was no water :) but after a while I wanted to turn it off, so it would be nice to have that option.

Also, I would like to have more matching of the theme, since the theme is the fear of the number 13, and here you have to collect 13 crowns and then you win, so it is the exact opposite.

I think it would be even more interesting if there were traps throughout the quest to maintain interest, but I understand that there is so much! It’s really impressive to fit the whole world into such a small size.

By the way, when I was looking for crowns, I had to remember the path to them, that is, orient myself in the world :), and also when there were several crowns close to each other, I tried to remember the approximate path to them, and then I oriented myself to flowers, trees, mountains , it was really interesting!

Very cool! It was fun and interesting for me! Congratulations!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
I had the theme in my head as having to evade the 13 crowns of the devil, but that left the game without a goal, so in writing up the story it became more the finding of the 13 crowns of the king.

I’m glad you figured out the ‘puzzle’. From the other comments, not getting the trick seems to have been a common problem among the players. The way you describe it is exactly how I wanted it to work, including having to retrace your steps since things look different at day or night, and a bit of risk once you have only two or one crowns to go!

Good catch on the wind/water sound. I recycled that from my entry from last year, where it was water, now with some extra filtering to get it ‘windy’.

You’re not the first to ask about a write-up, so I’m currently working on one. But that will take some time, there is baked shadows and lighting, generated meshes and textures, shaders, controllers, etc.

Thanks for playing the game to the end! (I played yours to the end as well, but I’m sure that took me less time :-) )

comments.author: A Doucheboard Warrior
comments.text: I like the graphics of the game and the kind of way 3D, however I’m a bit lost how to play it and which are the goals

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Juan,

You’re definitely not the only one. I had hoped the sky beam to find the gate and the different colored crowns were enough, but on the other hand I also wanted to see how big I could make the level, so some players just missed the differently colored crowns and / or the time gate, and without both you have no way of finishing the game.

In short: Find the 13 crowns of the king while avoiding the 13 crowns of the devil. Find the King’s Day Crowns at night, but grab them by day and vice versa.

Good luck!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
The environment is outstanding - there are so many details! The animated hand, the vegetation moving with the wind; the day-night cycle - it’s really really really good.

I honestly wish there were no crowns to take (I really don’t get which ones I should pick and which ones I shouldn’t), but just focus on pure exploration. Despite this, your asset generation and scene building are amazing!

comments.author: NoNiMad
comments.text:
Nice game! I got confused at first, like others it seems, but reading a few comment got me started and once I understood the rules it was an enjoyable search with the ambience and the chill graphics.

I feel like the same concept with different maps and more replayability would make a really cool twist on the memory puzzle kind of game! :)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game got a bit spooky when it got dark.

I got told off for trying to pick up a crown, and immediately burnt!

The graphics are impressive and the levels expansive and fun to explore.

Not entirely sure what I was supposed to be doing. But it was really impressive nonetheless.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Jasper,

The goal not being clear seems to be the consensus :-(

See my remarks below for some hints.

Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Akbar Sultanov
comments.text:
Theme: The game effectively captures this year’s theme, featuring 13 crowns and the fear of selecting the wrong ones.

Gameplay: The gameplay is very good, showcasing custom hands and a variety of scene objects. The music and grass movement create a special atmosphere. However, the main objective can be unclear at first, so additional instructions or hints at the beginning would benefit new players. Since it is an exploratory game, incorporating teleportation-based movement could enhance navigation within the scene.

Audio: The audio is excellent and adds to the overall experience.

Innovation: The game creatively utilizes day/night cycles and the search for crowns, which is a nice touch.

Graphics: The graphics are very nice—no comments here.

Controls: The controls are easy to use, with no issues or additional comments needed.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Akbar,

Teleport was definitely on my “if I had more time / bytes left” list.

I already added the sky beam on the gate because I expected people to have a bit of trouble on the goals, but for the other comments that was clearly not enough.

Thanks for Playing

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Impressive graphics, but couldn’t figure the gameplay. Can’t figure which cronws are which. Maybe they should have more distinct look than just the hue?

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Alexander,

Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Theme: Love the idea of the 13 crowns riddle but I wonder where is the fear coming from in this case?

Gameplay: This really feels like a complete game with all the various interactions, environments, audio and even moving grass!!! A tutorial would be nice to help guiding new users.

Audio: Amazing experience.

Innovation: I love the “twin worlds” offered by the time of the day.

Graphics: This is really good what you did for a 13k demo.

Controls: Intuitive and reactive.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Thanks for playing and voting!

It’s clearly not fear in the horror sense of the word, since as long as you don’t interact with the crowns you are safe. But I did definitely get a bit anxious when I had all but one crown but wasn’t sure if I had retraced my steps to the right one :-)

comments.author: Kieran Farr
comments.text:
Graphic: Great graphics and immersion feeling for 13k! Very impressed with day night contrast and adaptation of the environment between each. Nice glowing and extra effects around the crown and devil death mode. Nice natural elements like waving grass, terrain coloring varying by elevation, etc.

Audio: Nice ambient sfx to set the mood

Gameplay: Unsure what to do at first, didn’t understand why i couldn’t choose a crown or significance of day / night;

Innovation: Impressed with large area and high quality graphics under 13k! Impressed with day/night mode too!

Controls: Basic VR controls with joystick movement that is not always great for all users, but bonus points for a very polished desktop mode!

Theme: Seemed pretty on track with core theme – integrating 13 into the plot and adding some medieval spooky mystery.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Thanks for your kind remarks.

Your remark on gameplay seems to capture the consensus. Still not sure myself if I would want to improve on that. Figuring out of the mechanics is half the fun, but on the other hand, it looked like some players quit before they figured it out, so perhaps I didn’t hit the optimal curve there.

Thanks for playing and voting!

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text: The visual aspect of the game is very well done, and the changes between day and night are also very well achieved. I might consider changing the arm model so that the entire arm doesn’t rotate when moving the hand, or maybe just showing the hands without the forearm. Another thing I would change is the control system, since even when using the left joystick, the rotations make me feel dizzy. Trying to make it smoother, or maybe rotating 90º instantly could help. Great job!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Hi Fernando,

Thanks for playing and for voting.

I added the arms because especially on desktop the floating hand was a bit jarring. I couldn’t make up my mind about the arm model in VR so I left them in, but in the end forgot that I still had to make that decision :-) In VR, they are a bit silly, but still, I kind of like the arms over the floating hands. Clearly something that could be improved upon.

Teleport & snapturn was on my list, but I ran out of space and time.

Thanks again!

api.name: It adds up
api.description:
A game about collecting numbers, creating good hands, handling basic math operators, shop for upgrades and get big combos while avoiding the number 13. “It’s like math but somehow even more boring” (actual quote from my beta testing kids).

Controls

Mouse to steer your square. Left click to activate the shield, left clicking again while it’s active will deactivate the shield. The shield recharges when not in use. Colliding with a number while the shield is active will remove that number from the board, effectively allowing you to discard that number and hope that a better one appears in it’s place.

Your number of lives are indicated by the blue circles in the corner of your square. Four dots means four lives, three dots means three lives and so on. 0 lives means game over.

Playing the game

Each hand consists of collecting four number. How those numbers are treated depends on the randomised mission of the level. Beware though! If at any point you add up to 13 you will loose a life. For this mission it could be to pick up the actual number 13 or if A+B or A+B+C equals 13. Different missions have slightly different ways to reach 13 so be aware.

The mission AB > CD combines the numbers in a slightly different way. If A is 12 and B is 4 AB is 124.

After a hand is completed a score is generated and a new mission is generated.

Score

If you complete a mission you will be scored on the base numbers involved. Exactly how depends on the mission. If you have the mission A + B + C + D your base score is simple A+B+C+D. Your base score can then be multiplied through a few factors like time, numbers have same colour and the poker quality of the hand (pair, straight, three of a kind etc)

Shop

Every third completed hand the shop will activate. Here you can alter the base rules of the game, increase score multipliers for certain hands, change likelihood for colours and number and increase your life (at a cost) etc.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text:
High score: 131565, Best combo: 18900

This is pretty fun! Requires lots of juggling, especially late in the game when the screen starts filling up. Excellent interpretation of the theme, and you instilled a true fear of “13”. Fantastic music and sound effects as well. Tell your kids that I didn’t think it was boring at all! Nice job 😀

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Thanks for playing and taking the time for feedback! I’ll let my kids know 😅

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Very interesting game. Keeps you on your toes as you try to figure out what numbers to use for the given mission. Being able to remove numbers from the screen (like a host of 13s) is very helpful. Music was nice too.

Loved the quote from your kids.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Thanks for taking the time to give some feedback!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The game seems fun and i love the visuals and the music, but the game is surprisingly heavy for such simple graphics. It quickly became unplayable after just a minute of game :( Works a little better in Chrome than Firefox, but my computer still struggled.

I’m not sure I understand the missions, and the feedback i get from the game isn’t clear whether i’m doing it right or wrong.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Thanks for giving feedback! I failed to test my game on other machines than my own. Too bad because I have a feeling that the things causing poor performance is the effects layers that are not really part of the actual game and should be quickly fixable (either by optimising them or simply removing them I guess).

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game looks great, the music is also really good.

I have to be honest, the info panel has a lot of rules and aims, so the overall experience was a little overwhelming.

I ended up pretty much just hovering the cursor box over all the boxes which appeared in no particular order and without regard to what the sum was doing and I seemed to always progress with my score continually going up.

I didn’t end up using the shield, and when the shop list appeared I wasn’t sure which if any would be to my advantage?

If the dots in the corners of the square indicate health, why did the squares with numbers in I was collecting also have a dot in the corner sometimes?

As the game went on it appeared to become more laggy?

I enjoyed playing, will have to give it to my kids to try out.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text:
Hey and thanks for playing!

I didn’t nail the whole explaining the game mechanics and the various moving parts at all, ran out of time for that sadly.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: The music is incredible, well done! I liked the concept that matches very well the theme. I think a tutorial mode would be nice to help the player really understand the rules.

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Thanks for playing! I agree that a tutorial would have been helpful, ran out of time unfortunately. Typical gamejam I suppose 😅

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
How is this so much fun!!

Music is great, there are enough numbers that pop up so you don’t feel like you have to wait to long. The graphics are just on point for such a game.

No really, overall, not much to say, very well mastered!

comments.author: cykelkatalogen
comments.text: Thanks for you kind words and for taking time to give feedback!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: the music is cool but can lower the intensity a bit, graphics are awsome and op animation is cool. the game itself is cool took me offgaurd when everthing switch to 13 suddenly, having the equation switch get bit confusing to focus on squares

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
My result is 28004. The best combo is 5737.

From the first time I did not immediately understand what was happening and did not even know that I could activate the protective field, but then I read the description of the game and learned a lot. So at some point I could play almost forever :).

Although I haven’t fully figured out the points, I like being able to make poker-like combinations. Also at some point I realized that my eyes were just wandering all over the screen looking for 13 :).

It was interesting to remember a little math.

Another thing I would like to see is the ability to turn music on/off. Although the music is pretty playable, it would be nice to have that as an option. And also have a description of the game in the game itself, players do not always read the description of the game, and when it is in the game, then you relieve yourself of responsibility ;).

Maybe a moving background (stars) would add even more coolness :).

It was fun, congratulations!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Clever game mechanics! It might be hard to do in this kind of constrained competition, but some kind of tutorial where the player gradually learns the various mechanics of the game would greatly help new players get into the game. Good job nonetheless!

api.name: 13s Tactics
api.description:
13s Tactics, is a tactical/chess/puzzle game where you need to perform all your actions in 13 seconds before your enemies.

You will fear the number 13! Not just because you will run out of time but you will loose your characters after your turn!

And of course this game contains 13 levels!

Game instructions

General

  • Game board can be dragged around for a better interaction/position.
  • Game board position can be reset with the bottom right Reset Board Position button.
  • Click on the zoom +/- buttons to increase/reduce the game board size.
  • Click on the top right Reset level button to reset the level at any time.
  • Clicking the speaker button will mute the game sounds.
  • Clicking on the left character will select that character on the board and vice versa.
  • A level is completed when all enemies or player characters are eliminated.

Breaktime Turn

  • Player can select characters to understand their movement pattern.
  • When ready, the player can start the turn by clicking on the Start player turn button.

Player Turn

  • Perform as many/needed actions in 13 seconds.
  • After 13 seconds your turn finishes.

Enemy Turn

  • Enemies also have 13 seconds to perform their actions.
  • After 13 seconds their turn ends and we go back into Breaktime.

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Great speed-chess-ish gameplay and pretty sprites. The different movement patterns of units and the layout of the levels are simple but effective ways to have a lot of variation. Very clever and fun!

comments.author: Lucas Drummond
comments.text: Graphics and gameplay are very clever and well executed! The 13 second time-limit in the gameplay feels a bit arbitrary / makes it a challenge to click in the right spots quickly enough, but great premise.

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: The game looks good and I like the gameplay mechanics. But I don’t really like to be hurried like this in a strategy game. I sometimes would lose just because the click didn’t register. I think limiting the number of moves would work better than a time limit. The puzzles were quite good and thought provoking.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: The concept is nice and the graphics and sound are good

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: Good use of the theme as a game mechanic. The audio goes well with the pixelart style. The puzzles difficulty is well balanced. The controls are a bit difficult in a time crunch, particularly to chain quick motion. I wish I could use arrow keys instead of clicks.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: This is really polished overall, smooth controls and nice graphics. I got to lvl 8, and then the turn-time vs difficulty balance got to be a bit much for me

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Great entry, it took me a while to understand that it wasnt turn based by time based.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: The concept is very innovative! The time limit really creates a tense feeling of fear. The game design is spot on, gradually increasing in difficulty, and several levels have those ‘Ah ha’ moments that make solving them extremely satisfying. This is my favorite entry in the competition so far!! Just one thing that there’s a bug I encountered on level 13 where the tiles disappeared for some reason, and I saw a error in the console. But aside from that, this game is truly outstanding! Good job!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Very unusual gameplay. Not sure if time limit and map scrolling were necessary.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Game completed! Level 13 was a perfectly balanced challenge, great job! A clever and unique set of game mechanics. I enjoyed the pixel art, the music, and sound effects too. It’s tricky to mix tactics games with real world timer. It would be interesting to try a version of the game that changes “13 seconds” to “13 moves” to take away the time pressure. But this is great, really impressive!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Reached level 10!

Among the possible improvements, I would like to highlight the possibility of prematurely ending the move, so as not to wait for the entire timer, or its acceleration.

Nice game, congratulations!

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Very fun and creative game.

Awesome job.

The thirteen seconds really makes you think fast.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Loved the art style. The chess movement played well, and the addition of the shield carrier made for some smart puzzles that dealt with positioning and facing. Being able to fully wall off units to reserve for later turns to provoke enemies made for some interesting puzzles.

Kinda difficult to play on a trackpad. Level 10 especially was very difficult to complete within the 13s time limit and was making my hand tired. Most of the puzzles involve setting up everything perfectly on the first turn within the 13s otherwise you’d lose, which meant that the enemy tactics didn’t really matter and waiting their 13s turn felt long (especially when they would do nothing).

Gave up at level 11 as I couldn’t figure out how to get the enemy to move onto white spaces so my bishop could take them.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Not personally a huge fan of war games but this was an interesting puzzle nonetheless! Well executed with solid graphics and nice background music and sound effects.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
Simple, gorgeous, interesting and provides a bit of challenge.

I wish there were more levels!

I enjoy the thinking time and then the stress of scrambling all my units to the right places.

Great concept!

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: Ohh this is pretty smart! It’s kind of click chess meets Advanced Wars. Guide your soldier/s around the grid, making sure they’re in a good position to defeat the baddies (i.e. hit those carrying shields from behind) and then whack them. But try and do it within 13 seconds, before it swaps to the AIs turn. The movement patterns change between the levels, the soldier types change and you start to be able to control more than one of them, or have to take down multiple opponents. Graphically it’s lovely, the music is simple but haunting and you really have to think about this the further you get. Great use of the theme too.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun turn based game.

The music works well.

I liked the graphics and isometric levels.

The first time I played it I didn’t realise you could click on the far green points to move all that distance so each press I was only moving one square at a time and kept failing.

The diagonal moves were tricky, I couldn’t get in a position where I could attack.

Overall a good game for which I need more practice.

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Great action puzzle game, the upper levels really require you to take time and prepare your moves. 13s may not be enough to perform all moves so you may have to split in two turns, sacrifice pieces for the victory.

Very well thought out and executed, with the pause at the beginning, the ability to restart a level, clear 3D iso gfx, and audio to match. A masterpiece.

api.name: Wendol Village
api.description:
Gather resources and protect your peaceful village against the invading heroes. Thirteen famous heroes will try to raid your village.

Build defenses, raise an army, and use the power of your gods to send the invaders back home in their longboats.

Beware the thirteenth warrior!

RTS (real-time strategy) game with mouse or touch controls. Uses a modified version of the littleJS engine.

Controls

  • Left mouse or touch - select unit or give command to selected unit
  • Drag left mouse or touch - box to select multiple units
  • Right mouse - scroll map and deselect all units
  • Minimap - left click or touch to scroll map

Buildings

  1. House - cost 6 wood, 4 stone. Supports 2 population
  2. Farm - cost 6 wood, 4 stone. Grows 50 food that can be collected
  3. Wall - cost 2 wood, 1 stone. Protective barrier
  4. Barracks - cost 6 wood, 4 stone. Put workers inside to train them. Archers can shoot further while on top
  5. Town Hall - for training workers and storing resources, lose it and lose the game. Supports 3 population.
  6. Temple - friendly units can worship you here to gain mana. Mana is volatile so you can only store up to 25 of it.

God Powers

  1. Create Food - cost 5 mana, creates 10 food
  2. Healing - cost 8 mana, heals 2 points of damage on friends and buildings
  3. Poison - cost 10 mana, damages all enemies for 1 point
  4. Smite - cost 20 mana, damages all enemies for 2 to 4 points

Resources

(click worker, click resource)

  1. Wood - can be collected from trees.
  2. Stone - can be collected from stone found on map
  3. Food - collected from farms
  4. Mana - created by praying followers in the temple

Units

  1. Worker - 3 hp, collects resources and builds. Cannot fight
  2. Archer - 3 hp, shoots arrows at enemies. Can shoot further on top of barracks tower
  3. Spearman - 6 hp, tough guys who can hold their own in melee combat

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Very pretty and polished, with the full set of RTS features! The controls and interactions all feel “right”, and the audio is great. I love the little “uh“s and “oh“s of the workers and fighters! Really great submission.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Really great little RTS game. Great graphics and sounds.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Luke Nickerson
comments.text: This was an amazing RTS to fit into 13kb! The graphics and animations were great. I thoroughly enjoyed my time with this, and would play more if the game was expanded. The ramp-up of difficulty for the 13th wave was an unpleasant shock, but I guess that fits with the theme. Only criticisms: I couldn’t build walls for some reason, and the right-click movement was awkward.

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: Thanks for playing and reviewing. The wall graphic got corrupted so it is invisible. Hopefully I will be allowed to patch it soon.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Impressive game. Controlling the workers is okay, but when battles become intense it always gets very chaotic to me. I think it is because of the current control scheme - there is drag selection of multiple units but only if there aren’t already selected units at that moment, so the behavior change of left-click is not very intuitive. Deselecting all units by clicking one of them so I can select something else just don’t work. Right-click is used to deselect but it also moves the map which is confusing. The drag selection not working if you start from an occupied tile (trees, houses, etc.) adds to the confusion even more. Nevertheless, I’m hooked! I will try again when you patch the walls.

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. The corrupted wall graphic is patched so its back in the game. The arrows are now missing, but that was my fault so they will remain missing. You can hear them but you can’t see them.

comments.author: Jesper Rasmussen
comments.text:
What an amazing RTS game.

So impressive how many mechanics you included.

Love it.

Takes a few attempts to get a good strategy going.

And my, the last wave is hard.

Next time I will win :D

comments.author: Asiel Díaz Benítez
comments.text:
in mobile/touchscreen it is not easy to move the screen/camera, only via minimap which is too tricky for its size

the source code doesn’t include a license, is it open source? nice game!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I defeated the heroes! It took a few playthroughs before I found the the right balance and rhythm. Bosses 1-3 were mostly a matter of having the engine up and running. Bosses 4+ were all about trying to make the engine bigger. Other players’ comments about the 13th boss left me somewhat intimidated, so I converted all of my workers to warriors for the last boss, and thankfully it all worked out. The one thing I was not able to figure out is the Temple / worship / mana system. I tried having units pray to the alter, but I couldn’t find the god power menu. Despite that, this is still an insane amount of content for a js13k entry! I have seen several attempts at RTS games in js13k over the years, and I feel like this is the first one that really pulled it off and sucked me in. Excellent graphics and sound effect choices. Very impressive, well done!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I defeated the Heroes! The first level, and the last level was a lot more difficult than the other levels. After knowing how to pray, it become easier. Great music and sound effects, especially the voices of the farmers. Reminded me about Command & Conquer.

I understand the game has support for mobile controls, which makes it slightly different from regular RTS games, but I think it worked ok. A lot of content in 13kB.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: There’s a lot going on at once at times. That’s a good thing. I just wished that when playing on mobile that the jumping around the map had a on touchmove event listener too so that I didn’t have to constantly tap.

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text: Wow that’s really good! I could easily loose hours in this game! And not just because I really suck at RTS ;) Really impressive that you got all of this in just 13k!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
Having a full RTS going in 13k is amazing! I loved the pixel graphics and just the whole simple RTS game working very well and give the resource-management experience.

The right mouse button should perform actions and left mouse to select StarCraft, WarCraft-style would have been better because I found myself selecting another unit accidentally or moving somewhere near the unit I was trying to select. It got a bit frustrating.

Otherwise, overall an amazing game and experience to be had in this competition.

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text:
Outstanding work, I love this game! First of all the music is so good, my favorite so far this year. The pixel art graphics look amazing with a ton of detail and charm. Sound effects are great too and the speech works really well for the grunts. The gameplay is really solid RTS and with great game feel. It was easy for me to figure out what to do from playing and felt fun all around. This is the best RTS I ever remember seeing in JS13K.

So after a few games, I still want to come try playing again but here’s a few suggestions for improvements…. There are some issues with the way controls work for example you have combined unit select with command, and scroll map with deselect. The good news, is you don’t need to reinvent the wheel, just copy the way the buttons work straight out of warcraft. Another problem is the right click drag select doesn’t work if you already have a unit selected, things like that are modal can make controls confusing. Right click could be more like a click and drag the screen around, I can help you hook it up. Also, it is easy to loose track of your own units in the trees, not sure what to do but it happened to me.

Overall amazing game, I am beyond impressed! This is one of the first entries that I will be coming back to after voting to give it another go.

comments.author: Richard Chung
comments.text:
The game looks really great but I really struggle at the starting stage as I don’t know what to build at first I suck at this game wahhhh.

But overall the archers and spearman sprites are really good. The map is pretty well-designed and the enemy ship looks really cool,

The audio from the workers are really funny.

Having a bit catchy BGM would be really nice. Would definitely come back for the post-compo.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game looked really good

The pixel graphics looked ace. I tried it on both mobile and desktop and for me it plays better on desktop

The speech was a nice touch, but came through a little rushed and distorted

The game played a bit too slowly

comments.author: ZZ
comments.text: I should have read the instructions before playing! Took me a while to figure out praying was key :D Great fun. Only feedback would be to give the archers an animation like the swordsman. I had to trust the sound fx that the archers were attacking.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Great RTS game! The artstyle is really nice, and the gameplay is very entertaining

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: the game itself is amazing if you know how it work, i know integrating prolog or hints may consume size but it will add alot of value to game.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text: Very cool town builder! Especially impressed with the amount of freedom to place objects around the map and the extensive UI for a game with such a small file size. Having to click a character in order to open their actions could be a really finicky sometimes, but overall a really fun experience.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This game is so awesome! Incredibly cute and surprisingly complex! I’m seriously impressed with how much you packed into this. The little voices they make were really cute.

I know you were already at the size limit, but i really missed having more labels explaining what each thing did before clicking. Like the prices of things only appear if I can’t pay. And I understand there’s no path finding, but the thing where the got stuck slowly trying to cross obstacles was kind of annoying.Also, I don’t see how this meets the theme.

comments.author: Jonas Olmstead
comments.text: Thanks for the nice feedback! The game is based on “The 13th Warrior”, aka “Eaters of the Dead”. I guess its kind of obscure now. The difficulty builds with each warrior (each of which is from the book) and the 13th is super difficult and the king, Beowulf. Thats how it ties to the theme :-)

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: The little voices for units is a very nice touch. How you fit this into 13k is quite impressive. My biggest complaint is that the stone gather sound is too loud or too high-pitched or something.
games: XIII Days To Save The Kingdom

api.name: O HIII BAD SKATEPARK
api.description:
In this game, you don’t control a skater… You draw the skatepark!

The entry is inspired by Line Rider and named after an annagram of the theme. The goal is to be creative… while keeping the skater on its skate as long as possible. You can draw lines (fixed) and balls (mobile). The balls become blue when the skater touches them. The levels are shareable.

10 challenges are also available for the most fearless players!

Controls:

  • mouse to edit the track
  • keyboard and mouse wheel can also be used to move the camera
  • right click can also be used to erase an item

Decentralized challenges:

  • Arcadia (the game is also uploaded on arcadia.fun)
  • OP Guild (since you only control the level, the skater is the ultimate NPC!)

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Definitely linerider :-) The ghost-feature is great for drawing ramps where they need to be. The music choice (hall of the mountain king) is nice.

Sometimes, when returning to the editor, the game removed one of my lines? Strange…

Regarding the theme… ahem… well… i drew a “13” for the skater at the end of my course, where he had a terrible crash. ;-)

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Line rider + ball physics, awesome! I’m still working on the challenges, but this is fun. Clever use of emoji for the skater. Love that the title is an anagram. Fun, well done!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Using challenges for progression is a neat idea, need to remember to steal it if I ever make a sandbox game :)

“The balls become blue” is a fantastic description fitting many situations in life.

Seriously though, there’s so much potential here! Great job as always :)

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
As always, perfect use of emojis, Xem. Great entry! I did 5 challenges and stuck on 5x rotation with the skate.

I’m pretty bad designing tracks, so it would be cool to have prepared lvls in the game, so I could do one or two edits to help the skater to complete the level and achieve some height or POI.

PS

The balls become blue when the skater touches them. This is the best joke in js13k 2024.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
I loved the idea of challenges. I’m terrible at completing them, but they were interesting to attempt nonetheless. I also liked the ghost feature.

It took me longer than it should have to realize that the line I was drawing was a straight line and not one that followed my mouse. There were also some challenges where I thought I had completed them but the game didn’t think so. For example where the skater can only touch balls and the skate only lines. I drew a line under the skate, then small lines on either side of skate to keep it from moving, then placed a ball so it would hit the skater and bounce away and not hit the skate. Would be nice if there was some way to track which challenge you’re working on and have the game give feedback on what didn’t work (similar to how the balls turn blue when they hit you).

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: Interesting twist on Line Rider. The challenges are a great way to keep the player invested in the game. The Ghost feature is also a great addition. Makes it easier to compose course

comments.author: ckolin
comments.text: Cool little game with fun challenges. Drawing the lines was a bit awkward but I loved the soundtrack and the intro animation, as well as the ghost feature. Also, how did you even come up with that name - you must be really good at scrabble!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Haha, thanks, you can read the genesis of that name in the post-mortem I’m writing here: https://xem.github.io/articles/js13k24.html

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
An interesting concept for a game, definite linerider vibes.

The physics are good and the objects interacted well.

I like the way you’ve taken the theme and broken it apart (literally) to form the new word and take inspiration from a skate park.

It would be cool to be able to control the level you’ve drawn (e.g. to rock it from side to side).

The challenges were fun, some tricky ones in there.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Clever physics, the shot rider is handy for planning out where to put the next line to keep the lil guy skating along.

Being able to control the gravity / speed would have been great!

Good work!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: the physics are really great! I like trying to make little jumps after a steep ramp, then bounce off a ball for xtreme style!!! I kept losing my level creation progress, with lines vanishing, but maybe my levels were too complex! I wanted to share a cool level with a house, air trick, and a pool full of balls lol but I lost half the level on my two separate tries at making a level. this game would be super fun with a tiny bit more editor polish where a map of 100+ lines and balls was possible (and grid snapping for verts so lines were perfectly connected without fuss! that would be cool)!! =) but for 13kb this is so fun and I enjoyed making my skater bail in humorous ways! imagine if you recorded the physics velocity when the skater collides and call that total the “how much damage you took on this run!” stat, for funny fail highscores! very nice work.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: A fun little game! I succeeded with 6 of the challenges, but speaking of the challenges, they overflow the popup in an ugly way for me.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The simplicity of O HIII BAD SKATEPARK works in its favor, giving it an accessible and minimalistic vibe. The clean, white background and basic stick-figure-like character emphasize the focus on gameplay and mechanics, which is a great starting point for players looking for a straightforward experience. The customizable aspect of drawing lines and manipulating the environment adds an interactive and creative element that enhances user engagement. While the minimalist style can work well, the game could benefit from a bit more visual variety or flair to keep players visually engaged. Even small touches like adding color accents or dynamic animations could make the world feel more lively without detracting from the clean aesthetic. Adding textures or patterns to the background or elements on screen might help break the monotony and provide a better sense of immersion. The creative freedom of drawing and customizing the skatepark is a brilliant idea, and the simplicity leaves room for expansion. It’s great to see a game that encourages users to experiment with the environment. Keep building on the strong interactive element, and with a few visual tweaks, your game could offer both a creative and visually dynamic experience!

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text: Well done, you followed my advices to make it a fun game rather than just a demo :p The quality and accuracy of the physics are pretty amazing. The game could use a bit of graphical polish, but as it stands, it does the job and is really fun!

api.name: 13th Floor
api.description:
13th Floor is a stealth horror game requiring you to stay vigilant and stay in the shadows.

You receive a strange invitation to a party celebrating the 13th JS13k gaming competition. The party is on the 13th floor where there are 13 rooms, what could go wrong?

Instructions

You arrive at the 13th floor with a Key. Go to the room number you have the key for. Inside that room, another key will be hidden. Find it and go to the next room and repeat until you find the key to Room 1313.

Use your Flashlight (F) to find the rooms and search for items.

Some rooms will start unlocked. They have Health Packs hidden in them. Use them to restore health.

When It arrives, don’t let It see you. You can see It coming by watching for It’s light.

If It sees you, run and hide.

Controls

  • W, A, S, D: Move
  • Mouse: Look / Turn
  • E / Left Click: Action
  • F: Flashlight

comments.author: Feenposhleen
comments.text: Impressive on a technical level, and scary on a psychological one. Lighting effects are great, and it really has that scary ambience of horror games like Phasmophobia. It gets a bit repetitive after a while though, and the AI of “It” is a bit jank at times. I got stuck when “It” was just moving back and forth outside the room I was hiding in with very little HP.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Hey, thanks for giving the game a try and for the kind words! Regarding the repetition, I see your point, with 13kb though, I think almost every game is a bit repetitive, only so much I can do :). Regarding the AI, ya, with 13kb the creature can only be so smart, sometimes that can happen. If you’re willing to give it another go, there are rooms with health packs you can use to refill your health, and while they are semi randomly placed, they tend to be in the center hallway due to the algorithm used to spread out keys. If you grab one or two of those, you should be able to make it to the end easily. You were probably close as there are 8 keys to grab to the end.

Either way, really appreciate you taking the time to check out the game and leave feedback, good luck in the competition!

Thanks, Rob

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Nice. Love the technology! It’s not great on my potato laptop but very cool.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Amazing game. Love the shadows and how you manage to cramp all that geometry in 13KBs. Very inspiring. I really got nervous playing it!!.

My only recomendation would had been to render all geometry twice but scaling by (1,1,-1) and making the elevator floor alpha 0.5. That would had given and extra shininess. :D

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Amazing game!

I wonder if i will fall asleep tonight… the goosebumps are only slowly going away…

Very good gfx, and sfx.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Amazing work, as always. This is a technical masterpiece. Great lighting, great textures, great level design, intricate details. Fantastic 👍

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Incredible graphics and ambiance. “it” made me chilly!

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: I was already panicking just from reading your game’s description! I had to confront it during the first play-through to be able to continue the game with a decent heartbeat, so I could rate it objectively!!!

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Awesome entry, lot of different stuff in here. Special mention to the 3d engine.

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: Amazing!!! I love all of it the sound graphics, the horror theme, and the lightning behind the NPC are a nice touch, very well done! And the fact that all of these fit in a 13kb it’s insane. GG

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
The amount of details is insane – the shower curtains, the bathroom tiling, the pillows, the wallpaper! Combined with lighting, this is Left4Dead-level of graphics :) An amazing achievement.

I’m usually not a fan of horror games (nor movies), but I enjoyed the thrill and the mystery. At some point It started chasing right after me and I got goosebumps when I saw a shadow on the wall in front of me. Such a great effect.

I was getting a bit lost running through the corridors, so the plan for my next playthrough is to bring pen and paper and map the floor. Unless the room numbers are procedural?

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Yes! I’m so happy you said that about the shadow on the wall in front of you! That was one of the visions I had of the game in my mind at the very start, but it can of course only happen in certain circumstances, so very happy to hear someone experienced that!

But ya please give it another shot, there is a fun surprise at the end! And don’t forget about the health packs. You should be able to make it to the end pretty easily with one or two health packs.

The room numbers aren’t procedural, only the placement of the keys. Facing out of the elevator at the start, the layout is: Left hallway has rooms 1-3, center hallway has rooms on each side and so has rooms 4 - 9, right hallway has rooms 10-12. This image from my post mortem explaining pathfinding has an overhead view you can reference: https://roblouie.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/pathfinding.png

Even knowing this, sometimes you can get disoriented, but if you go to the center hallway and look for elevator doors you can reorient yourself.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really nice 3D work, Blows my mind how people mamage 3D so well in so little space.

Lots of attention to detail!

Never thought a JS13k game would spook me, I hate the anticipation of scary games even though I enjoy lots of horror genre movies!

The thought of jump scares is the worse.

Also; enjoyed reading your post-mortem!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: I didn’t finish the game, I’m sorry… But i don’t like horror games… Which make your game incredibly cool! The ambiance is really excellent and scary, gameplay, sound design and 3d graphics work really well! Perfect game for the jam!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Fantastic game! The 3D engine is incredible, materials are so sophisticated, and the atmosphere is really scary :) Besides 3D, what caught my attention is the spatial sound with the enemy steps turning around. Great entry!

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Spooky little game! Really well done.

The 3D is simple yet very immersive. I would have gone even further in darkening the screen even more when the flashlight is not on.

Sound effects top notch. The actions are simple enough to not have to think too much how to play.

Great game!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Thank you so much for playing the game and the kind words! I don’t necessarily disagree with you on the darker screen outside the flashlight, but since I didn’t have room for a gamma or brightness slider, I decided to err slightly on the side of better visibility.

And thank you for mentioning the sound effects, synthesizing those sounds was not easy, especially the elevator!

Thanks again!

  • Rob

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is so good. This legitimately scared me multiple, haha, especially with the death animation.

The sound is great; I love the minimal footstep sound effect, and then the music is really creepy when it kicks in. Maybe one nitpick is that the music could fade out (instead of just cutting out) once you are out of danger?

Another thing I really liked were all of the textures: wood, textured wall, carpet, wallpaper… All looked nice!

Just a couple of quality-of-life things I was wishing for while playing:

  • Would be nice if the hotel room numbers were a bit easier to see? Maybe if the numbers were larger or angled outwards, so you didn’t have to turn so much to see them?
  • Would be nice to have a 180 degree “spin” button; useful for running inside a room, turning around, and closing the door

But yeah, this was really fun. I’ll play again later to try and beat it!

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Amazing work, the graphics and atmosphere are very well done. I was scared many times while playing. As fun as it is, this is also a very fun game to watch other people play which is a good thing to have in the days of twitch so I hope it gets a lot of play. I also read the postmortem and that was great too. Thanks so much, I really enjoyed this game.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Hey thank you! Ya I got to watch two people play it on twitch and it was really fun to watch. Also watched some friends and family play. People really get scared, which is really rewarding. Glad you also enjoyed watching it. Obviously I like watching people play it because I made it, but to hear you liked watching people play it as well is really cool!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This is obviously one of the most impressive submissions this year. The complexity of the 3D level is beyond impressive. It looks amazing. The surround sound and the spooky music was really scary and fitting.

The game doesn’t need long instructions. You’re dumped in a hotel floor and must escape. The rest, you are shown, so the gameplay is pretty simple but engaging. It does get perhaps a bit boring after a while of doing the same task over and over. I found the walking and camera movements a bit too sluggish.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to move the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: One of my top favorites this year, it does everything right. Great game!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Impressive! Really scared me out!

The 3D, textures and all the details are amazing. It is surprising how did you get all of that into 13kb. Really nice work!

comments.author: Igor Khotin
comments.text:
The scariest game of this year’s compo! And a perfect match for this year’s theme as well!

Superb graphics, models, sound, and lights! Step sounds are especially cool and add a lot in the sense of imminent danger. Light is another crucial element and hiding in the closet while being searched for is so intense that I was trying to keep silent and not to give up my location. The music also very organically adds tension. The best immersion ever!

It is a perfect 13k entry. I hardly see anything else that could be included or improved given the 13k limitation.

We enjoyed playing your game on the Twitch stream with JetPakBoy and your hints were crucial to beating it. It would be nice to have a system to detect that you’re stuck and provide a hint like “look for a key” or “look for a health pack in one of the open rooms”. But I’m not sure if it’s even possible to squeeze that in. The game has a nice description though, so everything you need to know is already there. As with most of 13k games, you need to rely on a description and not the in-game tutorials.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Thank you! It was super fun watching you both play it, especially the comments from JetPakBoy, who said (and I’m paraphrasing), “It’s pretty traumatizing but it’s really good”. Thank you again for streaming it, it was awesome to watch!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
This game really got me running scared!

Great level, beds, wallpaper, bathrooms all looked great.

VEry good use of shadows, especially when you are running and you see the shadow of It cast over you to the wall in front , brrr!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Hey thank you so much! I’m glad you also experienced the shadow in front of you like that. That was one of the visions I had for the game when I first set out to make it, so really glad that came through!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: The sound, the way you use the shadow, is perfect to create the scary atmosphere. But then I saw the face and kinda cracked up how silly it looked. The gameplay was ok, but the experience was great!

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Very atmospheric horror game.

Impressive graphics (especially lighting and high res textures). Great sound design that adds to the atmosphere.

The details like sound of footsteps and the light casted by enemy when it’s chasing / looking for you give a sense of dread.

The only negative thing I could think of would be that controls feels a little bit sluggish.

Edit: I’ve checked the game on another machine and controls works fine.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Hey, thank you so much for all the kind words!

Ya, tuning the mouse sensitivity is tough when you don’t have room for a sensitivity slider! I think I have my mouse speed turned up slightly in windows as well so, probably a bit worse than intended.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Definite “The Shining” vibes.

Very very atmospheric, really dark and foreboding.

I had a good explore round, and at times it got a little laggy.

The game was very dark, once I turned on the light it was very helpful :)

I came across a lot of locked doors, but one that was open, I noticed when going in the bathroom that one of the options I could select was to hide. But hide from what exactly?

Spine tingling, being on the height of alertness as I explored the creepy corridors.

Was I able to run? I couldn’t find how.

I did get to room 1313, but it was locked

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Hey thank you! The Shining was a big inspiration. Since you didn’t hide from anything, you must not have found the first key even. See the instructions section above where it says:

You arrive at the 13th floor with a Key. Go to the room number you have the key for. Inside that room, another key will be hidden. Find it and go to the next room and repeat until you find the key to Room 1313.

In the lower right corner you will see a key icon and a room number. Go to that room and unlock and open the door and somewhere in that room will be another key. Find it and go to the next room. Once you find the first key….there will be something to hide from.

comments.author: Nik Lever
comments.text: Knocked out that you fit the environment and a 3d engine into a 13k limit. Respect.

comments.author: Michał Chojnacki
comments.text:
Being chased by horrific things isn’t my personal idea of fun, but I can appreciate a horror game going for atmosphere instead of cheap jump scares that tire quickly. Solid all round and impressive as 3D-in-13K.

My suggestion for something that could potentially still fit into 13KB after a bit massaging: make the flashlight a resource, not a button that you press once and forget about it. A limited battery (13Wh~) comes to mind immediately.

After that? Alan Wake-like mechanic to interact with the creature (stop/push away?) when it’s within the flashlight’s radius. This would introduce both a non-obvious time limit (battery capacity), and a choice dilemma (use light to get out of a pickle, or conserve light for finding the keys).

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text: It’s scary! Age limit must be 18+.

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text: I don’t think I’ve been so freakin’ terrified in a game in a while. The music and odd thumping was just making me freak out. It’s actually scary to go play it again so I can see the ending.

comments.author: nshillingford
comments.text:
Brilliant!

I was literally afraid while playing this. It took me a couple minutes to get the courage to even leave the first room after I picked the key up.

The way you slowly start to hear those first footsteps….oh man, such good horror design.

I’m not a fan of horror really, but I can appreciate anything that makes me feel an emotion. This definitely accomplished that. Great job.

api.name: Cookie Maze 3d
api.description:
You are trapped in a cookie maze

find all 13 cookies under 13 minutes to finish the level

game is in 3d and can change between normal and bird view

W-S to move forward and backward

A-D to rotate right and left

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Sadly, I couldn’t play it on my phone.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: controls are disabled by default, I have already tested on a modest android phone but webgl does take cpu capacity. sory for what you encountered but hope you try on pc and enjoy it.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game concept is good. I like that it’s a 3D version of a pacman maze, but made easier by the lack of ghosts.

For me the A-D keys did not rotate, they did a side-step, instead Q and E rotated the view, but in 90’ steps which made me very disorientated.

I wasn’t sure why some floor tiles were red and some were green?

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The game is very hard to play as the movements are very jarring and fast, and it’s not possible to rotate, as A/D moves sideways instead.

But the music is really good. You should have it enabled by default!

edit: oh, it seems that when enabling controls, the A/D does rotate the camera. The interface is a bit confusing.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Nice job on a 3D game. I played the first level in first person view, and then switched to birds eye view. The birds eye view is much much easier. It’d be interesting to think about gameplay options that could combine the best of both. Congrats on submitting!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
Congrats on the submission! There are a few things that could be improved, like making the movement smoother or improving the overall graphics.

Anyway, feels like you have the basis for a fun game!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Reminds me of an old school RPG. Music was nice.

The game was a bit difficult though in first person mode. It was hard to map out the maze when the movement was so quick. This was especially true if you held the move down and zoomed across the level.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
3D! Thats a step I not yet dared in js13k. Good job!

The gameplay reminds me a bit of those good old dungeoncrawlers (Eye of the Beholder, etc.)

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to move the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
