It’s just compelling, addictive, excellent fun. I totally lost myself in this. I powered my data all the way up to ‘Infinity’ and had an absolute blast doing so. Superb.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
You can immediately tell what you’re meant to do, and the game handles perfectly for you to dodge, weave, and slash your way around many enemies.

I really appreciate that you can see the loadout that you have selected, and since there’s a nice variety of things for you to pick each playthrough is fully different than the last.

It was a fun challenge to plan around the 13kb limit.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The clean and minimalist design works well for the retro-style dungeon crawler feel. The character and enemy sprites are simple yet effective, providing clear visual feedback for the player. The grid-based level design is easy to follow and keeps the focus on gameplay, while the color contrast between the background and the grid makes it visually clear and easy to navigate. The game could benefit from adding a bit more visual variety in the levels themselves. While the grid-based design works well, adding small environmental details or textures to break up the monotony would enhance the sense of progression and make each level feel more unique. With a few additional details and animations, the game could feel even more engaging. Great job on creating a functional and visually clear experience that suits the gameplay!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text: I love rougelike genres, so this was a joy to play! I think you could add some music on the background that would make the experience more immersive for the player.

api.name: cube 13
api.description:
You have to find a way out of the cube. Traps and puzzles will be on your way.

13 levels

13 lives

130 seconds

Each level is unique and made with love!

Desktop controls: WASD or arrow keys

Mobile controls: use touch virtual joystick

Good luck!

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Really nice game. I especially dig the graphics, it’s just like something we’d have on a PC in the early 90s ;) Gamepad support would be a nice addition. You should look into navigator.getGamepads(), it’s super easy to use.

comments.author: Superman
comments.text: Thank you very much! Good idea about gamepads!

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text: Cute. Simple and effective. I made it to the end, which is a good sign of an engaging game!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: i wish we could walk at least twice faster

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text:
A very nice looking puzzle game! I especially like the pixel art and the minimalistic sound style. It’s great that the game elements are introduced one by one so that the consept is easy to grasp. Also, being able to push several crates at once is a really nice touch!

What I would have liked to see more is more challenging levels. Perhaps the existing elements could have been used to create more mind-bending puzzles. Now, even the last levels were quite easy.

A couple of minor things came to mind how the game could be improved: The first few maps could be much smaller. as their purpose is just to teach how the game works, it would be more pleasing to get through them quickly, especially if one would have to start the game from all over. Also, on a couple of spots I had to needlessly teleport between two rooms while just pushing a crate. Perhaps this could have been avoided with level design.

The level with narrow cliffs over emptyness created a really good contrast to the previous levels! All in all, the game could be more challenging, but nevertheless it was fun playing it through!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice pixel are, all works really well, some nice puzzles!

Sound fx are good, neat animations.

Levels were a little large for what there was too do, not to say there should have been more but small grids might have worked well.

comments.author: kennyfully1988
comments.text: This is a great game. The virtual pad has a bug that defaults to right so some levels are hard to play on mobile but mainly everything else plays well !

comments.author: Gleb Volkov
comments.text:
I’d like to point out:

  • stylistically consistent graphics
  • bold sounds
  • awesome lasers!
  • falling cubes!!

130 sec is a little much for the first levels - you could use a pass-through timer to make people hurry up at the beginning. It would be great to increase the music rhythmically as the timer approaches 0.

Overall, the game is a pretty complete package. Well done!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A really fun little puzzle game. I loved the graphics and the pushing blocks dynamic worked well.

I initially thought only one block could be pushed which left me scratching my head. But was glad to see you can push more than one.

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
It was fun, short and sweet, with a bit of challenge. I loved the pixel art style and how walls and holes had depth. A solid and simple game.

I think that maybe the walk speed was a bit too slow given how far I needed to travel. Either that or make the first few levels much smaller since I just need to do one or two simple things before heading over to the exit.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
At the beginning I wasnt sure if I was gonna finish the game, but the progression worked really well and I ended up doing all the levels.

My only draw back was that the movement wasnt cell based, I felt it was strange for this kind of game.

Besides the levels where you learn new mechanics the complex ones felt too direct, maybe the last two were the only ones where I felt challenged.

Any way, great entry. I loved the one note music.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Nice game, multiple features after some levels, nice puzzles. I think the character is really slow and collision box should be improved a bit. As head can push and traps are detected only on feet, we can “glitch” a bit sometime

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text:
I really like how clear and clean the sprites all are - each with the same light and shadow, they made my eyes very happy.

I wish I could move a bit faster. And imagine how satisfying it would be if blocks would magnetically lock into place snapped on the grid perfectly once you stop pushing them, just to appease a sense of perfect alignment? But those are just wishlist future features, the game is great as is. nice work! =)

comments.author: Superman
comments.text: Thank you so much for your feedback! I’m working on a director’s version right now. The speed of man has increased! )))

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Solid game with a diversity of puzzles to solve. It has all your classics: moving boxes, falling tiles, teleport, lasers! The visuals were nive, and the little animations like the falling blocks and lasers were nice touches.

I found the player movement speed way too slow. It was frustrating. The sound effects were very loud and harsh.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Fantastic game! It was very interesting to play and solve puzzles, great fat sound effects, cool animations and lasers! Character looks awesome. Solid entry at all! Great job!

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Gauntlet meets Sokoban ! A very clever twist on both games, easy to grasp, with a clear progression on difficulty (traps, lasers, teleports).

I reckoned the character moved too slowly on the early levels, but changed my mind later, you need the precision moves once the difficulty increases. Completing the last levels in the given time is quite stressful. (did 736s with 9 lives left)

Oldschool graphics are good, although trapped tiles are not that visible (mostly for maze level). A great game overall.

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text: Cool puzzle game, I loved the graphics and the animation of the little guy, but it definitively needs a “restart” button in case you get stuck in a level because you pushed one symbol too close to a wall and there is no way to kill yourself.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Solid gameplay, nice pixelart graphics. Gets rather stressful in later levels! Moving a bit faster would be nice… ;-)

comments.author: StormEx
comments.text:
Very nice game! Passed all levels and want more.

  • a good and clear pixel art
  • nice different mechanics
  • smooth progression with specific (tutorial) levels for introducing new mechanic

Good job!

comments.author: Igor Khotin
comments.text:
Nice twist on Sokoban and similar puzzle games, but with mortal danger.

The difficulty curve was not even to me - I feel some stages in the middle were too easy. The 13th on the other hand - wow! That is the one I felt I’d lose since I lost too many lives. It was way harder than the ones before it.

It felt good to finally solve it, though. So overall impression is very positive :)

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Very nice game!

I love puzzle games like this. A bit disappointed the character moves so slowly, you kinda get bored by just trying to reach the door.

I would have tried to get your friends to try it before submitting it, they probably would have given you this feedback!

The art is cool, very well made. The sounds simple yet very effective.

Well done!

api.name: THE TERMINAL
api.description:
The terminal is a special access program from NACA, the precursor of NASA. The program is aiming to clear the moon surface before officially going to the moon and presenting the word with “One small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.”

Use WASD or arrow keys to steer the ship. Use space for shooting laser. You have only 12 laser missiles, but on the last level you can get more by going through stars circles. Left mouse button or enter key to advance after each terminal text.

comments.author: Luke Nickerson
comments.text: I’m in love with the beauty of this game – such great stylized retro goodness. Sadly, I am not good enough to pass the first test flight, so I won’t see more of the game. I hope someone makes a video of it, or you make an easy-mode after the competition.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Love the retro vector graphics, so cool.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Mohammed Saud
comments.text:
Absolutely beautiful retro terminal effects!

I found out that I could bypass(hack?) the initial password screen by repeatedly clicking on words even after the access denied message shows up. Not sure if it’s intended.

The 13 bullets restriction felt a little too tough, especially how given how quickly the enemy ship moved, would have been great if there was a way to refill bullets.

Apart from the password screen and bullets restriction, this was a lot of fun!

comments.author: Jerome Lecomte
comments.text: Nice throwback to the laser graphics video game times! Limiting the amno to 12 missiles only is problematic on missions where the goal is to destroy a target (e.g. I ran out before I could destroy the base and was “stuck”, not dead but couldn’t win either) The intro/cutscenes are a nice touch but should be skippable (for second play-through and following)

comments.author: xem
comments.text: beautiful! too bad that we die when we touch the (invisible) ceiling

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Looks good, but gameplay is a bit basic. Also, what’s the trick to the initial login sequence. I was just clicking words at random ntil I was let through.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice graphics and sounds. Grpahics were a little difficult on my eyes but I liked the style.

First level was quite easy to pass, laid low and made it to the end, second level went on for ever?

I shot my lasers early on so was unsure what to do after that.

Has potential needs some work on the gameplay.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I love the aesthetics. I’m really curious to poke around and see how you implemented this style!

With that said, I found it very tiring in the eyes specially during gameplay. The beeps were also really harsh and loud.

I don’t know if i missed something but I don’t understand the part with all the letters. I just clicking any word until I could get in. It allowed me infinite number of attempts (number went to negatives).

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
The graphics are incredible and it’s amazing that you got a Star Fox-like game in 13kb!

I got to the 3rd part and ran out of ammo when fighting the base. I think the game is unfinished given that shot every bullet I had on the boss and I tried to die but nothing killed me and the spinning circles of triangles did nothing; I thought they would give me more ammo. I couldn’t even die flying into the ground.

Also the first part of the game where you click on words; I have no idea which word is correct. Is there a guide for this?

Overall, I suppose having more than one game modes was fun but it left us with an unfinished game. But I appreciate the effort.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Ah, the code-cracking is clearly inspired by Fallout 4, but very well implemented. The first stage was easy, the following ones went on forever as soon as i fired my last shot. I tried to get killed by the enemies, but they could not hit me.

Anyways: The presentation as old terminal-screen is amazing! Especially in combination with he vector-graphics.

Good job, overall!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Very cool graphics, and a complete 3D engine, great result!

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
Very cool aesthetic and graphics, but super hard to aim.

I couldn’t get past the UFO mission.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text: Cant get enough of the retro visuals and sounds here, the glitched bleed effect is too good! I’m glad it wasnt too easy either, makes it feel much more like an old C64 arcade game if its a but punishing, those early games werent made to be easy afterall! Great work

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text: Impressive! I really like the graphics and the gameplay, even if it’s quite demanding ;) Well done!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game looked really good.

I liked the intro and the whole aesthetic was awesome.

The sound effects were good, and the graphics reminded me of something like Elite with the wireframe or a vetrex game.

The difficulty was pretty high on the first training mission, maybe ease the player in more gently.

Very impressive

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text:
The title screen is a little game in the game, then we have a great Star Wars like, with a beautiful screen filter.

Unfortunately no great use of number 13 in any way, but it’s still a great game.

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Very immersive experience. Super cool binary sceen. I’m still not quite sure how I hacked in… I just went with my gut! Super cool graphics, and pretty fun once you get to dodging and blastin’! I think some audio with the text would’ve helped to polish. Also, would’ve loved a sliq/techy game track to go with muh blastin’. Cool entry!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
One of my top entries this year. The theme is nowhere to be seen, but honestly I don’t care: the atmosphere, the story and the models absolutely made it up for me. I made it to the end but had to cheat a bit checking the source code to see a few things here and there - honestly, I love it all. It has some shortcomings and flaws (refilling the lasers in the final battle took me forever), but this inspired me and makes me feel like a kid again.

One last thing: I had no idea one could do full-screen “filters” just directly on canvas :O I was convinced this was shaders all along, but nope, full-on canvas 2d rendering. Amazing!

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Wow, the graphics and immersion in this game are really something else. From the get go I was mesmerized at what I was seeing on screen.

I didn’t quite figure out the intro trick, just kept clicking words until it let me in. I didn’t even realize I had to do something for a while the first time.

Nice little tutorial, but then after that like some others I ran out of ammo quickly and never could get past the first UFO level.

Nice work on everything though!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: wow that graphics are wicked!!! I really love the vector 3d lines and the glitches! I wonder if it would be more fun without the intro hacking puzzle. Imagine if that was a non-interactive intro instead? Because it looks cool but felt like it was in the way of the action. would have loved more cool sci fi sound effects to match the top notch visuals. amazing work!!!

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text:
Unfortunatelly I wasn’t able to play the game. Based on the visual, THE TERMINAL seems to have an aesthetic deeply rooted in the classic hacker/terminal interface. The binary code and matrix-like appearance of the screen give it a cryptic and intriguing feel, reminiscent of old-school text-based games or coding puzzles.

I’m so disappointed I couldn’t play your game. It seems to have well done graphic design.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
Visually this is outstanding! Wireframe vectors with a glorious motion blur / glitch effect across the whole thing. Great models, great sounds and an interesting concept, too. I liked that the very first screen full of text was a puzzle to enter the game. I didn’t have a clue what the puzzle actually was, but my second random guess at clicking the words let me in - so whatever!

The first mission has you flying down a vector tunnel, avoiding the purple spinning ships zooming towards you. This is seriously hard. It would be easier if it wasn’t for the fact that flying into the sides of the tunnel also kill you, rather than just stopping you moving any further. I died more times as a result of this, while trying to avoid a baddie, than I did from hitting the actual baddies. However I did progress to mission 2 which you take down this UFO. That was a challenge, too! I think through sheer luck more than skill I managed it and then on mission 3 I had to destroy the base. I ran out of lasers and no matter how many times I flew through the star circles I never got any more shots, so had to end the game there.

As I said at the start this is visually incredible. There are quite a few spelling mistakes worth fixing, but otherwise it’s a real showpiece game to look at. Gameplay though? Probably frustrating more than fulfilling, but those lovely vectors kept me trying and trying, which is always a win. Struggled to find any link to the theme at all though.

api.name: 13 Steps to Escape
api.description:
13 Steps to Escape is a 2D pixel art puzzle game.

Every level you have to reach the flag. You can push crates, pick keys to open locks, trigger switch, roll a boulder and many mechanisms. But you only got 13 steps or actions to perform before respawning!

How to play

You can play with keyboard

  • WASD, or ZQSD, or Arrows to move
  • Enter or Space to validate selections
  • E to undo actions
  • R to restart the level if you are really stuck
  • Esc to go back to main menu

Or you can play with gamepad controller

  • Stick to move
  • A to validate selections
  • L1/Y to undo actions
  • R1 to reset level
  • Start to go back to main menu

Level Editor

A level editor to let allow anybody to create and share custom levels!

You can create levels with mouse only.

  • Pick a tile and place it on level.
  • Every level must have a single flag and current spawn.
  • You can change orientation of some tiles using the selector or mouse wheel, which is much more easier
  • Some tiles got different colors instead of orientation changes, to allow multiple gong or switch

You can test your own levels, then share the url when it is ended, other players could enjoy your work!

Inspirations and Credits

This game is mostly inspired by the beautiful puzzle game Isles of Seas and Sky.

Thanks to:

  • Romain Gillet supporting me with color palette and proposing some assets
  • WTF’UX a professional UX designer giving useful feedbacks
  • Snaga and Saxo for testing and giving feedbacks
  • derBenniBanni for its remote debugging of gamepad controller

Game created and developed by Jonathan Vallet alias Satanimax

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Absolutely brilliant! Levels 5 and 8 in particular really had me stumped at first.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Thank you! There is a full walkthrough of the game for those you get stucked or to check if you solved puzzle as it was designed :) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tTcBJreNepI Level 7 and 8 are supposed to be the hardest ones

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: A fabulous take on the timed life mechanic, this game reminded me of Minit. Great job!

comments.author: Matthias-Leutard
comments.text: This game is really cool, very addictive! great quality work as usual !

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Very nice gameplay, which could have been a hit back in the good old days of the AMIGA :-)
The customization options and overall the pixelartstyle is great. The cozy music and the carefully placed sfx match the game… Also worth mentioning: two supported options for the controls (keyboard and gamepad) Great entry!

comments.author: IroncladDev
comments.text:
The way some blocks were designed were a bit unintuitive, but I got used to them.

Controls were very easy to use.

Great use of the theme.

Awesome job.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Interesting mechanics. Got stuck on fourth level, maybe could use some simpler levels to introduce new mechanics here and there, before giving the complex ones.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: merci pour les retours! le temps passé que chacun passe sur les jeux étant limité si on veut tout tester, j’ai préféré faire un onboarding en accéléré et faire moins de niveaux. les 3 premiers niveaux sont simples, les 3 suivants devraient être de difficulté moyenne. les 2 derniers sont plus complexes. Si un niveau est trop dur il y a la sélection de niveau pour tester les autres. Le niveau 4 joue sur l’idée de ne pas toujours changer de point de spawn pour revenir directement après avoir validé certaines étapes sans passer sur les pièges.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I loved the look of the game and how it played, the puzzles were fun.

I’ll have to play again with a gamepad, and come back to it to try the level editor too.

I could have kept on playing this had I not got stuck on level 4 - couldn’t find way to get the keys.

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: thank you very much, I spent a lot of time creating the puzzles to try to make the mechanics intuitive. Level 4 seemed to me to be of average difficulty, you can try the following ones with the level selection if it poses a problem

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice game! I didnt read the instructions so was wondering why I was respawning all the time :D

Figured it out by the number of steps in the top left!

Well made, good job!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Wow, a lot of content packed into 13kb. I was feeling very proud after beating level 7, and then level 8 😲 Excellent pixel art, sound effects, music, puzzle design. Fantastic use of theme. This is a total package. Amazing work!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text: Fantastic twist on a sokoban game. I especially loved thinking about how to use the spawn points to get back to places that would be inaccessible otherwise. I think level 5 and 7 took me the longest to complete and had some tricky parts to it. The addition of a level editor and character customization is just icing on the cake. Great work.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
The ability to customize your avatar - I have not seen such a thing here yet - cool!

I like the concept of 13 steps, as well as the ability to change the starting position relative to which these steps are calculated. The “undo last step” mechanic, paired with a 13-step limit, allows you to correct likely wrong routes without starting over.

The sound is pleasant and deepens the immersion in the game.

As for controls, I’m assuming you’re using “e.key” to find the key. In this case, if my OS language is not English, then this code will not work for me. I recommend using “e.code” instead to avoid this problem.

Nice game, congratulations!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The theme is implemented beautifully. I think this is my favorite game yet. It makes good use of the game mechanic, and I have several “aha”-moments. Not only that, but you have a character creation and level editor as well. Truly impressive what you’ve been able to pack into 13kB (12.12kB to be exact.)

The game becomes extra interesting from level 4 because you have to think carefully about which steps to take to complete the level. You cannot just do each task one at the time in any order you like anymore. It gives the mechanic a purpose, which I think is great.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: Oh this is so smart! First up, the graphics are lovely. Really nice and remind me of the classic Chip’s Challenge. As does the gameplay, actually. It plays similar to sokoban but with a much heavier dose of puzzle added to it. You have to make it where you need to get in 13 steps, using the activation markers to reach further out areas. Collect keys, move through one-way tiles, fill holes, etc. It’s properly challenging but rewarding to complete - and the fact it even includes a full-blown level editor in the 13KB is nothing short of inspiring. An excellent game, well worth playing.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Woah, this is great!

  • Cool customizable character design
  • Nice graphics
  • Atmospheric music (sounds great when the drums kick in)
  • Really nicely designed puzzles

Awesome job!

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I REALLY loved this game! It reminds me of The Adventures of Lolo. I got stuck on the last part of Level 7 for a solid bit, but I ended up prevailing! Great level design, good graphics, and a solid game track. Good form!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Thank you for the feedback! Level 7 is tricky to move boulders, but the last part can be skipped by an alternative way :D. But player learns the feature at level 8 only ^^. On the walkthrough video I’ve added this alternative way to pick the key without having to push the crate that locks the passage

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Excellent entry!

This game is great in pretty much every respect, even without the extras that you have thrown in like character creation and level editing!

Great pacing, intuitive controls and mechanics, packed out content (I’m gonna have to look and see what tricks you used to put so much into this entry), etc.

Levels 7 & 8 were beasts to solve compared to the first 6.

comments.author: Jude
comments.text: Fanominal Game, LOVE the Art and the custimazation would have been great with a small tutorial for the first level even if you just start off really simple. But all in all an amazing game and you should be very proud of your self

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
There’s a few entries that have grabbed the attention of my kids, this is one of them, which is very nice! Reminds me of Zelda.

I loved the music and the game is easy to figure out by yourself.

Congrats!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Great entry! This is really fun. As a fun of puzzle games, I really appreciated the clever use of the 13-step limit and how it ties into the theme. It was executed brilliantly!

The visuals and sound effects are also spot on. What I especially love is sometimes using up all the steps to return to the starting point was the correct solution—such brilliant level design is truly impressive!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: I’m not a fan of sokoban games (which is funny considering I made a game about pushing boxes haha), but I have to say I love the twists you added! A couple levels had me thinking hard how to get to the finish line with the 13 steps allowed. Nice game!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text: I think I JUST saw an indie title almost like this one. Very great pixel art and sokoban puzzle-solving game!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: It’s possible, I’ve wrote it in credits and on github: the art is mostly inspired by “Isles of Seas and Sky”, a great puzzle game I played this year (and a bit inspired by Minit). I re-created all the assets like theirs, as I loved the tone, and they were already perfectly affordant

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: the art style is so op and enabling map editor is very impressive feature to integrate in a 13kb game, the graphics very cool but using 13 steps repeatedly isnt just using 13 steps i do not agree of this to be the challenge

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text:
Wow, very cool game! I love the graphics. I wish I had done a pixel art game! Very nice vibe and good puzzles. I will look into the code to see what you did there. I remember from Discord (if I am not mistaken) that you were doing some crazy math.

Really good entry!

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Thank you! I was on discord this year, yes :) . I tried to comment my code as much as possible, and wrote a post mortem too (available on github). If you have any question you can ask me on discord.

api.name: Infernal Adolescence
api.description:
DEMONS ARE JUST LIKE US… UNTIL THEIR 13TH BIRTHDAY. Help a struggling teenage demon go through puberty, transforming from a snot-nosed brat to the vile creature we all fear.

The mechanics are all point-and-click buttons, and the mechanics are in the style of an incremental game. You can mute the audio, but I recommend turning it back on when you start learning the fiddle!

Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text: OMG I love the art and the style. Very interesting approach on IDLE/clicker game. Also, it was cool to practice violin and change notes. Resource bars are implemented satisfyingly, like a bottle with liquid. I wish the game was not that long, tho.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Like an alternative cookie clicker!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: A new demon has emerged! Nice, this was great. There was a just-right balance of mechanics and puzzle solving for a fun casual episode. The intro writing was excellent, and nicely set the stage. The UI was easy to interpret. I was never left feeling “what do I do next?”, there was always a clear next goal. I had fun! Nice job!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Very original and really fits the theme! I loved the graphics and the little details such as being able to play the violin. I had fun to play, well done!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Muhahaha, i win 😈

This was really fun. I liked how the progress wasn’t straightforward, and I had to figure out how to progress each max level to get other things. It’s not a mindless clicking game. Playing the violin was my least favourite part because not only was it super expensive, but it truly sounded horrible.

Really nice vibe, cute graphics and chill music. The violin music editor was completely pointless but a super cute addition. There were some design imperfections that, if you would address them, this game would be ready to release. One possible improvement, would be to somehow highlight the organs that are already fully mature.

Also, interesting and unique interpretation of theme.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
An interesting take on the theme XD

I’m liking the art style, and theres some interesting elements in the gameplay. Maybe I am not overly experienced with this kind of game, but a bit more direction/chatter could have been helpful.

Good work overall

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Behold mortals! Obey the demon! For it has sharpened horns and lots of confidence! And a band! Dont forget the band!

Hehe, nice “Demon Clicker” with a story. The pixelart style is great!

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I loved how this game fits the theme!

The game is also very fun, the only thing I missed was a way to save the game. Starting from scratch is a new way of torture :D

Congrats!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: This is a perfect game to me: it takes a formula (idle / clicker game), fits it with the theme, and adds very cool interesting mechanics (improving some stats reduces others - which makes sense in the world of the game too!). The tiny music player was a great touch. Atmospheric, nicely written, perfect fit for the theme - what else can we ask? 10/10! One of my favorites this compo :)

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text:
Aaaargh! A clicker idle game!. These kind of games are so addictive.

Nice idea and concept, but no actual use of competition theme.

By the way, I enjoyed a lot and I will come back to play it after I rated all game submissions.

comments.author: Lucas Drummond
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback! The idea of the theme connection was both the fear of turning 13 and going through puberty (puberty is scary!), and also that a 13 year old demon turns from a little kid to a fearsome monster.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The pixel graphics were well made, and look neat. The texture on stuff was great.

What was with the band and library? I wasn’t able to get them to do much?

The elements on the left all seemed to require money of which I didn’t have any, perhaps I was being impatient?

I’m afraid I didn’t hear any audio except when messing with the band - it was all pretty quiet.

All in all, an interesting concept and beautifully illustrated.

api.name: 13th Guy
api.description:
Don’t be the 13th guy
…in checkpoints
…when falling down
…when finishing the round

And don’t be one of the last 13th guys of the round. Only one can survive!

Use arrow keys or WASD to race.

comments.author: Mohammed Saud
comments.text: Really nice interpretation of the theme! I did not realize this type of game would be as fun even in a single player format, and I like that you could push players off the edges. The minimal art style is lovely.

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. We are glad you enjoyed it :)

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Strangely enjoyable. I also liked that you could nudge the competition over the edges moohahah.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. But remember, there is always is a risk falling down yourself when pushing the others 💀

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This was really fun! Unique gameplay and catchy music! The character designs and animation are very nice, too!

My only wish is that you could start over the same level if you get eliminated. But maybe I’m just not a strong enough gamer!

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: Thank you! Yeah, I understand that starting from the very beginning can be quite harsh :) We were in quite a hurry to get the game balanced so perhaps that is something we could have considered more.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Finished second in the last round!

It reminded me of the TV series “The Squid Game”. Very innovative! Nice, even fun graphics, good music too, but I’d like to be able to turn it on/off.

Also, I don’t have time to finish reading the control instructions before starting the first time, maybe it would be better to give the player the opportunity to move further by pressing a key, for example. It was also a bit boring to start from the beginning if you lost in later levels. I would really like to be able to continue from the current level, for example, if I lost on the 2nd level - restart from the 2nd level, on the 3rd - from the 3rd. Since the AI ​​knows the map and I don’t, it will probably take a few tries to win, and starting from start every time is a bit frustrating.

When I was pushed for the first time, I was pleasantly surprised, but when I realized that I could also push, well, revenge was sweet :D. It was also interesting to keep a distance from the AI. Nice balance!

It was also interesting not always to go first, when you clearly don’t have time, it’s better to come last, but alive :).

It’s a very very nice game though, congrats! I had fun!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text:
Thank you for the comments.

But would there less fear of 13 if you could retry? ;) But that might be if/when doing a new version based on comments and things we would like to add but did not have time or space.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: There are a lot of things I really like with this game: of course the music which is punchy and very well done; the concept itself wich is original and fits perfectly the theme; the IA behind the oponents; and the funny animations. I particularly liked to push the oponents in the void! Well done!

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: We are glad you liked it. Pushing opponents over the edge seems to be everyone’s favourite feature :D

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
Fall guys! But in 13k. My god how you managed to write an AI which is so enjoyable to play with. There should be some trick.

Great music, graphics and gameplay. I’m surprised!

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: Yeah, pretty evident what game gave us the inspiration :) Thank you for the comments!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: Awesome. I got 4th, 3rd, and 2nd. I really thought I had the last one, but then the guy came out of nowhere. Maybe some rubber banding happening? This is so great! Really wonderful experience! You captured the feeling of Fall Guys perfectly. I particularly enjoyed trying to push the other guys off of the moving platforms. Great graphics, great music, really well done!

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: Thank you for the comments! The AI plays on equal terms with the player. Or if it gets any advantage, then that wasn’t intentional :)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Cool music.

Stumble guys vibes.

Really tricky to play, getting pushed by NPCs feels harsh.

Nice game, well done

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you for the comments. You just need to fight back :)

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: I like how the theme is implemented. Being first is difficult. I only managed to get second.

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. Yeah, the balance is now not to be too easy - so let’s keep trying :)

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
Faaaall guyssss! The game’s a lot of fun, the music feel great, and in particular, I’m impressed by how real the NPCs feel. I had to double check that there was no Server category this year, and that this is indeed a single player game :)

I was having a little bit of trouble making out the topology of the course. Perhaps some 2.5D or an isometric view would have helped, but it’s a minor complaint. Great job overall!

The concept fits the theme very well, too. I really didn’t want to finish 13th and I felt the pressure when other beans chose a better path that I did.

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text:
Yes, NPC’s AI was not easy task and needed lot’s of testing - great if we managed to deliver it finally. :) We could also tweak the view a little but we also wanted have it be more unique than usual 3D.

Thank you for the comments and playing.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I loved the music! The game play is addictive!

Congrats on putting all of that together!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. It was hectic month to get this out in time :)

comments.author: Jonathan Vallet
comments.text: Excellent game, really fun to push opponents to be the first :D. AI of characters is really cool! took me 3 attempts to finish first at last round!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. You got the trick of winning :)

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really nice, I like that it felt you were on ice, made it tough at times and not knowing when you needed to slow down meant I over shot the mark a few times.

Liked that I could boop other people while they waited for platforms!

Nice music and simple bit fun gfx!

Good job

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: Thank you for playing! Getting the movement physics to work as it is was a lot of work, so it’s nice to hear that it worked out ok :) We also planned to have even more icy surfaces, but run out of time for that.

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
One of my favorite entries!

Fall Guys in 2D and triskaidekaphobia style!

What I liked:

You cannot see too far ahead of you. That adds to the suspense to which path should be taken.

Soundtrack was a nice touch.

Simple instructions.

I liked that you can push other players off.

What can be improved:

The movement mechanics feels too smooth. Just a preference.

Adding a bit more sound effects would ehnance player experience.

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: Thank you for the comments! That’s the effect we were looking for when limiting how far one could see, so we are glad if that turned out well. Good points to consider when working on our “director’s cut” -version :)

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Hahah this game seriously made me laugh. The way you can bump into others (or them into you!) and make them fall, was hilarious. Very squid-gamey. The character design is very goofy, but it works. The music is great.

I felt like the movements were a bit sluggish and the floor felt slippery. The instructions appear on the screen too fast for me to read them. Just keep them on screen until I press Enter again perhaps?

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text:
Thank you, so we managed to make interesting atmospehere there :)

We will try to fix those thing for director’s cut version, thanks you for the feedback.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Tremendously good fun. Will come back to this. need 1st place.

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Thank you. Good luck, 1st place is very possible. :)

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Wonderful take on the fall guys genre! Had much fun playing it, and throwing others in the abbys. :-)

Multiplayer would be great for this! (try rune.ai for instance)

comments.author: Tero Jäntti
comments.text: We are glad you liked it :) And thank you for the hint, rune.ai looks interesting. Although we are not planning on adding multiplayer, but we are working on a post-jam version (perhaps with even smarter AI opponents :)

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text: I really liked the atmosphere of this game! The music contributes a lot to it. The graphics are also cute. Even though the controls are not perfect, they do the job. It matches the theme of this year’s edition quite well too. Being the 13th literally kills you. Great job.

api.name: Shadow Survivor
api.description:
A small game made under 6kb of size (compressed) with pattern matching and feature extraction capabailities

Game Features

Survive the waves of enemies with canvas as the controls Draw ‘13’ to attack all enemies at once Draw ‘O’ to attach the nearest enemy Health of Player is determined by background color Enemy health is determined by color of enemy

Survive Until The dawn

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text: This is a cool idea (drawing attacks), but I wasn’t quite sure how to get the attacks to trigger. I could only achieve the “13 - attack all enemies” move one time.

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I got the attack to work a few times, but I wasn’t really sure how to keep it going, or how to time my attacks. I’m glad there was some audio in there, but I struggled with the controls and gamplay.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: After some experimenting I learned to do the attacks almost every time. It’s a nice tech experiment, but if it is the core element of the game, it might be worth considering if it is actually fun.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: This is a fun idea. My final score was 75. My best accuracy was 50. I couldn’t ever get a match with “13”, so I went all “O”. I’m very curious what the model looks like to evaluate the input! After I hit 75, the background turned black and the enemies stopped moving, so I assumed that was the end of the game? Interesting idea, fun experience, nice job!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
There needs to be hints on whether I’m doing the right thing. Either in visual cues or worded descriptions, it really needs to help me figure out how I drew the circle incorrectly.

It’s a very interesting mechanic on paper but the challenge seems to be to draw the circle correctly every time and nothing more.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text:
I also missed some hints in the game. I could attack by drawing a “13” once. I tried repeating it with similar drawings unsuccessfully. I also could hit enemies a few times by drawing an “O”, but not consistently.

I believe drawing curve patterns like 3 and O is difficult. If you have a not-so-precise mouse, you are in trouble. I see the link with the theme, but if you will have a post-compo version, maybe patterns like X, Z or a simple slash would be easier for the player…

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Not sure what i was doing, and drawing stuff seemed to not work much. I drew a willy, which unfortunately did nothing

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
I drew a “Z” for Zorro and junmped out of my chair as the attack went off! I Tried everything to attack but Z was a one off and I never attacked again.

A great idea but no idea how you consistantly trigger attacks, I drew all the circles sizes I could manage but sadly none worked!

Would love to see a play through where someone has this working.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The idea is very innovative, but it’s too unreliable. I don’t have an idea how how you expect me to draw a 13, so it rarely worked. The circle also worked maybe 1 out 10 times.

I’m very curious how you implemented the image recognition.

The audio was painfully loud.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Neat concept for a game. Has drawn-to-life vibes.

I enjoyed drawing tons of little sketches - almost all were not recognised.

I did some Os and 13s, but the scrolling enemies kept coming unphased and unaffected.

Not sure if I missed something here?

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: I really tried but I was not able to get my player to do anything. I tried drawing and submitting numbers but it didn’t seem to help at all. Getting a negative score is usually not the best. Better to start at some number and decrease, where 0 means game over.

comments.author: Dann Sullivan
comments.text: I’ve got to admit, I couldn’t get it to work properly - no input seemed to trigger anything. Perhaps it didn’t recognise the shape of the numbers I wrote in? Very fun idea though, I like the perspective and concept.

api.name: 13-Maze
api.description:
Click on the tile to move it.

Depending on the tile, your score goes up or down.

The game ends when the score reaches 13 points or more or -13 points or less.

Find the exit.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Interesting idea. I think you had a good idea, but the numbers should always be visible, even far away numbers, so the game could be about planning the route instead of something so random.

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I cleared the maze a few times. It’s an interesting question, how you could give the player more agency without revealing too much. Congrats on submitting, nice job.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A nice idea for a game, the graphics and concept were great.

I think it would be nice to have the parts of the level you’ve visited stay visible. The movements were very slow, and the number of points going up or down seemed to be random?

Perhaps having the current score always visible would be good to help keep track of it.

This was more a mobile game than a desktop game (with the portrait aspect ratio).

The font was a bit “electric” and jumpy.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Suggestion: you should change the gameplay so that the player can make movement-descisions based on information on the screen (not random steps with seemingly random results) this would add to the experience.

Also the popup-menu to move or get a hint is not working in regard of gameplay-flow. You coukld cahnge it to doubleclick for movement and single-click for “help”. Or make a help-button and use the single-click for movement. something like that.

Anyways: Congrats for making a game below 13kB :-)

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: nice work on an interesting game which reminds me of minesweeper in a good way! I feel like I didn’t quite understand exactly how to move with a sense of strategy and was more just exploring a minecraft world waiting to see what came next. thanks for joining js13k!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Honestly a viable concept, I think it could work better if the player had a Minesweeper-like way to get more information about the world as the game progresses. (Right now, as soon as I’ve exhausted the hints, it’s a “mad dash to the exit” kind of strategy.)

Nice text effect on the title screen! Works well with the background ambient audio.

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text: The challenge I had was tracking my score, and the choice of me moving from one tile to another tile is purely guesswork. The background sound feels like static, I think replacing it with something less buzzing would make the whole player experience more positive?

comments.author: Siorki
comments.text:
Interesting idea, would have been worth spending more time on the concept. The focus is on staying between -13 and +13 more than navigating inside a maze, so the restricted view area is not needed.

On the other hand, a display of the current value would have been handy. And I recommend keyboard controls instead of having to click twice for each move.

api.name: Please Finish In 13th Place
api.description:
You are a galaxy famous space pilot

Until one day…

Hitting walls will make you go even faster. Your brakes have been sabotaged. Yet, you must finish this race in 13th place.

A racing game where you must finish last. Use the mobile controls or A/D+space keys to make sure you don’t overtake any of the other racers.

I hope you enjoy the faux 3D graphics, the wacky concept and levels, and the music, in this game which gave me great joy to create and tweak.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Wild graphics! The game looks awesome. The way the ship turns and jumps also feels very natural.

Good music, too!

I think the course could have had a bit more variation, perhaps? I could “go straight” and be fine for a lot of the race

But anyway, this was a really stylish submission! Nice!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thanks for your comment!

I put a lot of love into the ship’s movement :) it’s not real 3D, instead I draw 3 triangles after manipulating their 2D coordinates according to velocity, jumping and other inputs.

The course could use more variation, yes :) I would like to improve it in the future.

Thanks again!

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: I thought the story was great😁 , audio goes really well with the game, and premise was really good, however things fell a little flat on the game play and controls. Solid entry. Also, I’d like to take this time to encourage makers/players to play the entries out of order!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thanks for your comment :)

For the music, I guess I just found something that worked well and fought the temptation to “improve” it because I know where that would lead.

The gameplay and controls should feel a lot more fun. It seems like the ship isn’t that agile right now! I might change this in the director’s cut version.

You’ve encouraged me to shuffle the DOM in the games page so I would see a diverse set of games!

(() => {
  let main = document.getElementsByTagName('main')[0]
  let shuffled = [...main.children].map(elm => ({ elm, rand: Math.random() })).sort((a, b) => a.rand - b.rand)
  shuffled.forEach(({ elm }) => main.appendChild(elm))
})()

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game plays great, the graphics do look really 3D so congrats on the effect.

It’s really sick and smooth.

But can’t for the life of me figure out how to slow down.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
The sextillionaire, he took your brakes! That’s the justification I came up with to remove the brakes. You have to collide with other racers and make them go faster than you, though the game never tells you! 😅

I appreciate your comment on the 3D. It took a long time to do and I’m pretty happy with how it came out.

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Nice intro screen. Love the narrative build up. The graphics are fun, and the music is immersive. Love the rainbow road “level”

Needs a “pause” button, and I’d be good to have an in-game “pause” pop up menu so you can restart the game, the track etc. Be great to have some sound effects when you go into walls etc. Not sure if game would need something to show how far “into” the game you are. I’m playing with no idea of how much left on each level etc.

I like the “tutorial” stage where you let players know the controls etc.

Some helpful instructions in your GitHub README, if you build it out a little more would be even better!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
A pause menu, sound effects and some game status information would probably be good to add indeed. I might add it to the director’s cut version!

After I read your comment I added some stuff to the README, so it should be possible for others to understand the source code and build process.

Thank you for your comment!

comments.author: Cody Ebberson
comments.text: I worked hard and helped the quadrillionaire get a new space yacht! Clever writing. Fun graphics, pretty trippy. There was something very smooth about the line drawing algorithm you used for the outline of the ship, and the ship had a nice bounce effect after jumping. Nice job!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thanks for your comment and your hard work!

The line drawing was just HTML5 canvas .stroke() but it looks interesting because I added a brightness filter to the player. Though I wish that canvas could draw pixelated lines like mspaint :(

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I really love the vibe you have going on, with the graphics and the music. Amazing.

Gameplay wise, there’s not a lot to do, as during most of the time I’m just there sitting waiting for something to happen. Also it seems that once you pass to 12th place, you basically lost already, and there’s nothing I can do to do back to 13th place.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Yeah, it gets boring sometimes :’( I might fix it in a “director’s cut” version.

That version might change what happens to you when you overtake someone. It may be game over, or restart the track. Or neither! I might implement a challenge where you get someone else to overtake you.

Thanks for your comment :)

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: OK, I won,… so I failed :) I don’t know if it is possible to finish at the 13th place, but I was not able to! I found the concept original and loved the changes of colors at each step. I think more curves would benefit the game and would be more spicy to the player. Nice work!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Congratulations on your failure!

It is indeed possible to finish in 1st place, as well as 13th place, and you get different endings, though neither is easy to achieve.

I agree it needs more curves, perhaps even easy ones that don’t cause a warning sign and can be concurrent with other events.

Thanks for your comment :)

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Well made game with trippy visuals. Pseudo 3D elements are nicely done (especially the ship). I don’t know if fisheye effect on walls was intentional, but it adds a charm to the game. Music hits hard.

I wouldn’t be surprised to find something like that released in the late 80’s, early 90’s on Amiga or PC.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thank you for your comment! I did work a lot on the ship’s movement :)

The fisheye effect was intentional. It looks weird and wraps around to the top of the screen sometimes, but I didn’t fix any visual glitches that I thought added character.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Solid entry, I did write up a much longer reply but it seems to have been lost.

Reminds me of the PS1 game WipeOut, really nice level changes and ship.

Lots of fun to play through even though the controls are a little tough at times.

I think the theme didnt quite help you out here, some other mechanic might have made the game more interesting, once you miss a player you have failed already.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
What inspired me to make the spaceship look and move like that was wipeout. As a kid, I played a demo of wip3out over and over again, so it was very easy for me to spend time coding the movement of the 4 2D coordinates that make up the ship.

I agree that you fail after you miss a player. I probably could have restarted the current level with a “FAILURE” message if that happened, and the game would have been better for it.

Thank you for your comment!

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
I like the twist of a racing game in which you have to do your worst and finish last :) I didn’t fully understand, however, what to do when I approach NPC racers. Am I supposed to run into them? If I do, the game says “SUCCESS”, but I’m not sure what actually happens then. Do I push them forward and then catch up to them again?

Are there other ways of slowing down? If I accidentally advance to the 12th position, can I somehow go back to being 13th? And since the game is about trying hard to finish 13th, I guess it’s a case of Triskaidekaphilia :)

The graphics are great, reminded my of Skyroads a lot. I really liked the abstract backgrounds, and the upbeat music matched the gameplay, too. I just wish I had a bit more control over steering. As it is right now, it feels like I’m mostly going straight all the time.

There’s a lot of potential here, and the style is great. Well done!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
You made me check out SkyRoads! What a trippy game :)

Yes, you need to run into other racers, which will kick them forward. You can’t personally slow down (the sextillionaire, he has removed your brakes), only transfer your speed into others’ rear ends. It’s a bit of an issue with the gameplay!

As you mention, it’s not currently possible to be overtaken by another racer. I might add a challenge to the director’s cut, where someone comes from behind and you have to dodge them or yourself be kicked forward.

I don’t understand what you mean by going straight all the time, but I do agree there are problems with steering currently. I think the track might be too thin.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Well, from the 3rd time I was able to successfully fail and present a new yacht to my customer :D (How to get a yacht for yourself? :)).

I felt like I was in a trance, similar to the feeling of not blinking your eyes for a long time in childhood, but on the contrary straining so much that you start seeing glitches in front of your eyes :D.

Very good work with colors, good contrast where you don’t get lost. I also like 3D. The hard turns were quite hard :), but in the end I really liked them! The background on which snakes are crawling is very cool! The music was also well immersed in the process!

It is quite innovative to finish last :).

I liked the idea and the story, it was fun and I laughed - I love it :).

I would like to be able to be overtaken by those whom you have overtaken, thereby maintaining uncertainty until the end of the race :).

The only thing I would like to have simply as an option is the ability to turn on/off the music, but luckily the music here was really good :). And also fitting the theme, if I want to finish 13th, then I love the number 13 and dislike all the other places, which is the opposite of the theme for me :).

As for controls, I’m assuming you’re using “e.key” to find the key. In this case, if my OS language is not English, then this code will not work for me. I recommend using “e.code” instead to avoid this issue.

Cool timer for the month in the last location!

Very impressive game, I had fun, I laughed - congratulations!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
It would be great to be overtaken by other drivers – or for the mechanic to be more clearly told to the player!

Sorry about e.key – I didn’t know! But it makes sense. I’ll keep it in mind for the future, thanks for telling me.

Thanks for playing :)

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: The graphics are very charming and the plot and concept ultra funny. Well done!

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text: Thank you!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Nice retro-artstyle and music, could be from one of those machines in Flynn’s Arcade :-)

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text: Thanks!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: fun game and lots of lights, no idea how to maintain the 13th place to win but the navigating is easy and colorful. good work

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text: You have to hit racers you’re getting close to in order to kick them forward. It turned out a bit too opaque, unfortunately :(

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Interesting concept, and I can see how it wasnt easy to code using a regular canvas2D.

The gameplay was weak but the presentation was neat.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thanks for playing!

Yeah, I ended up shipping too soon. I might add to the gameplay later.

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
Really enjoyed the graphics! Especially the rainbow road 😁

Fun concept, and great simplicity. I enjoyed this more than some of the games with more gameplay.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text: Thanks for your comment :) I’m adding some more gameplay today for the director’s cut version.

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
You failed by succeeding.

This one is unique. I really liked 3D visuals, every level is new one so want to progress to see more. In the end of the race I learned melody by hearth. Reversed gameplay made it more interesting and not just race to get top1. Punching your opponents was fun, I wish there was a possibility to slow down somehow to wait for your opponent if you failed.

Great work.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Thank you, and congratulations for your successful failure!

In the director’s cut version I won’t let you slow down but I’ll reset the level if you pass someone.

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I really like the graphics for this game, though the LEFT and RIGHT at the bottom of the screen did nothing. My only real issue is I don’t know how to lose.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
Sometimes, hardcore gamers find it really hard to lose at a game! This was the major challenge in my game.

Nobody else reported any issues with the left/right buttons :/ I wonder what browser you’re using!

api.name: FRI3
api.description:
A game for JS13k 2024 by Ilya Kuzmichev and Alexandra Alhovik

The goal of the game is to have fun through a series of bloody murders of happy students. Play and remember why you’re afraid of Friday the 13th.

A katana, an axe and shelters will help you in the murder, but beware of the police. You can level up by killing students and collecting xp points.

Have a juicy game and watch out for the police!

Controls

  • Keyboard: WASD or Arrows to move.
  • Or use virtual stick on your device touchscreen.

Technology

  • ziglang
  • zzfx - ported to zig and slightly modified

comments.author: Andy Sommerville
comments.text: Awesome look! Reminds me of South Park.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Thanks for the review. I hope it was fun to play!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text: Enjoyed the game, although it felt a bit simple since all I could really do was walk around? But the feel of the game and all the gore made it still feel fun. Love that all the campers are in their underwear lol, true to the film. Played for quite a while and eventually got a memory error, which made me notice you’re using wasm!? Pretty wild, would be curious to know more about how the game works.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text:
Thank you very much for the review! I hope it was fun to play! Yes, we tried to make a cartoon parody of the film, the difficulty is really specially simplified and the progression allows you to just enjoy the main mechanics, it becomes very easy to play.

The game is written in Zig and it was my personal technical challenge to fit the whole game into 13kb, which is a bit unusual due to completely different tricks. I think this is an interesting direction for the community

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Ahh, casual student slaughter.. nothing quite like it.

I like the graphical style, cool.
games: Badluck Butter Chicken Goes To Outer Space
Badluck Butter Chicken Flies The Unfriendly Skies

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Thank you very much for the review! I hope you had fun playing! We thought that the phobia of the number 13 can be not only the player or the main character, but also vice versa.

comments.author: Superman
comments.text: A very fun game! I played with great pleasure! I also looked at the technical side of the game. Zig + wasm is used here. I consider this an additional challenge! Very cool!

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: thanks for the review, hope it was fun :) indeed it was my personal challenge to make 13kb in web assembly

comments.author: Michelle Duke
comments.text:
Feels a little weird playing a murder game, but that’s just me. Weird concept, but good use of theme; not my type of game, but you’ve done a great job on it.

Music and sound effects are great. Love the intro screen with instructions, great transitions (scene transitions), and intuitive controls. A lot of blood… but good animation on it. White boxes up the top I assume are the number of people to kill, but even having a face or something next to this to tell what they are would be useful.

Nice job on your GitHub README.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Thank you very much for your feedback! We are very pleased that you paid attention to our game. We were upset that you didn’t manage to get to the most fun part of the game. This year we wanted to devote more time to the technical challenge - to use the web assembly to implement the game. (All murders in the game are fictional and have nothing to do with the real world)

comments.author: Hogia
comments.text: Amazing, love the art style and the animation, is very fun to play and not cool to murder innocents :))!

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Hello! Thank you very much for your feedback! Yes, it really is! Provocative irony. I hope it was interesting to play

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Interesting that you used Zig and not JS, never used this so started reading up on it.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Hello! Feels a little limited in possibilities, but why not!? The topic requires experiments and disclosure, the process was really interesting. I hope it will benefit the community. Big Thanks for the attention!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Great graphics, and I really like how the difficulty is set to a level that everyone can enjoy. The sound effects and animation are excellent. I also enjoy the characters’ reaction.

It feels like a solid starting point, and I hope to see potential for future development—especially more weapon options. It reminds me of Vampire Survivors, but with more active combat!“

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Thank you very much for this wonderful review! Interestingly, we are thinking in the same direction! There was not enough time to add the choice of skill when getting a level and progression with additional options for weapons and equipment… in general, the vector of future development is planned :)

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: What a bloody game :)) It is super fun to play and the atmosphere is amazing between violence and laugh. I loved the graphics and the dynamism of the game. Very well done!!

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Thank you very much! Also thanks you give attention to blood effects, and violence-laugh vibe! We are happy you enjoy playing our game!!!

comments.author: Michał Chojnacki
comments.text:
Absolutely love the fact that you made Zig work for this. And it’d be even more awesome if that somehow, somewhere, turned into a tutorial-like article/post-mortem for others.

Currently most pipelines produce runtimes that are way too big to get baked into a WASM module for the purposes of this competition. This left little choice, other than basically C. I mean, it is possible to shrink others - like Rust - down to meet the limit, but it basically equates to throwing the entire standard library out the window, and you end up with a lot of useless overhead anyhow and little room for an actual game.

Would love to see more people taking on this challenge. Not sure how much of Zig you had to rip apart for this, but I do know that a Rust hello world print to console compiles to a ~150KB Win binary, and what it took me to write a 64KB networked Spotify player, including a Ogg Vorbis decoder and similar bells and whistles… (And yes, it can print hello world, too).

It was an awesome learning experience to write it - and I hope yours was as at least as educational and entertaining as mine. And a tad less frustrating, hopefully ;)

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text:
Thank you very much for the review!

In first place thank you for checing out it was made in Zig! Thas very special and interesting experience. There were a challenge and I personally just happy project fits 13kb!

Yes, we plan to make post-mortem related to Zig part later! Sure, it is awesome learning experience!

And hope you enjoy playing our game!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Makes me think of a Slayaway Camp without the puzzles :D. Got a good chuckle from the gameplay. Overall solid entry.

It took me a bit to realize what the green and red bars / drops meant.

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Hello! Very nice and thank you for the good feedback! We have been thinking for a very long time how to fit into the size limit and make the start of the game softer and more understandable at the beginning, but we are glad that the points of lives and experience are still becoming clear. Initially, we wanted to make puzzles and some keys to get into the rooms with goals, but web assembly is a completely new experience for us and there was not enough time to throw something else :) once again, we are very happy and appreciate your feedback! Thank you!!

comments.author: Lewis Nakao
comments.text:
Wow, very fun graphics, sound effects, music, and blood splatter. It’s feels like the MW2 “No Russian” airport level. Leveling up was fun.

While on Firefox during a fairly long session, the canvas disappeared and got this error message in the console:

downloadable font: Table discarded (font-family: “a” style:normal weight:1..700 stretch:100 src index:0) source: data:application/octet-stream;base64,d09G…

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text: Hello! Thank you very much for the good review! We are aware of the error after about 10 years of the game, we deliberately left it so that curious players could see that the game actually works in a web assembly environment :) it’s a very interesting experience for us, as well as for the community, I hope :) also we love you enjoy playing our entry!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A well made game, graphics and animations were good.

I liked all the little power ups and holes in the floor to switch rooms.

Lots of have play here, and opportunities for replayability.

Not sure if dispatching students is a tasteful game narrative these days? Also, why were they in their pants?

comments.author: Elias Ku
comments.text:
Hello! Thank you very much for your review!

The plot of the game refers to the movie “Friday the 13th”, this is according to the idea of a summer camp where everyone sunbathes in swimsuits and swimming trunks (eurostyle).

I hope it was interesting to play our project

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: My sort of humour! Fun game and thats what its all about. No idea what i was doing but so what 🤪😂

comments.author: Andrey Yurchenkov
comments.text: Awesome game, need just more features.

comments.author: 0xrinegade
comments.text: zigga zigcel ziglet ziglana ziggot zigger

comments.author: Gleb Volkov
comments.text: WOW, it’s awesome! 👌 The mix of dark humor and fast-paced action makes it super fun to play. The katana and axe, really add to the thrill of the game, and leveling up by taking down happy students is surprisingly satisfying. The police add a great challenge, so you have to stay on your toes while racking up XP. The design is simple but effective, and the game really captures the spooky vibe of Friday the 13th. Overall, it’s a great way to spend time and have a lot of fun!

comments.author: StormEx
comments.text:
A-mazing game. I really love your motion design and detailzation. Very good input from mobile device and from pc. Leveling, funny dialogs, hidings - all this stuff make this game like a real finished product.

Blood, a lot of bloold and yes - I’m sceared!!!

title: -13-Seconds-To-Kill-
author: Cannnto, ThiagoOSA and Rogério Freitas Mateus

api.name: -13-Seconds-To-Kill-
api.description:
While exploring an ancient underground labyrinth, intrepid adventurer T’reze found a sword. As he wielded it, it clung to the young man’s hand and a sinister voice echoed in his mind: “I am XIII and the more souls you sacrifice to feed me, the more power I will give you. This sword represents my appetite and if it’s fire goes out, I will be hungry and I will take your soul.” The power granted by XIII pleased the adventurer for a while, but the deaths he was forced to cause began to take a toll on his sanity. Now, T’reze goes to the lair of Dreizehn, the wicked necromancer who created the cursed sword, whose soul is the only thing that can satiate XIII forever. The risk, however, is great: while inside the necromancer’s tower, the artifact’s hunger will be even more brutal.

Game Controllers: WASD control player; mouse1 melee atack, mouse2 fireball projectile; press “c” to pass the dialogue press “e” to interact hope you guys enjoy.

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Great story and room design. Naturally, I hated the REVERSE room! Glad you put some decent audio in there. I wish there was an option to remove the blood from the equations, I had a couple of rooms that were too messy! Hmm, maybe that was by design.. Good entry either way. I encourage other players/makers to play these games out of order! Seems a bit unfair that the early game are getting all the plays and comments!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Nice kill animations. It’s great that you added both music and sfx. The explosion sfx worked well, but the “swing sword” sfx didn’t fit. I was expecting a “swish/swosh” sound (if that makes sense.)

The reverse room was difficult, and I kept loosing HP over time. Not sure if that was intended or not.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not play the game: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, so when I try to move the browser search suddenly shows up. You should call .preventDefault() on all keyboard events to prevent this.
games: Lord Balsekil's Lairs of Bad Luck

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text: Nice idea and backstory! Well done!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I am enjoy this game type. I do not have “search as you type” enabled when I simply turn it off so I can enjoy all the games. The backwards level was difficult for me. I couldnt search as I typed which may have helped.

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
I liked the rouge-like concept, but clicking LMB rapidly made my hand ache after 5 mins!

The aiming mechanism can be improved, making it more accurate for the player to targer the enemies.

I think you could adjust the difficulty of the first room to allow players to adapt to the game and ramp up familiarity on how to eliminate enemies.

Not too sure whether the room on the left is supposed to invert controls and pose as a challenge to the player, or was it a bug.

Overall, good attempt!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game looked good and played well.

I liked the graphics style and the ticking like a heartbeat music.

I seemed to die really easily, there was a lot of enemies spawning away.

The fireballs were good, but took ages to cast. The melee was also good, but I had to spam the mouse, perhaps holding the button down would be good enough to continue swiping?

api.name: 13th Gate
api.description:
Speed past the ever-closing gate in this abstract flying (or gliding?) game. Collect enough speed boosts to get to the end of the level with enough time to spare, or perhaps spend them jumping over obstacles to reach it more safely.

  • All 3 dimensions and precise collisions with those inadvertently placed spikes.
  • 5 fixed levels with increasing complexity and always different sixth level
  • Difficulty levels that change score multiplier, hitbox size and ship maneuverability
  • About 4 KB unused space reserved for missing enemies, soundtrack, SFX and cutscenes

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Nice runner. Some variation on the levels would had been nice.

Also adding some sound for the engine would give more velocity sensation.

comments.author: Mohammed Saud
comments.text:
I liked it, it’s satisfying when you manage to squeeze through tiny gaps. And I like that the closing gates bring a strategy element to the levels, you have to plan out your path so that you don’t end up in a corner where all gates are closed.

Coming to improvements, the controls were a bit too slippery for me, and made quick maneuvers hard, and sometimes many of the speed boosts would spawn under/behind spikes making them unable to get picked. And I wasn’t able to finish the 5th level on normal difficulty because all the gates would close before I could reach the end.

Nice game!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
