comments.text: Wow, impressive demake of Outrun, with the length of it too! JS-13K brakes for no one! Will check the source code with great interest to figure out the levels’ format and level editor, if you’ve used any.

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Thanks so much. The track is all procedurally generated, this helps me save space and makes it faster to iterate. The code is all on github, check it out!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Holy shit. This was quite the ride.

Despite the aesthetics, this is more of a driving experience than a race game. It’s unbelievable how much diversity you managed to pack in terms of biomes, plants, street signs, billboards. Just wow.

Also, I’m shocked how smooth it runs on my basic computer, even on Firefox. There are other games this year that melted my browser with just a few CSS effects.

Background music was dearly missed.

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Thanks so much, I worked really hard on the rendering performance, both planning how it would work and executing on that. There are a few things that would make it even faster but I couldn’t fit them (like using static meshes for vehicles). Next year it is my mission to have background music in my js13k game!

comments.author: Jesper Rasmussen
comments.text:
Awesome driving game :)

Fantastic 3D graphics, beautiful looking varied landscapes in great colours.

Very good controls / physics. Love that you cannot stear the car while it is in the air and that it is hard to accelerate up a hill.

Reminds me a lot of the old “Lotus Esprit Turbo Challenge” from the Amiga :)

All in all a very fun game to play <3

Improvement ideas:

  • Skidding / drifting / centrifugal force - so that you cannot take the hardest turns as fast. The car feels very glued to the track. Could also make different road surfaces feel more different.
  • Some indication of how far you are in the section.
  • Prevent traffic jams. Some times by bad luck the other drives makes it very difficult to get around them.
  • Maybe the accelerate button is not really needed? Just accelerate automatically all the time. You could control the speed w breaking.

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text:
Thank you, so many people have mentioned Lotus that I had to go back and check it out, I was mostly inspired by Sega’s OutRun. Looks like a very cool game, I have faint memories of playing something similar in DOS.

Great improvement ideas! I want to rework the physics model to include skidding and drifting, I had to really simplify to make it fit for js13k. Also the section hud distance marker is another thing I had to cut but will bring back in enhanced edition.

The traffic system is something I want to play with much more to not only eliminate jams but also include things like rival racers, varying speeds, lane changing, accidents on the side of the road, cross traffic. all kinds of cool stuff to play with!

I think accelerate is kind of necessary, even though it may seem like you are holding that most of the time, it just feels right for a driving game. Also you need to let off the gas to navigate around traffic. When I include other features like drifting, turbos, etc I think it will feel even better.

comments.author: Alex Delderfield
comments.text:
Such a great game & entry! One of my favourites this gamejam, I am really liking what you did with the 3D here. And there are so many levels to drive through, youve packed so much into this entry. Its fun seeing all the signs in-game as well, this game truly feels like the retro driving game of js13k.

I’d be really interested in reading a post-mortem of how this game came together.

(Also great work with ZzFX - such a good asset for gamejams like this!)

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Thanks so much, I will try to write the post mortem before next js13k! Really awesome to see all the cool games that used ZzFX, I am so happy to have helped other devs realize their vision.

comments.author: Fábio Santos
comments.text:
I loved this game through and through! I like how much color and personality is in it through only procedural graphics.

I think the way the road bends is really cool. I suppose this is seeded randomly generated splines?

I found the audio weirdly relaxing.

I really liked the gameplay and the challenge. It was just right. I would have liked a “hard mode” though!

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text:
Thanks! Yes, the road is all proc gen with a fixed seed. So it can generate random tracks but for this jam I wanted to focus on just one fixed seed to let me dial it in. There are a few params for this to control the min/max turn, how often it turns, and similar params for the hills which use a 1D noise algo.

The gameplay balance at least time pressure wise is on the easy side now because I want people to be able to beat it on their 2nd or 3rd playthrough even at low skill levels. I will tweak all that as I build it out into a larger game for wider release.

comments.author: Michel Sabchuk
comments.text: Impressive! The game includes so much features and runs smoothly! Definitely one of the best 2024 entries!

comments.author: Frank Force
comments.text: Thank you, there were so many great entries this year!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: Love the billboards. Overall pretty fun, can’t say I get the Theme tie-in but otherwise well done. For whatever reason my FPS dropped substantially in the tunnels, but otherwise it ran great.

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text:
What can I say?! Amazingly well done, as always! I followed the progress on X and really looked forward to playing the game. And Dr1v3n Wild really delivers. It is a great racing game building upon the heritage of games such as Out Run.

If I was to complain on anything it would be the engine sound that doesn’t have enough oumph! BUT in my mind I remembered racing games on the C64, such as Out Run and Super Cycle having a fairly low register “rumbling” engine sound (if you can say that about any sound from the SID chip), but when looking at longplays of the games on YouTube there wasn’t really any engine sound in Out Run (but pretty decent music) and the engine sound in Super Cycle was absolutely terrible. Pitstop 2 did a better job. So I take back my complaint about the engine sound in Dr1v3n Wild. It’s good enough!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: This game is amazing! The way you managed to fit so many details is astonishing, very well done :)

comments.author: Nicklas Löf
comments.text:
As an old Outrun player since the 80s I can’t do anything else than love this game! :)

it worked without issues on MacOS in both Firefox (with unlimited frame rate) and in Safari.

comments.author: Emanuele Feronato
comments.text:
Ok, no proper use of the competition theme, but an Out Run like game in just 13Kb is quite awesome.

Also congratulations for building the tracks with different graphics.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I got to stage 5, 3:30:0. The game is really impressive, it’s 3D, which is a feat in itself, but it’s also a good 3D game with lots of content. It’s truly amazing what you’ve added in 13kB. I like how the commercial banners are placed along the track, and they look crisp too. (Is this the start of LittleJS3D?)

I tried both the mouse and the arrow controls. The arrows are easier to use than the mouse, but I imagine the mouse controls could easily translate into touch controls for a mobile version. The controls are tight, and I feel in control of the car. When the camera changes due to the turns, the controls can feel somewhat unpredictable, but it wasn’t a major issue.

The stages are varied enough to make it interesting for the player to keep going. Adjusting the environment, the lanes (from 3 to 2), additional grass on the road and so on. Maybe there’s more after Stage 5.

comments.author: Nik Lever
comments.text: Just amazed that you fit that into 13k. Stunning work. Great fun to play.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The retro 3D style immediately brings to mind classic arcade racing games, which is both fun and nostalgic. The vibrant palm trees, ocean background, and smooth road design create a lively atmosphere, perfect for the game’s fast-paced action. The color palette is bright and playful, making the visuals stand out and contribute to the overall excitement. While the retro aesthetic is charming, adding a bit more variation to the scenery, such as different objects or dynamic elements along the road (like moving clouds or birds), could enhance the sense of progress and make the environment feel more dynamic. This would help keep the experience visually engaging as players advance through stages. You’ve successfully captured the retro arcade racing vibe with a clean and appealing design. The simplicity of the visuals allows players to focus on the fast-paced action, and the bright color scheme makes the game enjoyable to look at. Great job recreating the essence of classic racing games while keeping things visually fresh!

comments.author: Igor Khotin
comments.text:
Amazing entry! One you could use to demonstrate what a 13k game could do. Beautifully designed scenery across multiple stages. It is unbelievable how much could be squeezed into 13k.

And my kid still plays it, which tells a lot about great replayability value.

It is a true masterpiece that deserves great respect in the game jamming and demo scene communities!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text:
Amazing homage to Outrun, I was very impressed by the number of stages you manage to cramp in 13Ks, and they all felt different!

Great entry, very inspiring.

api.name: Thirteen Minutes To Space
api.description:
Unfinished game about a time loop (unimplemented :sob:) where you need to stop it by heading to space (unimplemented :sob:)

I never finished it. I was really slow while making this, and then i got really overwhelmed. I figured I’d still share what I got done. I hope to finish it one day!!

Controls

ENTER: Start game LEFT/RIGHT ARROWS: Move left/right SPACE BAR: Jump SHIFT: Sprint (you go faster)

Final size

6.3KB compressed

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text:
It definitely feels like you had a great concept in mind when developing this game! The motion is very fluid and the gameplay shows promise. It’s always difficult to realize a vision, especially when progress slows down, as it always does in certain, often arbitrary, moments in a game’s development timeline. I don’t know if you’re looking for any advice, but I’ll dispense just a bit of it: as with any larger project, break the daily plan down into small achievable goals, so that you can appreciate/gauge the progress, even (and especially) when forward momentum dips a bit.

Congrats on starting, and I hope to see a finished version in the future!

With the ominous theme of “Scared of 13” looming over you, every shot counts as you fight for survival against increasingly fierce enemies. Use W A S D to move around or Arrow Keys E to shoot or Click

api.name: 10plus3
api.description:
10plus3 is a math puzzle game with a simple yet addictive mechanic: Starting from a random number, use the arrow keys to select the mathematical operations in the correct order to get 13 as the final result.

It may help fighting your triskaidekaphobia, or not. At least, you will exercise your brain.

Controls: arrow keys

Btw: don’t try to finish the game, levels are randomly generated :)

api.name: M7 Summit
api.description:
Thanks for opening this page!

Purpose The objective is to select the mountain you operate and defeat the world’s most famous mountains. In keeping with the theme, when the total number of attacks on the two mountains reachesthirteen, a painful future will await the attacked mountain. If you want to be victorius, you should wait until the 13th attack from the enemy.

User guide A: Attack Left arrow: Move left Right arrow: Move right

I was actually going to include a local mountain in the hidden character, but I gave up because I ran out of time.

Looking forward to getting your feedback!

I like the pixelart imges of the mountains. They are pretty accurate :)

I can fling some fire/ice balls at the other mountain, and i can move my mountain until it touches the other one. I even won by doing nothing…? Probably the “I ran out of time” is the root of the problem…

Nice approach on the “backend” of the game, everything is done by HTML-Nodes and CSS

api.name: Witch Hunter
api.description: Fight witches inspired by historical figures as a real inquisitor as Friday the 13th approaches…

comments.author: Mike Helgeson

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Mike,

I’m sorry to hear that you had trouble getting the game to work beyond the initial character selection.

Here are some things to check:

  • Compatibility: Ensure your device or browser is compatible with the game. I tested in Desktop and Meta Quest2.
  • Technical Issues: Try playing in incognito mode.
  • Game Mechanics: On desktop, clicking the white circle to initialize the A-Frame cursor is essential for proper gameplay.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Could pass from the selection screen, but the landscape was cool.

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Sorry, I didn’t realize this was a XR category.

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Noah,

Thanks for playing! I understand that figuring out how to navigate the forest and interact with elements can be a bit tricky at first.

To interact with the cat, click the button located below the text near the cat. This will allow you to engage in a conversation. Remember to click the white circle to initialize the A-Frame cursor on desktop.

Before entering the fight, be sure to heal and prepare yourself. This will make the battle easier to manage.

Please note that there’s a known memory leak issue in some browsers, like Opera and Firefox. If you encounter performance problems, try playing in incognito mode.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
I somehow passed the selection, and even encountered a witch and EVEN managed to blame her (causing damage) once… hmm… many clicks, some had worked.

So the main gameplay is a mystery to me.

BUT: the graphics of the landscape are very cool, and i had some fun zooming through the trees (like flying on a broom)

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Christoph,

Thanks for playing!

It’s great to hear that you’ve managed to encounter a witch and even inflict damage. That’s a good start!

On desktop, clicking the white circle to initialize the A-Frame cursor is essential for proper gameplay. If you encounter problems, try in incognito mode, there’s a known memory leak issue in some browsers.

I appreciate your feedback on the graphics. I’m glad you enjoyed the experience of flying through the trees! Let me know if you have any other questions.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond

Since the landscape is using the default A-Frame environment component you definitely had time to at least make sure the basic gameplay works. Should have been entered in the unfinished category.

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Samuel,

Thank you for taking the time to try my game. I’m sorry to hear that your experience wasn’t as enjoyable as I had hoped.

I understand that the game’s VR and desktop experience could be improved. In VR, please ensure you’re using a compatible headset, I tested the game on Meta Quest2 and desktop browser. On desktop, clicking the white circle to initialize the A-Frame cursor is essential for proper gameplay.

While the game may have some limitations, I’m proud of its unique features, such as the historical characters and real-world references. I believe these elements offer a distinctive gameplay experience.

comments.author: Burian Sándor

Hi Jerome,

Thanks for playing!

Try clicking on the buttons under the characters to pass from the main character selection. Here is a gameplay example: https://youtu.be/c4Uj-spZBOA

If you face problems with replaying, try an incognito window or delete browser history, there’s a known memory leak issue in some browsers…

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke

Since I don’t have a VR setup I tried it on desktop and for the most part it was fun to just look around the grid.

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Jasper,

Thanks for playing! I tried the rollermaze as well, It was fun to play!

comments.author: Akbar Sultanov
comments.text:
Theme: The game effectively uncovers the main topic of the year, centered around witches and Friday the 13th.

Gameplay: Although the game claims to be based on real characters, it feels somewhat unpolished. The starting point is not intuitive, making it challenging for players to know how to begin. Additionally, gaze-based interaction in XR/VR mode should be reconsidered, as it detracts from the viewing experience.

Audio: There is no audio. The game would definitely benefit from it.

Innovation: While the author attempts to create an interactive experience through textual prompts, the game is not easily accessible or enjoyable to play.

Graphics: The UI and environment need refinement to help players better orient themselves and enhance overall gameplay.

Controls: The controls require further improvement for a smoother experience.

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Akbar,

Thanks for the feedback, most of which I agree myself. Unfortunately, the size limit and the amount of time I was able to devote to the competition this year limited the capabilities of the game, but I hope it was enjoyable for you because I enjoyed the process of making it!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Theme: First game I am evaluating about Friday the 13th. This is exactly on topic and I have to admit I thought they would almost all be about it :-)

Gameplay: The game was not as intuitive as it could have been. I wonder if more hints for the user would help getting started.

Audio: Not Available.

Innovation: Loved the twist with historical characters.

Graphics: Simpler graphics may have helped with a more intuitive game.

Controls: I guess the current selection system could have been refined for a more enjoyable experience.

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Sébastien,

Thank you for trying it and for your feedback, it’s really constructive! If I have time, I will try to develop it further in this direction!

comments.author: Kieran Farr
comments.text:
Theme: Attempts a silly spooky vibe which seems in line with the theme

Gameplay: Was hard to get past the character selection screen

Audio: Some effort made, but falls short of enhancing gameplay.

Innovation: Applies a 2D concept to VR without meaningful adaptation to XR medium.

Graphics: Visuals are basic

Controls: I had a hard time figuring out how to get past the character selection screen

comments.author: Burian Sándor
comments.text:
Hi Kieran,

Thanks for playing! I tried to create a pixel style but not minecraft-style game :)

api.name: Spell Spells
api.description:
This is a roguelike deckbuilder where you have to collect letters and then make the best word you can to defeat monsters. Like Slay the Spire, but with words!

Story: You are a young wizard 🧙‍♂️, just learning how to make spells. Your grandma 👵 has used her magic foresight to determine that an evil dragon 🐉 will arrive in 13 days to destroy our hometown. She gives you a book of letters so that you can learn to make spells. Until the dragon comes, you’ll need to go out every night to increase your skill by defeating monsters. Why don’t other help and why don’t the shopkeepers just give you everything free, if you’re going to save everyone’s lives? Because they don’t believe the dragon is real.

Strategies:

  1. Keep upgrading 1 letter, like a J. Get the Zipper Mouth 🤐 so that when you don’t get the J, just skip your turn. Remove lots of other letters. Get the 1st Place Medal 🥇 to 2x the first letter, since J is always the first letter.
  2. Long words. Get the Nerd Face 🤓 and Slot Machine 🎰 to 5x long words. Collect exclamation marks to make it easier to make long words. Also get 🆖 and try to add ING to words to make them long.
  3. Healing. Get the Syringe 💉 to damage enemies when you heal. Get the Stethoscope 🩺 and Bandage 🩹 to heal more. Collect letters that heal.
  4. Paperclips. Get the Linked Paperclips 🖇 and Filing Cabinet 🗄 to boost kept letters. Collect more letters with paperclips on them.

Also play on curtastic.com and follow me on X @curtastic

Credits:

Code: Curtastic

Game Design: Curtastic

CSS art: Curtastic

Victory Music: “Alta Trinita Beata” - Laudario di Cortona

Game Over Music: “The Bonnie Banks o’ Loch Lomond” - Unknown author

Dictionary hosting: Thirdweb blockchain

The word dictionary is stored on the Thirdweb blockchain (at ipfs://QmbL7RFBtKXr7KXEaAWA1sgLxHkUV2wtk2NEfNadTLibhF) I can add more English words if any are missing.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Really fun game! Solid gameplay– I made it to level 9.

Nice little details with celebration emoji animation, speaking the cast word, etc., too

Just for some small suggestions:

  1. In the level-up screen, I didn’t realize that the wand upgrades would show a description when clicked; I wasn’t buying them at first, because I wasn’t sure what they did
  2. Also in the level-up screen, I can’t see past the 4th (?) row of my bag of letters. I guess making the bag of letters scrollable would be cool?

But overall really fun! I’ll definitely play some more later!

comments.author: Zark Attack
comments.text: Unique, and surprisingly fun entry! My biggest complaint is with the main music track, but other than that, I really loved your refreshing game! I was able to make it to the 12th battle.

comments.author: Raptor Dev
comments.text:
Very fun entry. I don’t normally like deck/card games but my friend convinced me to try it out.

Lots of effort put into this one. Not sure how all of this fits into 13k, and it seems the dictionary is pulled externally unless you only use the honor system aka no checks in game.

Really like the powerups and overall game loop. Took me a few rounds to realize the powerups stay once you buy them. I’m still a bit unclear on how a few things work so I may have to play once or twice more.

I got to round 9 and got a run of bad letters with no vowels like twice in a row :(

As far as audio, I didn’t see anyone else comment but I had an annoying beep at a regular interval until I muted everything. I thought at first there was some sort of timer I was missing.

comments.author: Stanisław Małolepszy
comments.text:
It turns out Scrabble is very hard in English (for a non-native speaker). Thankfully, it didn’t really matter all that much thanks to the deck building mechanics that you designed: upgrading letters, trimming them out of the deck, and buying artifacts. It’s a lot of fun to build an “engine” to battle the opponents. I’ve not yet finished the game, but I’ll be coming back to it for sure.

I really like how the game looks, too. The emojis work well but what really caught my attention were all the animations that explained what was going on. Great attention to detail and to the user experience! (As an author of a deckbuilder myself, I struggled with this aspect of the design in my game.)

Having some more audio effects and music would be nice.

comments.author: Ewa Mazur
comments.text: The playful mix of emojis and simple iconography gives Spell Spells a unique charm. The use of emojis for characters adds a casual, fun element that feels familiar and accessible to a broad audience. The interface is clean, which helps in focusing on the word-spelling gameplay. The task of battling a mosquito with words brings a clever twist that keeps players engaged, especially when combined with the lighthearted atmosphere. The overall visual experience could benefit from more consistency in style. The combination of different visual elements like emojis, standard icons, and word tiles could be unified with more cohesive design choices. For example, introducing a common color palette or a matching art style for all elements would give the game a more polished feel. Additionally, animations could be added when casting spells or during interactions with enemies, to make the game feel more dynamic and rewarding. Overall, Spell Spells brings a fun and accessible experience, using familiar visual cues to create a comfortable and engaging environment. With some fine-tuning on the visual cohesion and additional animations, it has the potential to be even more immersive. The lighthearted tone and unique emoji-based design make it stand out, and it’s clear that a lot of creativity went into the concept. Keep up the great work!
